Rotherham.label: The Emphasised Bible
Rotherham.overlayPolicy: mismatch chapters stored under translationOverlays.Rotherham without altering target verse sequence
Rotherham.emptySourceRowsPolicy: empty Rotherham source rows are governed omissions and are not imported as blank text

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: The beginning of the good news of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
text.Rotherham: Beginning of the glad-message of Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
text.NABRE: I. The Preparation for the Public Ministry of Jesus Chapter 1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ [the Son of God].
text.DRB: The beginning of the Gospel of Jesus Christ the Son of God.

text.NRSV: As it is written in the prophet Isaiah, “See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way;
text.Rotherham: According as it is written in Isaiah the prophet—Lo! I send my messenger before thy face, Who shall prepare thy way,—
text.KJV: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
text.NABRE: The Preaching of John the Baptist. As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: “Behold, I am sending my messenger ahead of you; he will prepare your way.
text.DRB: As it is written in Esay the Prophet : ( Behold I send mine Angel before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee.)

text.NRSV: the voice of one crying out in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight,’”
text.Rotherham: A voice of one crying aloud—In the wilderness, prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight, be making his paths.
text.KJV: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
text.NABRE: A voice of one crying out in the desert: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight his paths.’”
text.DRB: A voice of one crying in the desert. Prepare ye the way of our Lord, make straight his paths.

text.NRSV: John the baptizer appeared in the wilderness, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: John the Immerser came, in the wilderness, proclaiming an immersion of repentance for remission of sins;
text.KJV: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
text.NABRE: John [the] Baptist appeared in the desert proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
text.DRB: John was in the desert baptizing, and preaching the Baptism of penance unto remission of sins.

text.NRSV: And people from the whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him, and were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
text.Rotherham: and there were going out unto him all the Judaea country and all they of Jerusalem, and were being immersed by him in the Jordan river, openly confessing their sins;
text.KJV: And there went out unto him all the land of Judea, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.
text.NABRE: People of the whole Judean countryside and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem were going out to him and were being baptized by him in the Jordan River as they acknowledged their sins.
text.DRB: And there went forth to him all the country of Jewry, and all they of Jerusalem, and were baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.

text.NRSV: Now John was clothed with camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
text.Rotherham: and John was clothed with camel’s-hair and a leathern girdle about his loins, and was eating locusts and wild honey;
text.KJV: And John was clothed with camel’s hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;
text.NABRE: John was clothed in camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He fed on locusts and wild honey.
text.DRB: And John was clothed with camel's hair, and a girdle of a skin about his loins: and he did eat locusts and wild honey.

text.NRSV: He proclaimed, “The one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the thong of his sandals.
text.Rotherham: and he proclaimed, saying—He that is mightier than I cometh after [me], of whom I am not worthy to stoop and unloose the strap of his sandals:
text.KJV: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
text.NABRE: And this is what he proclaimed: “One mightier than I is coming after me. I am not worthy to stoop and loosen the thongs of his sandals.
text.DRB: And he preached, saying: There cometh a stronger than I after me, whose latchet of his shoes I am not worthy stooping down to loose.

text.NRSV: I have baptized you with water; but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: I, have immersed you, with water,—He, will immerse you, with the Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: I have baptized you with water; he will baptize you with the holy Spirit.”
text.DRB: I have baptized you with water; but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was immersed into the Jordan by John;
text.KJV: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
text.NABRE: The Baptism of Jesus. It happened in those days that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized in the Jordan by John.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, in those days came Jesus from Nazareth of Galilee; and was baptized of John in Jordan.

text.NRSV: And just as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens torn apart and the Spirit descending like a dove on him.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens rending asunder, and, the Spirit, as a dove, descending unto him;
text.KJV: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
text.NABRE: On coming up out of the water he saw the heavens being torn open and the Spirit, like a dove, descending upon him.
text.DRB: And forthwith coming up out of the water, he saw the Heavens opened, and the Spirit as a dove descending, and remaining on him.

text.NRSV: And a voice came from heaven, “You are my Son, the Beloved; with you I am well pleased.”
text.Rotherham: and a voice [came] out of the heavens—Thou, art my Son, the Beloved,—In thee, I delight.
text.KJV: And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
text.NABRE: And a voice came from the heavens, “You are my beloved Son; with you I am well pleased.” The Temptation of Jesus.
text.DRB: And a voice was made from Heaven: Thou art my beloved Son, in thee I am well pleased.

text.NRSV: And the Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, the Spirit urgeth him forth into the wilderness;
text.KJV: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.
text.NABRE: At once the Spirit drove him out into the desert,
text.DRB: And forthwith the Spirit drove him out into the desert.

text.NRSV: He was in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan; and he was with the wild beasts; and the angels waited on him.
text.Rotherham: and he was in the wilderness, forty days, tempted by Satan, and was with the wild beasts,—and, the messengers, were ministering unto him.
text.KJV: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
text.NABRE: and he remained in the desert for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among wild beasts, and the angels ministered to him.
text.DRB: And he was in the desert forty days, and forty nights: and was tempted of Satan, and he was with beasts, and the Angels ministered to him.

text.NRSV: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came to Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God,
text.Rotherham: And after John was delivered up, Jesus came into Galilee, proclaiming the glad-message of God,
text.KJV: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
text.NABRE: II. The Mystery of Jesus The Beginning of the Galilean Ministry. After John had been arrested, Jesus came to Galilee proclaiming the gospel of God:
text.DRB: And after that John was delivered up, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the Gospel of the Kingdom of God,

text.NRSV: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has come near; repent, and believe in the good news.”
text.Rotherham: [and saying]—The season is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God hath drawn near,—Repent ye, and have faith in the glad-message.
text.KJV: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.
text.NABRE: “This is the time of fulfillment. The kingdom of God is at hand. Repent, and believe in the gospel.” The Call of the First Disciples.
text.DRB: and saying: That the time is fulfilled, and the Kingdom of God is at hand: be penitent, and believe the Gospel.

text.NRSV: As Jesus passed along the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the sea—for they were fishermen.
text.Rotherham: And, passing by near the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon, and Andrew the brother of Simon, casting a net in the sea,—for they were fishers;
text.KJV: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
text.NABRE: As he passed by the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting their nets into the sea; they were fishermen.
text.DRB: And passing by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother, casting nets into the sea (for they were fishers)

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me and I will make you fish for people.”
text.Rotherham: and Jesus said unto them—Come after me, and I will make you become fishers of men;
text.KJV: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you fishers of men.”
text.DRB: and Jesus said to them: Come after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.

text.NRSV: And immediately they left their nets and followed him.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, leaving the nets, they followed him.
text.KJV: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
text.NABRE: Then they abandoned their nets and followed him.
text.DRB: And immediately leaving their nets, they followed him.

text.NRSV: As he went a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John, who were in their boat mending the nets.
text.Rotherham: And, going forward a little, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, and them who were in the boat putting in order the nets;
text.KJV: And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
text.NABRE: He walked along a little farther and saw James, the son of Zebedee, and his brother John. They too were in a boat mending their nets.
text.DRB: And being gone thence a little further, he saw James of Zebedee, and John his brother, and them repairing their nets in the ship:

text.NRSV: Immediately he called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men, and followed him.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, he called them,—and, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat, with the hired men, they came away after him.
text.KJV: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
text.NABRE: Then he called them. So they left their father Zebedee in the boat along with the hired men and followed him.
text.DRB: and forthwith he called them. And leaving their father Zebedee in the ship with his hired men, they followed him.

text.NRSV: They went to Capernaum; and when the sabbath came, he entered the synagogue and taught.
text.Rotherham: And they journey into Capernaum. And, straightway, on the Sabbath, entering into the synagogue, he began teaching;
text.KJV: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.
text.NABRE: The Cure of a Demoniac. Then they came to Capernaum, and on the sabbath he entered the synagogue and taught.
text.DRB: And they enter into Capharnaum, and he forthwith upon the Sabboths going into the Synagogue, taught them.

text.NRSV: They were astounded at his teaching, for he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
text.Rotherham: and they were being struck with astonishment at his teaching,—for he was teaching them as one having authority, and, not as the Scribes.
text.KJV: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.
text.NABRE: The people were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as one having authority and not as the scribes.
text.DRB: And they were astonished at his doctrine. For he was teaching them as having power, and not as the Scribes.

text.NRSV: Just then there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit,
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, there was in their synagogue, a man in an impure spirit,—and he cried out aloud,
text.KJV: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
text.NABRE: In their synagogue was a man with an unclean spirit;
text.DRB: And there was in their Synagogue a man in an unclean Spirit; and he cried out,

text.NRSV: and he cried out, “What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, the Holy One of God.”
text.Rotherham: saying—What have we in common with thee, Jesus of Nazareth? Hast thou come to destroy us? I know thee, who thou art, The Holy One of God.
text.KJV: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
text.NABRE: he cried out, “What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
text.DRB: saying: What to us and to thee Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know who thou art, the Saint of God.

text.NRSV: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus rebuked him, [saying]—Be silenced, and come forth out of him!
text.KJV: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
text.NABRE: Jesus rebuked him and said, “Quiet! Come out of him!”
text.DRB: And Jesus threatened him, saying: Hold thy peace, and go out of the man.

text.NRSV: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.
text.Rotherham: And the impure spirit, tearing him, and calling out with a loud voice, came forth out of him;
text.KJV: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.
text.NABRE: The unclean spirit convulsed him and with a loud cry came out of him.
text.DRB: And the unclean Spirit tearing him, and crying out with a great voice, went out of him.

text.NRSV: They were all amazed, and they kept on asking one another, “What is this? A new teaching—with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
text.Rotherham: and they were amazed, one and all, so that they began to discuss among themselves, saying—What is this? New teaching! With authority, to the impure spirits also, he giveth orders, and they obey him!
text.KJV: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
text.NABRE: All were amazed and asked one another, “What is this? A new teaching with authority. He commands even the unclean spirits and they obey him.”
text.DRB: And they marveled all, in so much that they questioned among themselves, saying: What thing is this? What is this new doctrine? For with power he commandeth the unclean spirits also, and they obey him.

text.NRSV: At once his fame began to spread throughout the surrounding region of Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the report of him, straightway, on every hand, into the surrounding country of Galilee.
text.KJV: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
text.NABRE: His fame spread everywhere throughout the whole region of Galilee.
text.DRB: And the bruit of him went forth incontinent into all the country of Galilee.

text.NRSV: As soon as they left the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, out of the synagogue, going forth, he went unto the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
text.KJV: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
text.NABRE: The Cure of Simon’s Mother-in-Law. On leaving the synagogue he entered the house of Simon and Andrew with James and John.
text.DRB: And immediately going forth out of the Synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

text.NRSV: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told him about her at once.
text.Rotherham: Now, the mother-in-law of Simon, was lying in a fever, and, straightway, they speak to him concerning her;
text.KJV: But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.
text.NABRE: Simon’s mother-in-law lay sick with a fever. They immediately told him about her.
text.DRB: And Simon's wife's mother lay in a fit of a fever: and forthwith they tell him of her.

text.NRSV: He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up. Then the fever left her, and she began to serve them.
text.Rotherham: and, coming near, he raised her up, grasping her hand,—and the fever left her, and she began ministering unto them.
text.KJV: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
text.NABRE: He approached, grasped her hand, and helped her up. Then the fever left her and she waited on them.
text.DRB: And coming near he lifted her up taking her by the hand: and incontinent the ague left her, and she ministered unto them.

text.NRSV: That evening, at sunset, they brought to him all who were sick or possessed with demons.
text.Rotherham: And, evening arriving, when the sun went in, they were bearing unto him all who were sick, and them who were demonized;
text.KJV: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
text.NABRE: Other Healings. When it was evening, after sunset, they brought to him all who were ill or possessed by demons.
text.DRB: And when it was evening after sun set, they brought to him all that were ill at ease and that had devils.

text.NRSV: And the whole city was gathered around the door.
text.Rotherham: and all the city was gathered unto the door;
text.KJV: And all the city was gathered together at the door.
text.NABRE: The whole town was gathered at the door.
text.DRB: And all the city was gathered together at the door.

text.NRSV: And he cured many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.
text.Rotherham: and he cured many that were sick with divers diseases, and, many demons, he cast out, and suffered not the demons to be talking,—because they knew him [to be Christ].
text.KJV: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
text.NABRE: He cured many who were sick with various diseases, and he drove out many demons, not permitting them to speak because they knew him.
text.DRB: And he cured many that were vexed with diverse diseases; and he cast out many devils, and he suffered not them to speak that they knew him.

text.NRSV: In the morning, while it was still very dark, he got up and went out to a deserted place, and there he prayed.
text.Rotherham: And, very early by night arising, he went out [and departed] into a desert place, and, there, was praying;
text.KJV: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
text.NABRE: Jesus Leaves Capernaum. Rising very early before dawn, he left and went off to a deserted place, where he prayed.
text.DRB: And rising very early, and going forth he went into a desert place: and there he prayed.

text.NRSV: And Simon and his companions hunted for him.
text.Rotherham: and Simon, and they who were with him, went in quest of him,
text.KJV: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
text.NABRE: Simon and those who were with him pursued him
text.DRB: And Simon sought after him, and they that were with him.

text.NRSV: When they found him, they said to him, “Everyone is searching for you.”
text.Rotherham: and found him, and say unto him—All, are seeking thee;
text.KJV: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.
text.NABRE: and on finding him said, “Everyone is looking for you.”
text.DRB: And when they had found him, they said to him, That all seek for thee.

text.NRSV: He answered, “Let us go on to the neighboring towns, so that I may proclaim the message there also; for that is what I came out to do.”
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Let us be going elsewhere, into the neighbouring country-towns, in order that, there also, I may be making proclamation,—for, to this end, came I forth;
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
text.NABRE: He told them, “Let us go on to the nearby villages that I may preach there also. For this purpose have I come.”
text.DRB: And he saith to them: Let us go into the next towns and cities, that I may preach there also; for to this purpose am I come.

text.NRSV: And he went throughout Galilee, proclaiming the message in their synagogues and casting out demons.
text.Rotherham: and he came, making proclamation, into their synagogues, throughout the whole of Galilee,—and, was casting the demons out.
text.KJV: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.
text.NABRE: So he went into their synagogues, preaching and driving out demons throughout the whole of Galilee.
text.DRB: And he was preaching in their Synagogues, and in all Galilee, and casting out devils.

text.NRSV: A leper came to him begging him, and kneeling he said to him, “If you choose, you can make me clean.”
text.Rotherham: And there cometh unto him, a leper, beseeching him and [kneeling],—saying unto him—If thou be willing, thou canst cleanse me;
text.KJV: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
text.NABRE: The Cleansing of a Leper. A leper came to him [and kneeling down] begged him and said, “If you wish, you can make me clean.”
text.DRB: And a leper cometh to him beseeching him; and kneeling down saith to him: If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

text.NRSV: Moved with pity, Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, and said to him, “I do choose. Be made clean!”
text.Rotherham: and, moved with compassion, he stretched forth the hand, and touched him, and saith unto him—I am willing, Be cleansed!
text.KJV: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
text.NABRE: Moved with pity, he stretched out his hand, touched him, and said to him, “I do will it. Be made clean.”
text.DRB: And Jesus having compassion on him, stretched forth his hand; and touching him, he saith unto him: I will. Be thou made clean.

text.NRSV: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed;
text.KJV: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
text.NABRE: The leprosy left him immediately, and he was made clean.
text.DRB: And when he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was made clean.

text.NRSV: After sternly warning him he sent him away at once,
text.Rotherham: and, strictly charging him, straightway, he urged him forth;
text.KJV: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
text.NABRE: Then, warning him sternly, he dismissed him at once.
text.DRB: And he threatened him, and forthwith cast him forth.

text.NRSV: saying to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
text.Rotherham: and saith unto him—Mind! unto no one, say aught,—but withdraw, thyself, show unto the priest, and offer for thy cleansing what things Moses enjoined, for a witness unto them.
text.KJV: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
text.NABRE: Then he said to him, “See that you tell no one anything, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses prescribed; that will be proof for them.”
text.DRB: And he saith to him: See thou tell nobody, but go, shew thyself to the high Priest, and offer for thy cleansing the things that Moyses commanded, for a testimony to them.

text.NRSV: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely, and to spread the word, so that Jesus could no longer go into a town openly, but stayed out in deserted places; and people came to him from every quarter.
text.Rotherham: But, he, going forth, began to be proclaiming many things, and blazing abroad the story, so that, no longer, was it possible for him, openly, into a city, to enter,—but, outside, in desert places, [was he], and they were coming unto him from every quarter.
text.KJV: But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.
text.NABRE: The man went away and began to publicize the whole matter. He spread the report abroad so that it was impossible for Jesus to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, and people kept coming to him from everywhere.
text.DRB: But he being gone forth, began to publish, and to blase abroad the word: so that now he could not openly go into the city, but was abroad in desert places, and they came together unto him from all sides.

text.NRSV: When he returned to Capernaum after some days, it was reported that he was at home.
text.Rotherham: And, entering again into Capernaum, after some days, it was heard say—He is in a house;
text.KJV: And again he entered into Capernaum after some days; and it was noised that he was in the house.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 The Healing of a Paralytic. When Jesus returned to Capernaum after some days, it became known that he was at home.
text.DRB: And again he entered into Capharnaum after some days, and it was heard that he was in the house,

text.NRSV: So many gathered around that there was no longer room for them, not even in front of the door; and he was speaking the word to them.
text.Rotherham: and many were gathered together, so that no longer was there room even in the approaches to the door,—and he began speaking unto them the word;
text.KJV: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.
text.NABRE: Many gathered together so that there was no longer room for them, not even around the door, and he preached the word to them.
text.DRB: and many came together, so that there was no place; no not at the door; & he spake to them the Word.

text.NRSV: Then some people came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.
text.Rotherham: and they come, bearing unto him a paralytic, upborne by four,—
text.KJV: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.
text.NABRE: They came bringing to him a paralytic carried by four men.
text.DRB: And they came to him bringing one sick of the palsy, who was carried of four.

text.NRSV: And when they could not bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him; and after having dug through it, they let down the mat on which the paralytic lay.
text.Rotherham: and, not being able to get near him by reason of the multitude, they uncovered the roof where he was, and, having broken it up, they began letting down the couch whereon the paralytic was lying;
text.KJV: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
text.NABRE: Unable to get near Jesus because of the crowd, they opened up the roof above him. After they had broken through, they let down the mat on which the paralytic was lying.
text.DRB: And when they could not offer him unto him for the multitude, they uncovered the roof where he was: and opening it they did let down the couch wherein the sick of the palsy lay.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
text.Rotherham: and, Jesus, seeing their faith, saith unto the paralytic—Child! forgiven are thy sins!
text.KJV: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
text.NABRE: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Child, your sins are forgiven.”
text.DRB: And when Jesus had seen their faith, he saith to the sick of the palsy: Son, thy sins are forgiven thee.

text.NRSV: Now some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts,
text.Rotherham: Now there were certain of the Scribes there, sitting and deliberating in their hearts,—
text.KJV: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
text.NABRE: Now some of the scribes were sitting there asking themselves,
text.DRB: And there were certain of the Scribes sitting there and thinking in their hearts:

text.NRSV: “Why does this fellow speak in this way? It is blasphemy! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
text.Rotherham: Why doth, this, man thus talk? he is speaking profanely! Who can forgive sins, save one, God?
text.KJV: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
text.NABRE: “Why does this man speak that way? He is blaspheming. Who but God alone can forgive sins?”
text.DRB: Why doth he speak so? He blasphemeth. Who can forgive sins but only God?

text.NRSV: At once Jesus perceived in his spirit that they were discussing these questions among themselves; and he said to them, “Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, straightway, taking note in his spirit, that [thus] they are deliberating within themselves, saith [unto them]—Why, these things, are ye deliberating in your hearts?
text.KJV: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
text.NABRE: Jesus immediately knew in his mind what they were thinking to themselves, so he said, “Why are you thinking such things in your hearts?
text.DRB: Which by and by Jesus knowing in his Spirit, that they so thought within themselves, saith to them: Why think you these things in your hearts?

text.NRSV: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Stand up and take your mat and walk’?
text.Rotherham: Which is easier—to say unto the paralytic, Forgiven are thy sins, or to say, Rise, [and] take up thy couch, and be walking?
text.KJV: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
text.NABRE: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Rise, pick up your mat and walk’?
text.DRB: Whether is easier, to say to the sick of the palsy: Thy sins are forgiven thee; or to say: Arise, take up thy couch, and walk?

text.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic—
text.Rotherham: But, that ye may know that the Son of Man hath authority to be forgiving sins upon the earth, he saith to the paralytic:
text.KJV: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
text.NABRE: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority to forgive sins on earth”—
text.DRB: But that you may know that the Son of man hath power in earth to forgive sins (he saith to the sick of the palsy)

text.NRSV: “I say to you, stand up, take your mat and go to your home.”
text.Rotherham: To thee, I say, Rise, take up thy couch, and be going thy way unto thy house.
text.KJV: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
text.NABRE: he said to the paralytic, “I say to you, rise, pick up your mat, and go home.”
text.DRB: Isay to thee, arise, take up thy couch, and go into thy house.

text.NRSV: And he stood up, and immediately took the mat and went out before all of them; so that they were all amazed and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
text.Rotherham: And he arose, and, straightway taking up the couch, went forth before all,—so that all were beside themselves and were glorifying God, [saying]—Thus, we never saw it!
text.KJV: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
text.NABRE: He rose, picked up his mat at once, and went away in the sight of everyone. They were all astounded and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this.”
text.DRB: And forthwith he arose; and taking up his couch, went his way in the sight of all, so that all marveled, and glorified God, saying: That we never saw the like.

text.NRSV: Jesus went out again beside the sea; the whole crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text.Rotherham: And he went forth again by the sea,—and all the multitude was coming unto him, and he began teaching them.
text.KJV: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
text.NABRE: The Call of Levi. Once again he went out along the sea. All the crowd came to him and he taught them.
text.DRB: And he went forth again to the sea; and all the multitude came to him, and he taught them.

text.NRSV: As he was walking along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, passing by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting over the tax-office,—and saith unto him—Be following me! And, arising, he followed him.
text.KJV: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alpheus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
text.NABRE: As he passed by, he saw Levi, son of Alphaeus, sitting at the customs post. He said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
text.DRB: And when he passed by, he saw Levi of Alphæus sitting at the custom place; and he saith to him: Follow me. And rising up he followed him.

text.NRSV: And as he sat at dinner in Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were also sitting with Jesus and his disciples—for there were many who followed him.
text.Rotherham: And it cometh to pass, that he is reclining in his house, and many tax-collectors and sinners were reclining with Jesus and his disciples; for there were many, and they began following him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
text.NABRE: While he was at table in his house, many tax collectors and sinners sat with Jesus and his disciples; for there were many who followed him.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat in his house, many Publicans and sinners did sit down together with Jesus and his Disciples. For they were many, who also followed him.

text.NRSV: When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and Pharisees, seeing that he was eating with the sinners and the tax-collectors, began saying unto his disciples—With the tax-collectors and sinners, is he eating?
text.KJV: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
text.NABRE: Some scribes who were Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors and said to his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.DRB: And the Scribes & the Pharisees seeing that he did eat with Publicans and sinners, said to his Disciples: Why doth your Master eat & drink with Publicans and sinners?

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick; I have come to call not the righteous but sinners.”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing it, Jesus saith unto them—No need, have the strong of a physician, but they who are sick, I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.
text.KJV: When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
text.NABRE: Jesus heard this and said to them [that], “Those who are well do not need a physician, but the sick do. I did not come to call the righteous but sinners.” The Question About Fasting.
text.DRB: Jesus hearing this, saith to them: The whole have not need of a Physician, but they that are ill at ease. For I came not to call the just, but sinners.

text.NRSV: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and people came and said to him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples of John and the Pharisees were fasting; and they come and say unto him—For what cause, do, the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees, fast, whereas, thy disciples, fast not?
text.KJV: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
text.NABRE: The disciples of John and of the Pharisees were accustomed to fast. People came to him and objected, “Why do the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
text.DRB: And the Disciples of John and the Pharisees did use to fast: and they come, and say to him: Why do the Disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast; but thy Disciples do not fast?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot fast while the bridegroom is with them, can they? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them—Is it possible for the sons of the bridechamber, while the bridegroom is with them, to be fasting? So long as they have the bridegroom with them, it is impossible, to fast.
text.KJV: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “Can the wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them they cannot fast.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: Why, can the children of the marriage fast, as long as the bridegroom is with them? So long time as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.

text.NRSV: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.
text.Rotherham: But there will come days, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and, then, they will fast, in that day.
text.KJV: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
text.NABRE: But the days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.
text.DRB: But the days will come when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them; and then they shall fast in those days.

text.NRSV: “No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak; otherwise, the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and a worse tear is made.
text.Rotherham: No one, seweth a patch of unshrunk cloth upon an old mantle,—otherwise, at least, the shrinking teareth away from it—the new from the old—and, a worse rent is made.
text.KJV: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
text.NABRE: No one sews a piece of unshrunken cloth on an old cloak. If he does, its fullness pulls away, the new from the old, and the tear gets worse.
text.DRB: Nobody soweth a piece of raw cloth to an old garment: otherwise he taketh away the new piecing from the old, and there is made a greater rent.

text.NRSV: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and so are the skins; but one puts new wine into fresh wineskins.”
text.Rotherham: And, no one, poureth new wine into fold skins,—otherwise, at least, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and, the skins. [But new wine is for unused skins.]
text.KJV: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
text.NABRE: Likewise, no one pours new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the skins are ruined. Rather, new wine is poured into fresh wineskins.” The Disciples and the Sabbath.
text.DRB: And nobody putteth new wine into old bottles: otherwise the wine bursteth the bottles, and the wine will he shed, and the bottles will be lost. But new wine must be put into new bottles.

text.NRSV: One sabbath he was going through the grainfields; and as they made their way his disciples began to pluck heads of grain.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that he, on the Sabbath, was passing through the cornfields, and his disciples began to be going forward, plucking the ears of corn.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
text.NABRE: As he was passing through a field of grain on the sabbath, his disciples began to make a path while picking the heads of grain.
text.DRB: And it came to pass again when he walked through the corn on the Sabboths, and his Disciples began to go forward and to pluck the ears.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?”
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, were saying,—See! why are they doing, on the Sabbath, what is not allowed?
text.KJV: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
text.NABRE: At this the Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the sabbath?”
text.DRB: And the Pharisees said to him: Behold, why do they on the Sabboths that which is not lawful?

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need of food?
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Have ye never read what David did, when he had need and hungered,—he, and they who were with him:
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Have you never read what David did when he was in need and he and his companions were hungry?
text.DRB: And he said to them: Did you never read what David did, when he was in necessity, and himself was an hungered and they that were with him?

text.NRSV: How he entered the house of God, when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and he gave some to his companions?”
text.Rotherham: [how] he entered into the house of God, while Abiathar was High-priest, and, the presence-bread, did eat,—which it is not allowed to eat, save unto the priests,—and gave, unto them also who were with him?
text.KJV: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
text.NABRE: How he went into the house of God when Abiathar was high priest and ate the bread of offering that only the priests could lawfully eat, and shared it with his companions?”
text.DRB: how he entered into the house of God under Abiathar the high Priest, and did eat the loaves of Proposition, which it was not lawful to eat but for the Priests, and did give unto them which were with him?

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “The sabbath was made for humankind, and not humankind for the sabbath;
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—The Sabbath, for man, was made, and not, man, for, the Sabbath:
text.KJV: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “The sabbath was made for man, not man for the sabbath.
text.DRB: And he said to them: The Sabboth was made for man, and not man for the Sabboth.

text.NRSV: so the Son of Man is lord even of the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: So that the Son of Man is, Lord, even of the Sabbath.
text.KJV: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
text.NABRE: That is why the Son of Man is lord even of the sabbath.”
text.DRB: Therefore the Son of man is Lord of the Sabboth also.

text.NRSV: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
text.Rotherham: And he entered again into a synagogue, and there-was there, a man having, his hand, withered;
text.KJV: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 A Man with a Withered Hand. Again he entered the synagogue. There was a man there who had a withered hand.
text.DRB: And he entered again into the Synagogue, and there was a man there that had a withered hand.

text.NRSV: They watched him to see whether he would cure him on the sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
text.Rotherham: and they were narrowly watching him, whether, on the Sabbath, he would cure him, that they might accuse him.
text.KJV: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
text.NABRE: They watched him closely to see if he would cure him on the sabbath so that they might accuse him.
text.DRB: And they watched him whether he would cure on the Sabboths; that they might accuse him.

text.NRSV: And he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come forward.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto the man who hath his hand withered, Arise into the midst!
text.KJV: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
text.NABRE: He said to the man with the withered hand, “Come up here before us.”
text.DRB: And he saith to the man that had the withered hand: rise up into the midst.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath, or to do harm, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
text.Rotherham: and saith unto them—Is it allowed, on the Sabbath, to do good, or to do evil? To, save, life, or, to slay? but they remained silent.
text.KJV: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath rather than to do evil, to save life rather than to destroy it?” But they remained silent.
text.DRB: And he saith to them: Is it lawful on the Sabboths to do well or ill? to save a soul, or to destroy? but they held their peace.

text.NRSV: He looked around at them with anger; he was grieved at their hardness of heart and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
text.Rotherham: And, looking round upon them with anger, being at the same time grieved on account of the hardening of their heart, he saith unto the man—Stretch forth thy hand! and he stretched it forth, and his hand, was restored.
text.KJV: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
text.NABRE: Looking around at them with anger and grieved at their hardness of heart, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out and his hand was restored.
text.DRB: And looking round about upon them with anger, being sorrowful for the blindness of their heart, he saith to the man: Stretch forth thy hand. And he stretched it forth; and his hand was restored unto him.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees went out and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, going out straightway with the Herodians, were giving counsel against him, that they should, destroy, him.
text.KJV: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
text.NABRE: The Pharisees went out and immediately took counsel with the Herodians against him to put him to death.
text.DRB: And the Pharisees going forth, immediately made a consultation with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.

text.NRSV: Jesus departed with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him;
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, with his disciples, retired unto the sea; and, a great throng from Galilee, followed, also from Judaea,
text.KJV: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judea,
text.NABRE: The Mercy of Jesus. Jesus withdrew toward the sea with his disciples. A large number of people [followed] from Galilee and from Judea.
text.DRB: But Jesus with his Disciples retired to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee & Jewry followed him,

text.NRSV: hearing all that he was doing, they came to him in great numbers from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the region around Tyre and Sidon.
text.Rotherham: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and beyond the Jordan, and around Tyre and Zidon, a great throng, hearing whatsoever things he was doing, came unto him.
text.KJV: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumea, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
text.NABRE: Hearing what he was doing, a large number of people came to him also from Jerusalem, from Idumea, from beyond the Jordan, and from the neighborhood of Tyre and Sidon.
text.DRB: and from Jerusalem, & from Idumæa, and beyond Jordan. And they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, hearing the things which he did, came to him.

text.NRSV: He told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, so that they would not crush him;
text.Rotherham: And he spake unto his disciples that a little boat might attend him, because of the multitude,—that they might not be pressing upon him.
text.KJV: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
text.NABRE: He told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, so that they would not crush him.
text.DRB: And he spake to his Disciples that a boat might attend on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.

text.NRSV: for he had cured so many that all who had diseases pressed upon him to touch him.
text.Rotherham: For he cured many, so that they were besieging him, that they might touch him—as many as had plagues;
text.KJV: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
text.NABRE: He had cured many and, as a result, those who had diseases were pressing upon him to touch him.
text.DRB: For he healed many, so that there pressed in upon him for to touch him, as many had hurts.

text.NRSV: Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and shouted, “You are the Son of God!”
text.Rotherham: and the impure spirits, as soon as they beheld him, were falling down to him, and crying aloud, while he was speaking, Thou, art the Son of God!
text.KJV: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
text.NABRE: And whenever unclean spirits saw him they would fall down before him and shout, “You are the Son of God.”
text.DRB: And the unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down unto him: and they cried saying:

text.NRSV: But he sternly ordered them not to make him known.
text.Rotherham: and sternly was he rebuking them, lest they should make him manifest.
text.KJV: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
text.NABRE: He warned them sternly not to make him known.
text.DRB: Thou art the Son of God. And he vehemently charged them that they should not disclose him.

text.NRSV: He went up the mountain and called to him those whom he wanted, and they came to him.
text.Rotherham: And he goeth up into the mountain and calleth near whom, he, pleased,—and they went away unto him;
text.KJV: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.
text.NABRE: The Mission of the Twelve. He went up the mountain and summoned those whom he wanted and they came to him.
text.DRB: And ascending into a mountain, he called unto him whom he would himself, and they came to him.

text.NRSV: And he appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him, and to be sent out to proclaim the message,
text.Rotherham: and he appointed a twelve, whom also he named, Apostles,—that they might be with him, and that he might be sending them forth to make proclamation,
text.KJV: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
text.NABRE: He appointed twelve [whom he also named apostles] that they might be with him and he might send them forth to preach
text.DRB: And he made that twelve should be with him, and that he might send them to preach.

text.NRSV: and to have authority to cast out demons.
text.Rotherham: and to have authority to cast out the demons;
text.KJV: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:
text.NABRE: and to have authority to drive out demons:
text.DRB: And he gave them power to cure infirmities, and to cast out devils.

text.NRSV: So he appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
text.Rotherham: and he appointed the twelve (and imposed a name on Simon—) Peter,
text.KJV: And Simon he surnamed Peter;
text.NABRE: [he appointed the twelve:] Simon, whom he named Peter;
text.DRB: And he gave to Simon the name Peter.

text.NRSV: James son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);
text.Rotherham: and James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James (and imposed on them a name—Boanerges, that is, sons of thunder);
text.KJV: And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:
text.NABRE: James, son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James, whom he named Boanerges, that is, sons of thunder;
text.DRB: And James of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he called their names, Boanerges, which is, the sons of thunder.

text.NRSV: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,
text.Rotherham: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the zealot;
text.KJV: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alpheus, and Thaddeus, and Simon the Canaanite,
text.NABRE: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus; Thaddeus, Simon the Cananean,
text.DRB: And Andrew and Philippe, and Barthlemew and Matthew, and Thomas and James of Alphæus, and Thaddæus and Simon Cananæus,

text.NRSV: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
text.Rotherham: and Judas Iscariot, who also delivered him up. And he cometh into a house;
text.KJV: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
text.NABRE: and Judas Iscariot who betrayed him.
text.DRB: and Judas Iscariote, who also betrayed him.

text.NRSV: Then he went home; and the crowd came together again, so that they could not even eat.
text.Rotherham: and the multitude come together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
text.KJV: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
text.NABRE: Blasphemy of the Scribes. He came home. Again [the] crowd gathered, making it impossible for them even to eat.
text.DRB: And they come to a house; and the multitude resorteth together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

text.NRSV: When his family heard it, they went out to restrain him, for people were saying, “He has gone out of his mind.”
text.Rotherham: And they who were near to him, hearing of it, went out to secure him,—for they were saying—He is beside himself!
text.KJV: And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
text.NABRE: When his relatives heard of this they set out to seize him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.”
text.DRB: And when his had heard of it, they went forth to lay hands on him. For they said that he was become mad.

text.NRSV: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul, and by the ruler of the demons he casts out demons.”
text.Rotherham: And, the Scribes who from Jerusalem had come down, were saying—He hath, Beelzebul; and—In the ruler of the demons, casteth he out the demons.
text.KJV: And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
text.NABRE: The scribes who had come from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,” and “By the prince of demons he drives out demons.”
text.DRB: And the Scribes which were come down from Jerusalem, said: That he hath Beelzebub; and that in the prince of devils he casteth out devils.

text.NRSV: And he called them to him, and spoke to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
text.Rotherham: And, calling them near, in parables, began he to say unto them—How can, Satan, cast, Satan out?
text.KJV: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
text.NABRE: Jesus and Beelzebul. Summoning them, he began to speak to them in parables, “How can Satan drive out Satan?
text.DRB: And after he had called them together, he said to them in parables: How can Satan cast out Satan?

text.NRSV: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
text.Rotherham: And, if a kingdom against itself be divided, that kingdom cannot be made to stand;
text.KJV: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
text.NABRE: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
text.DRB: And if a Kingdom be divided against itself, that Kingdom cannot stand.

text.NRSV: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
text.Rotherham: And, if a house against itself be divided, the house shall be unable to stand;
text.KJV: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
text.NABRE: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
text.DRB: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

text.NRSV: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but his end has come.
text.Rotherham: And, if, Satan, hath risen up against himself, and become divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
text.KJV: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
text.NABRE: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand; that is the end of him.
text.DRB: And if Satan be risen against himself, he is divided, and cannot stand, but hath an end.

text.NRSV: But no one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his property without first tying up the strong man; then indeed the house can be plundered.
text.Rotherham: But, no one is able into the house of the mighty one to enter, and, his spoils, to carry off, unless, first the mighty one, he bind, and, then his house, will he plunder!
text.KJV: No man can enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
text.NABRE: But no one can enter a strong man’s house to plunder his property unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can plunder his house.
text.DRB: Nobody can rifle the vessels of the strong, being entered into his house, unless he first bind the strong, & then shall he rifle his house.

text.NRSV: “Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for their sins and whatever blasphemies they utter;
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—All things shall be forgiven unto the sons of men,—the sins and the profanities wherewithal they shall profane;
text.KJV: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, all sins and all blasphemies that people utter will be forgiven them.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, that all sins shall be forgiven the sons of men, & the blasphemies wherewith they shall blaspheme:

text.NRSV: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit can never have forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin”—
text.Rotherham: But, whosoever shall revile against the Holy Spirit, hath no forgiveness, unto times age-abiding,—but is guilty of an age-abiding sin:
text.KJV: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:
text.NABRE: But whoever blasphemes against the holy Spirit will never have forgiveness, but is guilty of an everlasting sin.”
text.DRB: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost, he hath not forgiveness for ever, but shall be guilty of an eternal sin. Because they said: He hath an unclean spirit.

text.NRSV: for they had said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
text.Rotherham: because they were saying—An impure spirit, he hath!
text.KJV: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
text.NABRE: For they had said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
text.DRB: And there come his mother and brethren; and standing without they sent unto him calling him,

text.NRSV: Then his mother and his brothers came; and standing outside, they sent to him and called him.
text.Rotherham: And there come his mother and his brethren, and, standing, outside, they sent unto him, calling him.
text.KJV: There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.
text.NABRE: Jesus and His Family. His mother and his brothers arrived. Standing outside they sent word to him and called him.
text.DRB: And the multitude sat about him; and they say to him:

text.NRSV: A crowd was sitting around him; and they said to him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside, asking for you.”
text.Rotherham: And there was sitting around him a multitude, and they say unto him—Lo! thy mother and thy brethren, outside, are seeking thee!
text.KJV: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
text.NABRE: A crowd seated around him told him, “Your mother and your brothers [and your sisters] are outside asking for you.”
text.DRB: Behold thy mother and thy brethren without seek thee.

text.NRSV: And he replied, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
text.Rotherham: And, answering them, he saith—Who are my mother and my brethren?
text.KJV: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
text.NABRE: But he said to them in reply, “Who are my mother and [my] brothers?”
text.DRB: And answering them, he said: Who is my mother and my brethren?

text.NRSV: And looking at those who sat around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
text.Rotherham: And, looking around on them who, about him, in a circle, were sitting, he saith, See! my mother and my brethren!
text.KJV: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
text.NABRE: And looking around at those seated in the circle he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text.DRB: And looking upon them which sat round about him, he saith: Behold my mother and my brethren.

text.NRSV: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
text.Rotherham: Whosoever shall do the will of God, the same, is my brother and sister and mother.
text.KJV: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.
text.NABRE: [For] whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
text.DRB: For whosoever shall do the will of God, he is my brother and my sister and mother.

text.NRSV: Again he began to teach beside the sea. Such a very large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat on the sea and sat there, while the whole crowd was beside the sea on the land.
text.Rotherham: And, again, began he to be teaching by the sea; and there come together unto him a very great multitude, so that, he, into a boat, entering, was sitting upon the sea,—and, all the multitude, were, near the sea upon the land.
text.KJV: And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 The Parable of the Sower. On another occasion he began to teach by the sea. A very large crowd gathered around him so that he got into a boat on the sea and sat down. And the whole crowd was beside the sea on land.
text.DRB: And again he began to teach at the sea side; and a great multitude was gathered together unto him so that he went up into a boat, and sat in the sea, and all the multitude about the sea was upon the land.

text.NRSV: He began to teach them many things in parables, and in his teaching he said to them:
text.Rotherham: And he began to teach them, in parables, many things, and was saying unto them, in his teaching—
text.KJV: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,
text.NABRE: And he taught them at length in parables, and in the course of his instruction he said to them,
text.DRB: And he taught them in parables many things, and said to them in his doctrine:

text.NRSV: “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
text.Rotherham: Hearken! Lo! the sower went forth to sow;
text.KJV: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:
text.NABRE: “Hear this! A sower went out to sow.
text.DRB: Hear ye; Behold, the sower went forth to sow.

text.NRSV: And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some, indeed, fell by the pathway, and the birds came, and devoured it;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
text.NABRE: And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
text.DRB: And whiles he soweth, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came, and did eat it.

text.NRSV: Other seed fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil, and it sprang up quickly, since it had no depth of soil.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell on the rocky places, [even] where it had not much earth,—and, straightway, it sprang forth, by reason of its not having depth of earth;
text.KJV: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
text.NABRE: Other seed fell on rocky ground where it had little soil. It sprang up at once because the soil was not deep.
text.DRB: And othersome fell upon rocky places where it had not much earth: and it shot up immediately, because it had not deepness of earth:

text.NRSV: And when the sun rose, it was scorched; and since it had no root, it withered away.
text.Rotherham: and, when the sun arose, it was scorched,—and, by reason of its not having root, it was dried up;
text.KJV: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
text.NABRE: And when the sun rose, it was scorched and it withered for lack of roots.
text.DRB: and when the sun was risen, it parched, and because it had not root, it withered.

text.NRSV: Other seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no grain.
text.Rotherham: And, some, fell among the thorns, and the thorns came up and choked it, and, fruit, it yielded not;
text.KJV: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
text.NABRE: Some seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it and it produced no grain.
text.DRB: And some fell among thorns: and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded not fruit.

text.NRSV: Other seed fell into good soil and brought forth grain, growing up and increasing and yielding thirty and sixty and a hundredfold.”
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell into the good ground, and was yielding fruit, coming up and growing,—and was bearing, thirtyfold and sixtyfold and a hundredfold,
text.KJV: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
text.NABRE: And some seed fell on rich soil and produced fruit. It came up and grew and yielded thirty, sixty, and a hundredfold.”
text.DRB: And some fell upon good ground; and it yielded fruit that grew up & increased, and it brought forth, one thirty, one sixty, and one an hundred.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Let anyone with ears to hear listen!”
text.Rotherham: And he was saying—He that hath ears to hear, let him hear!
text.KJV: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
text.NABRE: He added, “Whoever has ears to hear ought to hear.”
text.DRB: And he said: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: When he was alone, those who were around him along with the twelve asked him about the parables.
text.Rotherham: And when he was alone they who were about him with the twelve questioned him as to the parables;
text.KJV: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.
text.NABRE: The Purpose of the Parables. And when he was alone, those present along with the Twelve questioned him about the parables.
text.DRB: And when he was alone, the Twelve that were with him, asked him the parable.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “To you has been given the secret of the kingdom of God, but for those outside, everything comes in parables;
text.Rotherham: and he was saying unto them—To you, the sacred secret hath been given of the kingdom of God, whereas, to them who are outside, in parables are all things coming to pass,—that
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:
text.NABRE: He answered them, “The mystery of the kingdom of God has been granted to you. But to those outside everything comes in parables,
text.DRB: And he said to them: To you it is given to know the mystery of the Kingdom of God; but to them that are without, all things are done in parables:

text.NRSV: in order that ‘they may indeed look, but not perceive, and may indeed listen, but not understand; so that they may not turn again and be forgiven.’”
text.Rotherham: They may surely look and yet not see, and surely hear and yet not understand, lest once they should return and it be forgiven them.
text.KJV: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.
text.NABRE: so that ‘they may look and see but not perceive, and hear and listen but not understand, in order that they may not be converted and be forgiven.’”
text.DRB: that seeing they may see, and not see; and hearing they may hear, and not understand: lest at any time they should be converted and their sins be forgiven them.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Know ye not this parable? How then will ye get to know all the parables?
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? Then how will you understand any of the parables?
text.DRB: And he saith to them: Do you not know this parable? And how shall you know all parables?

text.NRSV: The sower sows the word.
text.Rotherham: The sower, soweth the word;
text.KJV: The sower soweth the word.
text.NABRE: The sower sows the word.
text.DRB: He that soweth, soweth the word.

text.NRSV: These are the ones on the path where the word is sown: when they hear, Satan immediately comes and takes away the word that is sown in them.
text.Rotherham: And these are they beside the pathway where the word is sown,—and, as soon as they hear, straightway, cometh Satan, and snatcheth away the word which hath been sown into them;
text.KJV: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
text.NABRE: These are the ones on the path where the word is sown. As soon as they hear, Satan comes at once and takes away the word sown in them.
text.DRB: And they by the way side, are these: where the word is sown, and when they shall have heard, immediately cometh Satan, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.

text.NRSV: And these are the ones sown on rocky ground: when they hear the word, they immediately receive it with joy.
text.Rotherham: And these are likewise they upon the rocky places sown,—who, as soon as they hear the word, straightway, with joy receive it,
text.KJV: And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
text.NABRE: And these are the ones sown on rocky ground who, when they hear the word, receive it at once with joy.
text.DRB: And they likewise that are sown upon the rocky places, are these: who when they hear the word, immediately with joy receive it;

text.NRSV: But they have no root, and endure only for a while; then, when trouble or persecution arises on account of the word, immediately they fall away.
text.Rotherham: and have no root in themselves, but, only for a season are,—afterwards, when there ariseth tribulation or persecution by reason of the word, straightway, they find cause of stumbling;
text.KJV: And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word’s sake, immediately they are offended.
text.NABRE: But they have no root; they last only for a time. Then when tribulation or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.
text.DRB: And they have not root in themselves, but are temporal: afterward when tribulation is risen and persecution for the word, forthwith they are scandalized.

text.NRSV: And others are those sown among the thorns: these are the ones who hear the word,
text.Rotherham: And others are they who among thorns are sown,—these are they who hear the word,
text.KJV: And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,
text.NABRE: Those sown among thorns are another sort. They are the people who hear the word,
text.DRB: And other there be that are sown among thorns: these are they that hear the word,

text.NRSV: but the cares of the world, and the lure of wealth, and the desire for other things come in and choke the word, and it yields nothing.
text.Rotherham: and, the anxieties of the age, and the deceit of wealth, and the covetings about the remaining things, entering in, choke up the word, and, unfruitful, it becometh;
text.KJV: And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.
text.NABRE: but worldly anxiety, the lure of riches, and the craving for other things intrude and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
text.DRB: and the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches, and concupiscences about other things entering in choke the word, and it is made fruitless.

text.NRSV: And these are the ones sown on the good soil: they hear the word and accept it and bear fruit, thirty and sixty and a hundredfold.”
text.Rotherham: And those yonder are they who on the good ground are sown,—who, indeed, hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit—thirtyfold and sixtyfold and a hundredfold.
text.KJV: And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.
text.NABRE: But those sown on rich soil are the ones who hear the word and accept it and bear fruit thirty and sixty and a hundredfold.”
text.DRB: And these are they that were sown upon the good ground, which hear the word, and receive it, and yield fruit one thirty, one sixty, and one an hundred.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Is a lamp brought in to be put under the bushel basket, or under the bed, and not on the lampstand?
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—Doth the lamp come that under the measure it should be put, or under the couch? Is it not that upon the lampstand it may be put?
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?
