text.NABRE: Parable of the Lamp. He said to them, “Is a lamp brought in to be placed under a bushel basket or under a bed, and not to be placed on a lampstand?
text.DRB: And he said to them: Cometh a candle to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? And not to be put upon the candlestick?

text.NRSV: For there is nothing hidden, except to be disclosed; nor is anything secret, except to come to light.
text.Rotherham: For it is not hidden, save that it may be made visible; neither did it get hidden away, but that it might come into a place where it could be seen.
text.KJV: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
text.NABRE: For there is nothing hidden except to be made visible; nothing is secret except to come to light.
text.DRB: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be made manifest: neither was any thing made secret, but that it shall come to light.

text.NRSV: Let anyone with ears to hear listen!”
text.Rotherham: If any one hath ears to hear, let him hear.
text.KJV: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.
text.NABRE: Anyone who has ears to hear ought to hear.”
text.DRB: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Pay attention to what you hear; the measure you give will be the measure you get, and still more will be given you.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—Be taking heed what ye are hearing:—with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured unto you, and added unto you;
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: and unto you that hear shall more be given.
text.NABRE: He also told them, “Take care what you hear. The measure with which you measure will be measured out to you, and still more will be given to you.
text.DRB: And he said to them: See what you hear. In what measure you mete, it shall be measured to you again, and more shall be given to you.

text.NRSV: For to those who have, more will be given; and from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.”
text.Rotherham: For, he that hath, it shall be given, unto him, and, he that hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken from him.
text.KJV: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.
text.NABRE: To the one who has, more will be given; from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken away.”
text.DRB: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, that also which he hath, shall be taken away from him.

text.NRSV: He also said, “The kingdom of God is as if someone would scatter seed on the ground,
text.Rotherham: And he was saying—Thus, is the kingdom of God: As a man may cast seed upon the earth,
text.KJV: And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;
text.NABRE: Seed Grows of Itself. He said, “This is how it is with the kingdom of God; it is as if a man were to scatter seed on the land
text.DRB: And he said: So is the Kingdom of God, as if a man cast seed into the earth,

text.NRSV: and would sleep and rise night and day, and the seed would sprout and grow, he does not know how.
text.Rotherham: and be sleeping and rising, night and day,—and the seed be sprouting, and lengthening itself,—how, he, knoweth not:
text.KJV: And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how.
text.NABRE: and would sleep and rise night and day and the seed would sprout and grow, he knows not how.
text.DRB: and sleep, and rise up night and day, and the seed spring, and grow up whiles he knoweth not.

text.NRSV: The earth produces of itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full grain in the head.
text.Rotherham: of itself, the earth beareth fruit,—first, a blade, afterwards, an ear, after that, full corn in the ear;
text.KJV: For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.
text.NABRE: Of its own accord the land yields fruit, first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.
text.DRB: For the earth of itself bringeth forth fruit, first the blade, then the ear, afterward the full corn in the ear.

text.NRSV: But when the grain is ripe, at once he goes in with his sickle, because the harvest has come.”
text.Rotherham: but, as soon as the fruit yieldeth itself up, straightway, he sendeth forth the sickle, because standing by is the harvest.—
text.KJV: But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.
text.NABRE: And when the grain is ripe, he wields the sickle at once, for the harvest has come.”
text.DRB: And when the fruit hath brought out itself, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because harvest is come.

text.NRSV: He also said, “With what can we compare the kingdom of God, or what parable shall we use for it?
text.Rotherham: And he was saying—How shall we liken the kingdom of God, or, in what parable, shall we put it?
text.KJV: And he said, Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?
text.NABRE: The Mustard Seed. He said, “To what shall we compare the kingdom of God, or what parable can we use for it?
text.DRB: And he said: To what shall we liken the Kingdom of God? or to what parable shall we compare it?

text.NRSV: It is like a mustard seed, which, when sown upon the ground, is the smallest of all the seeds on earth;
text.Rotherham: As a grain of mustard seed,—which, whensoever it may be sown upon the earth, is less than all the seeds that are upon the earth;
text.KJV: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:
text.NABRE: It is like a mustard seed that, when it is sown in the ground, is the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
text.DRB: As a mustard seed; which when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that are in the earth:

text.NRSV: yet when it is sown it grows up and becomes the greatest of all shrubs, and puts forth large branches, so that the birds of the air can make nests in its shade.”
text.Rotherham: and, as soon as it is sown, springeth up, and becometh greater than all garden plants, and produceth large branches, so that, under the shade thereof, the birds of heaven can find shelter.
text.KJV: But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.
text.NABRE: But once it is sown, it springs up and becomes the largest of plants and puts forth large branches, so that the birds of the sky can dwell in its shade.”
text.DRB: And when it is sown, it riseth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and maketh great boughs, so that the birds of the air may dwell under the shadow thereof.

text.NRSV: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it;
text.Rotherham: And, with many such parables as these, was he speaking unto them the word,—according as they were able to hear;
text.KJV: And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were able to hear it.
text.NABRE: With many such parables he spoke the word to them as they were able to understand it.
text.DRB: And with many such parables he spake to them the word, according as they were able to hear:

text.NRSV: he did not speak to them except in parables, but he explained everything in private to his own disciples.
text.Rotherham: but, without a parable, was he not speaking unto them,—privately, however, unto his own disciples, was he explaining all things.
text.KJV: But without a parable spake he not unto them: and when they were alone, he expounded all things to his disciples.
text.NABRE: Without parables he did not speak to them, but to his own disciples he explained everything in private.
text.DRB: and without parable he did not speak unto them; but apart, he explicated all things to his Disciples.

text.NRSV: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let us go across to the other side.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them, on that day, when evening came, Let us cross over unto the other side;
text.KJV: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.
text.NABRE: The Calming of a Storm at Sea. On that day, as evening drew on, he said to them, “Let us cross to the other side.”
text.DRB: And he saith to them in that day, when evening was come: Let us pass over to the other side.

text.NRSV: And leaving the crowd, they took him with them in the boat, just as he was. Other boats were with him.
text.Rotherham: and, dismissing the multitude, they take him with them, as he was, in the boat, and, other boats, were with him.
text.KJV: And when they had sent away the multitude, they took him even as he was in the ship. And there were also with him other little ships.
text.NABRE: Leaving the crowd, they took him with them in the boat just as he was. And other boats were with him.
text.DRB: And dismissing the multitude, they take him so as he was in the boat: and there were other boats with him.

text.NRSV: A great windstorm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so that the boat was already being swamped.
text.Rotherham: And there ariseth a great tempest of wind,—and, the waves, were dashing over into the boat, so that, already being filled, was the boat.
text.KJV: And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it was now full.
text.NABRE: A violent squall came up and waves were breaking over the boat, so that it was already filling up.
text.DRB: And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the boat, so that the boat was filled.

text.NRSV: But he was in the stern, asleep on the cushion; and they woke him up and said to him, “Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?”
text.Rotherham: And he was in the stern, on the cushion, sleeping. And they arouse him, and say unto him, Teacher! carest thou not that we perish?
text.KJV: And he was in the hinder part of the ship, asleep on a pillow: and they awake him, and say unto him, Master, carest thou not that we perish?
text.NABRE: Jesus was in the stern, asleep on a cushion. They woke him and said to him, “Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?”
text.DRB: And he was in the hinder part of the boat, sleeping upon a pillow; and they raise him, and say to him: Master, doth it not pertain to thee that we perish?

text.NRSV: He woke up and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” Then the wind ceased, and there was a dead calm.
text.Rotherham: And, roused up, he rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea—Hush! be still! and the wind lulled, and it became a great calm.
text.KJV: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
text.NABRE: He woke up, rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Quiet! Be still!” The wind ceased and there was great calm.
text.DRB: And rising up he threatened the wind, and said to the sea: Peace, be still. And the wind ceased; and there was made a great calm.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Why are you afraid? Have you still no faith?”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Why, are ye, fearful? Not yet, have ye faith?
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Why are ye so fearful? how is it that ye have no faith?
text.NABRE: Then he asked them, “Why are you terrified? Do you not yet have faith?”
text.DRB: And he said to them: Why are you fearful? Neither yet have you faith? And they feared with great fear: and they said one to another, who is this (thinkest thou) that both wind and sea obey him?

text.NRSV: And they were filled with great awe and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”
text.Rotherham: And they were caused to fear a great fear, and were saying one to another—Who then is, this, that, both the wind and the sea, give ear unto him?
text.KJV: And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?
text.NABRE: They were filled with great awe and said to one another, “Who then is this whom even wind and sea obey?”

text.NRSV: They came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gerasenes.
text.Rotherham: And they came unto the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gerasenes.
text.KJV: And they came over unto the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 The Healing of the Gerasene Demoniac. They came to the other side of the sea, to the territory of the Gerasenes.
text.DRB: And they came beyond the strait of the sea into the country of the Gerasens.

text.NRSV: And when he had stepped out of the boat, immediately a man out of the tombs with an unclean spirit met him.
text.Rotherham: And, when he went forth out of the boat, [straightway], there met him, from amongst the tombs, a man, in an impure spirit:
text.KJV: And when he was come out of the ship, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,
text.NABRE: When he got out of the boat, at once a man from the tombs who had an unclean spirit met him.
text.DRB: And as he went out of the boat, immediately there met him out of the sepulchres a man in an unclean spirit,

text.NRSV: He lived among the tombs; and no one could restrain him any more, even with a chain;
text.Rotherham: who had his dwelling among the tombs, and, not even with a chain, any longer, was anyone able to bind him,—
text.KJV: Who had his dwelling among the tombs; and no man could bind him, no, not with chains:
text.NABRE: The man had been dwelling among the tombs, and no one could restrain him any longer, even with a chain.
text.DRB: that had his dwelling in the sepulchres, and neither with chains could any man now bind him:

text.NRSV: for he had often been restrained with shackles and chains, but the chains he wrenched apart, and the shackles he broke in pieces; and no one had the strength to subdue him.
text.Rotherham: because of his having been, many times, with fetters and chains, bound, and the chains having been plucked asunder by him, and the fetters smashed, and no one was mighty enough to tame him;
text.KJV: Because that he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been plucked asunder by him, and the fetters broken in pieces: neither could any man tame him.
text.NABRE: In fact, he had frequently been bound with shackles and chains, but the chains had been pulled apart by him and the shackles smashed, and no one was strong enough to subdue him.
text.DRB: For being often bound with fetters and chains, he had burst the chains, and broken the fetters, and nobody could tame him.

text.NRSV: Night and day among the tombs and on the mountains he was always howling and bruising himself with stones.
text.Rotherham: and, continually, night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, was he crying aloud, and cutting himself in pieces with stones.
text.KJV: And always, night and day, he was in the mountains, and in the tombs, crying, and cutting himself with stones.
text.NABRE: Night and day among the tombs and on the hillsides he was always crying out and bruising himself with stones.
text.DRB: And he was always day and night in the sepulchres and in the mountains, crying and cutting himself with stones.

text.NRSV: When he saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and bowed down before him;
text.Rotherham: And, seeing Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down to him;
text.KJV: But when he saw Jesus afar off, he ran and worshipped him,
text.NABRE: Catching sight of Jesus from a distance, he ran up and prostrated himself before him,
text.DRB: And seeing Jesus a far off, he ran and adored him:

text.NRSV: and he shouted at the top of his voice, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, do not torment me.”
text.Rotherham: and, crying out with a loud voice, saith—What have in common with thee? O Jesus, Son of God Most High? I adjure thee by God,—Do not torment me!
text.KJV: And cried with a loud voice, and said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of the most high God? I adjure thee by God, that thou torment me not.
text.NABRE: crying out in a loud voice, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, do not torment me!”
text.DRB: And crying with a great voice, said: What to me and thee Jesus, the Son of God most high: I adjure thee by God that thou torment me not.

text.NRSV: For he had said to him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”
text.Rotherham: For he was saying to him—Go forth thou impure spirit, out of the man;
text.KJV: For he said unto him, Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit.
text.NABRE: (He had been saying to him, “Unclean spirit, come out of the man!”)
text.DRB: For he said unto him: Go out of the man thou unclean spirit.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” He replied, “My name is Legion; for we are many.”
text.Rotherham: and he was questioning him—What is thy name? and he saith to him—Legion, is my name, because we are, many;
text.KJV: And he asked him, What is thy name? And he answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.
text.NABRE: He asked him, “What is your name?” He replied, “Legion is my name. There are many of us.”
text.DRB: And he asked him, what is thy name? And he saith to him: My name is Legion; because we are many.

text.NRSV: He begged him earnestly not to send them out of the country.
text.Rotherham: and he was beseeching him much that he would not send them forth outside the country.
text.KJV: And he besought him much that he would not send them away out of the country.
text.NABRE: And he pleaded earnestly with him not to drive them away from that territory.
text.DRB: And he besought him much, that he would not expel him out of the country.

text.NRSV: Now on the hillside a large herd of swine was feeding;
text.Rotherham: Now there was there, near the mountain, a large herd of swine, feeding;
text.KJV: Now there was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding.
text.NABRE: Now a large herd of swine was feeding there on the hillside.
text.DRB: And there was there about the mountain a great herd of swine, feeding.

text.NRSV: and the unclean spirits begged him, “Send us into the swine; let us enter them.”
text.Rotherham: and they besought him, saying—Send us into the swine, that, into them, we may enter;
text.KJV: And all the devils besought him, saying, Send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.
text.NABRE: And they pleaded with him, “Send us into the swine. Let us enter them.”
text.DRB: And the spirits besought him, saying: Send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.

text.NRSV: So he gave them permission. And the unclean spirits came out and entered the swine; and the herd, numbering about two thousand, rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and were drowned in the sea.
text.Rotherham: and he suffered them. And the impure spirits, going out, entered into the swine, and the herd rushed down the cliff into the sea, about two thousand, and were choked in the sea.
text.KJV: And forthwith Jesus gave them leave. And the unclean spirits went out, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea, (they were about two thousand;) and were choked in the sea.
text.NABRE: And he let them, and the unclean spirits came out and entered the swine. The herd of about two thousand rushed down a steep bank into the sea, where they were drowned.
text.DRB: And Jesus immediately granted unto them. And the unclean spirits going out entered into the swine: and the herd with great violence was carried headlong into the sea, about two thousand, and were stifled in the sea.

text.NRSV: The herdsmen ran off and told it in the city and in the country. Then people came to see what it was that had happened.
text.Rotherham: And, they who had been feeding them, fled, and bare tidings into the city and into the fields,—and they came to see what it was that had happened.
text.KJV: And they that fed the swine fled, and told it in the city, and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that was done.
text.NABRE: The swineherds ran away and reported the incident in the town and throughout the countryside. And people came out to see what had happened.
text.DRB: And they that fed them, fled and carried news into the city and into the fields. And they went forth to see what was done:

text.NRSV: They came to Jesus and saw the demoniac sitting there, clothed and in his right mind, the very man who had had the legion; and they were afraid.
text.Rotherham: And they come unto Jesus, and view the demonized man, clothed and of sound mind, him who had had the legion,—and they were struck with fear.
text.KJV: And they come to Jesus, and see him that was possessed with the devil, and had the legion, sitting, and clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
text.NABRE: As they approached Jesus, they caught sight of the man who had been possessed by Legion, sitting there clothed and in his right mind. And they were seized with fear.
text.DRB: And they come to Jesus, and they see him that was vexed of the devil, sitting, clothed, and well in his wits: & they were afraid.

text.NRSV: Those who had seen what had happened to the demoniac and to the swine reported it.
text.Rotherham: And the beholders narrated to them, how it happened to the demonized man, and concerning the swine.
text.KJV: And they that saw it told them how it befell to him that was possessed with the devil, and also concerning the swine.
text.NABRE: Those who witnessed the incident explained to them what had happened to the possessed man and to the swine.
text.DRB: And they that had seen it, told them, in what manner he had been dealt withal that had the devil; & of the swine.

text.NRSV: Then they began to beg Jesus to leave their neighborhood.
text.Rotherham: And they began to beseech him, to depart from their bounds.
text.KJV: And they began to pray him to depart out of their coasts.
text.NABRE: Then they began to beg him to leave their district.
text.DRB: And they began to desire him, that he would depart from their coasts.

text.NRSV: As he was getting into the boat, the man who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.
text.Rotherham: And, as he was entering into the boat, he who had been demonized was beseeching him, that, with him, he might be.
text.KJV: And when he was come into the ship, he that had been possessed with the devil prayed him that he might be with him.
text.NABRE: As he was getting into the boat, the man who had been possessed pleaded to remain with him.
text.DRB: And when he went up into the boat, he that had been vexed of the devil, began to beseech him that he might be with him:

text.NRSV: But Jesus refused, and said to him, “Go home to your friends, and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and what mercy he has shown you.”
text.Rotherham: And he suffered him not, but saith unto him—Withdraw into thy house, unto thine own, and bear tidings unto them, how many things the Lord for thee hath done, and hath had mercy on thee.
text.KJV: Howbeit Jesus suffered him not, but saith unto him, Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.
text.NABRE: But he would not permit him but told him instead, “Go home to your family and announce to them all that the Lord in his pity has done for you.”
text.DRB: And he admitted him not, but saith to him: Go into thy house to thine, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had mercy upon thee.

text.NRSV: And he went away and began to proclaim in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him; and everyone was amazed.
text.Rotherham: And he departed, and began proclaiming, in the Decapolis, how many things Jesus had done for him,—and, all, were marvelling.
text.KJV: And he departed, and began to publish in Decapolis how great things Jesus had done for him: and all men did marvel.
text.NABRE: Then the man went off and began to proclaim in the Decapolis what Jesus had done for him; and all were amazed. Jairus’s Daughter and the Woman with a Hemorrhage.
text.DRB: And he went his way, and began to publish in Decapolis how great things Jesus had done to him: and all marveled.

text.NRSV: When Jesus had crossed again in the boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around him; and he was by the sea.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus crossing over in the boat again unto the other side, there was gathered a large multitude unto him, and he was by the lake.
text.KJV: And when Jesus was passed over again by ship unto the other side, much people gathered unto him: and he was nigh unto the sea.
text.NABRE: When Jesus had crossed again [in the boat] to the other side, a large crowd gathered around him, and he stayed close to the sea.
text.DRB: And when Jesus had passed in boat again over the strait, a great multitude assembled together unto him, and he was about the sea.

text.NRSV: Then one of the leaders of the synagogue named Jairus came and, when he saw him, fell at his feet
text.Rotherham: And there cometh one of the synagogue-rulers, by name Jairus, and, seeing him, falleth at his feet,
text.KJV: And, behold, there cometh one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name; and when he saw him, he fell at his feet,
text.NABRE: One of the synagogue officials, named Jairus, came forward. Seeing him he fell at his feet
text.DRB: And there cometh one of the Arch-synagogues, named Jairus: and seeing him, he falleth down at his feet.

text.NRSV: and begged him repeatedly, “My little daughter is at the point of death. Come and lay your hands on her, so that she may be made well, and live.”
text.Rotherham: and beseecheth him much, saying—My little daughter is at her last!—that, coming, thou wouldest lay thy hands upon her, that she may be made well, and live;
text.KJV: And besought him greatly, saying, My little daughter lieth at the point of death: I pray thee, come and lay thy hands on her, that she may be healed; and she shall live.
text.NABRE: and pleaded earnestly with him, saying, “My daughter is at the point of death. Please, come lay your hands on her that she may get well and live.”
text.DRB: And besought him much, saying: That my daughter is at the point of death, come, impose thy hands upon her, that she may be safe and live.

text.NRSV: So he went with him. And a large crowd followed him and pressed in on him.
text.Rotherham: and he departed with him. And there was following him a large multitude, and they were pressing upon him.
text.KJV: And Jesus went with him; and much people followed him, and thronged him.
text.NABRE: He went off with him, and a large crowd followed him and pressed upon him.
text.DRB: And he went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they thronged him.

text.NRSV: Now there was a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman, who had had a flow of blood twelve years,
text.KJV: And a certain woman, which had an issue of blood twelve years,
text.NABRE: There was a woman afflicted with hemorrhages for twelve years.
text.DRB: And a woman which was in an issue of blood twelve years,

text.NRSV: She had endured much under many physicians, and had spent all that she had; and she was no better, but rather grew worse.
text.Rotherham: and suffered much from many physicians, and spent all her means, and profited, nothing,—but had, rather, become worse,
text.KJV: And had suffered many things of many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was nothing bettered, but rather grew worse,
text.NABRE: She had suffered greatly at the hands of many doctors and had spent all that she had. Yet she was not helped but only grew worse.
text.DRB: and had suffered many things of many Physicians, and had bestowed all that she had, neither was any thing the better, but was rather worse:

text.NRSV: She had heard about Jesus, and came up behind him in the crowd and touched his cloak;
text.Rotherham: hearing the things about Jesus, came in the crowd from behind, and touched his mantle;
text.KJV: When she had heard of Jesus, came in the press behind, and touched his garment.
text.NABRE: She had heard about Jesus and came up behind him in the crowd and touched his cloak.
text.DRB: when she had heard of Jesus, she came in the press behind him, and touched his garment.

text.NRSV: for she said, “If I but touch his clothes, I will be made well.”
text.Rotherham: for she was saying—If I can touch so much as his mantle, I shall be made well;
text.KJV: For she said, If I may touch but his clothes, I shall be whole.
text.NABRE: She said, “If I but touch his clothes, I shall be cured.”
text.DRB: For she said: That if I shall touch but his garment, I shall be safe.

text.NRSV: Immediately her hemorrhage stopped; and she felt in her body that she was healed of her disease.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, the fountain of her blood was dried up, and she knew, in her body, that she was healed from the plague.
text.KJV: And straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague.
text.NABRE: Immediately her flow of blood dried up. She felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.
text.DRB: And forthwith the fountain of her blood was dried; and she felt in her body that she was healed of the malady.

text.NRSV: Immediately aware that power had gone forth from him, Jesus turned about in the crowd and said, “Who touched my clothes?”
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, Jesus, taking note in himself of the power which, out of him, went forth, turning round in the crowd, was saying—Who hath touched my garments?
text.KJV: And Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that virtue had gone out of him, turned him about in the press, and said, Who touched my clothes?
text.NABRE: Jesus, aware at once that power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd and asked, “Who has touched my clothes?”
text.DRB: And immediately Jesus knowing in himself the virtue that had proceeded from him, turning to the multitude, said: Who hath touched my garments?

text.NRSV: And his disciples said to him, “You see the crowd pressing in on you; how can you say, ‘Who touched me?’”
text.Rotherham: and his disciples were saying to him—Thou seest the crowd pressing upon thee,—and sayest thou—Who hath touched me?
text.KJV: And his disciples said unto him, Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
text.NABRE: But his disciples said to him, “You see how the crowd is pressing upon you, and yet you ask, ‘Who touched me?’”
text.DRB: And his Disciples said to him: Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, & sayest thou, who hath touched me?

text.NRSV: He looked around to see who had done it.
text.Rotherham: and he was looking round to see her who, this thing had done.
text.KJV: And he looked round about to see her that had done this thing.
text.NABRE: And he looked around to see who had done it.
text.DRB: And he looked about to see her that had done this.

text.NRSV: But the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came in fear and trembling, fell down before him, and told him the whole truth.
text.Rotherham: And, the woman, overcome with fear, and trembling, knowing what had happened to her, came, and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
text.KJV: But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
text.NABRE: The woman, realizing what had happened to her, approached in fear and trembling. She fell down before Jesus and told him the whole truth.
text.DRB: But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

text.NRSV: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace, and be healed of your disease.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said to her—Daughter! thy faith hath made thee well; withdraw into peace, and be whole from thy plague.
text.KJV: And he said unto her, Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace, and be whole of thy plague.
text.NABRE: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has saved you. Go in peace and be cured of your affliction.”
text.DRB: And he said to her: Daughter, thy faith hath made thee safe, go in peace, and be whole of thy malady.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, some people came from the leader’s house to say, “Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the teacher any further?”
text.Rotherham: While yet he is speaking, they come from the synagogue-ruler’s, saying—Thy daughter, is dead! Why, further, annoy the teacher?
text.KJV: While he yet spake, there came from the ruler of the synagogue’s house certain which said, Thy daughter is dead: why troublest thou the Master any further?
text.NABRE: While he was still speaking, people from the synagogue official’s house arrived and said, “Your daughter has died; why trouble the teacher any longer?”
text.DRB: As he was yet speaking, they come to the Archsynagogue, saying: That thy daughter is dead: why doest thou trouble the Master any further?

text.NRSV: But overhearing what they said, Jesus said to the leader of the synagogue, “Do not be afraid, only believe.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, overhearing the word being spoken, saith unto the synagogue-ruler—Do not fear, only have faith!
text.KJV: As soon as Jesus heard the word that was spoken, he saith unto the ruler of the synagogue, Be not afraid, only believe.
text.NABRE: Disregarding the message that was reported, Jesus said to the synagogue official, “Do not be afraid; just have faith.”
text.DRB: But Jesus having heard the word that was spoken, saith to the Archsynagogue: Fear not, only believe.

text.NRSV: He allowed no one to follow him except Peter, James, and John, the brother of James.
text.Rotherham: And he suffered no one to follow with him, save Peter and James and John the brother of James.
text.KJV: And he suffered no man to follow him, save Peter, and James, and John the brother of James.
text.NABRE: He did not allow anyone to accompany him inside except Peter, James, and John, the brother of James.
text.DRB: And he admitted not any man to follow him, but Peter & James and John the brother of James.

text.NRSV: When they came to the house of the leader of the synagogue, he saw a commotion, people weeping and wailing loudly.
text.Rotherham: And they come into the house of the synagogue-ruler, and he observeth a tumult, and persons weeping and wailing greatly;
text.KJV: And he cometh to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and seeth the tumult, and them that wept and wailed greatly.
text.NABRE: When they arrived at the house of the synagogue official, he caught sight of a commotion, people weeping and wailing loudly.
text.DRB: And they come to the Archsynagogue's house, and he seeth a tumult, and folk weeping and wailing much.

text.NRSV: When he had entered, he said to them, “Why do you make a commotion and weep? The child is not dead but sleeping.”
text.Rotherham: and, entering, he saith unto them—Why are ye making a tumult, and weeping? The child, is, not dead, but, is sleeping;
text.KJV: And when he was come in, he saith unto them, Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.
text.NABRE: So he went in and said to them, “Why this commotion and weeping? The child is not dead but asleep.”
text.DRB: And going in, he saith to them: Why make you this ado and weep? the wench is not dead, but sleepeth.

text.NRSV: And they laughed at him. Then he put them all outside, and took the child’s father and mother and those who were with him, and went in where the child was.
text.Rotherham: and they were deriding him. But, he, thrusting all forth, taketh with him the father of the child, and the mother, and them who were with him,—and entereth where the child was;
text.KJV: And they laughed him to scorn. But when he had put them all out, he taketh the father and the mother of the damsel, and them that were with him, and entereth in where the damsel was lying.
text.NABRE: And they ridiculed him. Then he put them all out. He took along the child’s father and mother and those who were with him and entered the room where the child was.
text.DRB: And they derided him. But he having put forth all, taketh the father and the mother of the wench, and them that were with him, and they go in where the wench was lying.

text.NRSV: He took her by the hand and said to her, “Talitha cum,” which means, “Little girl, get up!”
text.Rotherham: and, grasping the hand of the child, he saith unto her—Talitha, koum! which is, when translated—O damsel! to thee, I say, Arise!
text.KJV: And he took the damsel by the hand, and said unto her, Talitha cumi; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, arise.
text.NABRE: He took the child by the hand and said to her, “ Talitha koum,” which means, “Little girl, I say to you, arise!”
text.DRB: And holding the wench's hand, he saith to her: Talitha cumi, which is being interpreted, wench (I say to thee) arise.

text.NRSV: And immediately the girl got up and began to walk about (she was twelve years of age). At this they were overcome with amazement.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, the damsel arose, and was walking about; for she was twelve years of age. And they were beside themselves, straightway, with a great transport;
text.KJV: And straightway the damsel arose, and walked; for she was of the age of twelve years. And they were astonished with a great astonishment.
text.NABRE: The girl, a child of twelve, arose immediately and walked around. [At that] they were utterly astounded.
text.DRB: And forthwith the wench rose up, and walked, and she was twelve years old: and they were astonished with great astonishment.

text.NRSV: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and told them to give her something to eat.
text.Rotherham: and he commanded them, again and again, that, no one, should get to know this thing,—and bade that food should be given her to eat.
text.KJV: And he charged them straitly that no man should know it; and commanded that something should be given her to eat.
text.NABRE: He gave strict orders that no one should know this and said that she should be given something to eat.
text.DRB: And he commanded them earnestly that nobody should know it: and he bade that something should be given her to eat.

text.NRSV: He left that place and came to his hometown, and his disciples followed him.
text.Rotherham: And he went forth from thence, and cometh into his own city,—and his disciples follow him.
text.KJV: And he went out from thence, and came into his own country; and his disciples follow him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 The Rejection at Nazareth. He departed from there and came to his native place, accompanied by his disciples.
text.DRB: And going out from thence, he went into his country; and his Disciples followed him.

text.NRSV: On the sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were astounded. They said, “Where did this man get all this? What is this wisdom that has been given to him? What deeds of power are being done by his hands!
text.Rotherham: And, when it was Sabbath, he began to be teaching in the synagogue, and, the greater part, as they heard, were being struck with astonishment, saying—Whence hath this man these things?—and—What the wisdom which hath been given to this man?—and—Such mighty works as these, through his hands, are coming to pass!
text.KJV: And when the sabbath day was come, he began to teach in the synagogue: and many hearing him were astonished, saying, From whence hath this man these things? and what wisdom is this which is given unto him, that even such mighty works are wrought by his hands?
text.NABRE: When the sabbath came he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were astonished. They said, “Where did this man get all this? What kind of wisdom has been given him? What mighty deeds are wrought by his hands!
text.DRB: And when the Sabboth was come, he began to teach in the Synagogue: and many hearing him were in admiration at his doctrine, saying: How came this fellow by all these things? & what wisdom is this that is given to him, and such virtues as are wrought by his hands?

text.NRSV: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary and brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon, and are not his sisters here with us?” And they took offense at him.
text.Rotherham: Is not, this, the carpenter? the son of Mary, the brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? And are not his sisters here with us?—and they were finding cause of stumbling in him.
text.KJV: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, the brother of James, and Joses, and of Juda, and Simon? and are not his sisters here with us? And they were offended at him.
text.NABRE: Is he not the carpenter, the son of Mary, and the brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? And are not his sisters here with us?” And they took offense at him.
text.DRB: Is not this the Carpenter, the son of Mary, the brother of James, and Joseph, and Jude, and Simon? Why, are not also his sisters here with us? And they were scandalized in him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Prophets are not without honor, except in their hometown, and among their own kin, and in their own house.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus was saying unto them—A prophet is not without honour, save in his own city and among his kinsfolk, and in his house;
text.KJV: But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor except in his native place and among his own kin and in his own house.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: That there is not a Prophet without honour, but in his own country, and in his own house, and in his own kindred.

text.NRSV: And he could do no deed of power there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people and cured them.
text.Rotherham: and he could not, there, do so much as a single mighty work,—save, on a few sick, he laid his hands and cured them.
text.KJV: And he could there do no mighty work, save that he laid his hands upon a few sick folk, and healed them.
text.NABRE: So he was not able to perform any mighty deed there, apart from curing a few sick people by laying his hands on them.
text.DRB: And he could not do any miracle there, but only cured a few that were sick, imposing his hands.

text.NRSV: And he was amazed at their unbelief. Then he went about among the villages teaching.
text.Rotherham: And he marvelled because of their unbelief. And he was going round the villages in a circuit, teaching.
text.KJV: And he marvelled because of their unbelief. And he went round about the villages, teaching.
text.NABRE: He was amazed at their lack of faith. The Mission of the Twelve. He went around to the villages in the vicinity teaching.
text.DRB: And he marveled because of their incredulity, and he went about the towns in circuit teaching.

text.NRSV: He called the twelve and began to send them out two by two, and gave them authority over the unclean spirits.
text.Rotherham: And he calleth near the twelve, and began to be sending them forth two and two,—and was giving them authority over the impure spirits:
text.KJV: And he called unto him the twelve, and began to send them forth by two and two; and gave them power over unclean spirits;
text.NABRE: He summoned the Twelve and began to send them out two by two and gave them authority over unclean spirits.
text.DRB: And he called the Twelve; and began to send them two and two, and gave them power over unclean spirits.

text.NRSV: He ordered them to take nothing for their journey except a staff; no bread, no bag, no money in their belts;
text.Rotherham: and charged them that they should take, nothing, for a journey, save a staff only,—no bread, no satchel, no copper, for the belt;
text.KJV: And commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, save a staff only; no scrip, no bread, no money in their purse:
text.NABRE: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey but a walking stick—no food, no sack, no money in their belts.
text.DRB: And he commanded them that they should take nothing for the way, but a rod only: not scrip, not bread, nor money in their purse,

text.NRSV: but to wear sandals and not to put on two tunics.
text.Rotherham: but having bound on light sandals, and not to put on, two tunics;
text.KJV: But be shod with sandals; and not put on two coats.
text.NABRE: They were, however, to wear sandals but not a second tunic.
text.DRB: but shod with sandals, and that they should not put on two coats.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Wherever you enter a house, stay there until you leave the place.
text.Rotherham: and he was saying unto them—Wheresoever ye shall enter into a house, there, abide, until ye go forth from thence;
text.KJV: And he said unto them, In what place soever ye enter into an house, there abide till ye depart from that place.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Wherever you enter a house, stay there until you leave from there.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Withersoever you shall enter into an house, there tarry till you depart thence.

text.NRSV: If any place will not welcome you and they refuse to hear you, as you leave, shake off the dust that is on your feet as a testimony against them.”
text.Rotherham: And, whatsoever place shall not welcome you nor hearken unto you, when ye are going forth from thence, shake off the dust that is under you feet, for a witness against them.
text.KJV: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you, when ye depart thence, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them. Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
text.NABRE: Whatever place does not welcome you or listen to you, leave there and shake the dust off your feet in testimony against them.”
text.DRB: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you; going forth from thence shake off the dust from your feet for a testimony to them.

text.NRSV: So they went out and proclaimed that all should repent.
text.Rotherham: And they went forth and made proclamation, in order that men should repent;
text.KJV: And they went out, and preached that men should repent.
text.NABRE: So they went off and preached repentance.
text.DRB: And going forth they preached that they should do penance:

text.NRSV: They cast out many demons, and anointed with oil many who were sick and cured them.
text.Rotherham: and, many demons, were they casting out,—and were anointing with oil, many sick, and were curing them.
text.KJV: And they cast out many devils, and anointed with oil many that were sick, and healed them.
text.NABRE: They drove out many demons, and they anointed with oil many who were sick and cured them. Herod’s Opinion of Jesus.
text.DRB: And they cast out many devils, and anointed with oil many sick, and healed them.

text.NRSV: King Herod heard of it, for Jesus’ name had become known. Some were saying, “John the baptizer has been raised from the dead; and for this reason these powers are at work in him.”
text.Rotherham: And King Herod heard,—for, famous, had become his name; and he was saying—John the Immerser hath arisen from among the dead, and, for this cause, are the powers working mightily in him;
text.KJV: And king Herod heard of him; (for his name was spread abroad:) and he said, That John the Baptist was risen from the dead, and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
text.NABRE: King Herod heard about it, for his fame had become widespread, and people were saying, “John the Baptist has been raised from the dead; that is why mighty powers are at work in him.”
text.DRB: And king Herod heard (for his name was made manifest) and he said: That John the Baptist is risen again from the dead, and therefore virtues work in him.

text.NRSV: But others said, “It is Elijah.” And others said, “It is a prophet, like one of the prophets of old.”
text.Rotherham: But, others, were saying—It is Elijah, and, others were saying—A prophet, like one of the prophets!
text.KJV: Others said, That it is Elias. And others said, That it is a prophet, or as one of the prophets.
text.NABRE: Others were saying, “He is Elijah”; still others, “He is a prophet like any of the prophets.”
text.DRB: And others said: That it is Elias. But others said: That it is a Prophet, as one of the Prophets.

text.NRSV: But when Herod heard of it, he said, “John, whom I beheaded, has been raised.”
text.Rotherham: But, when Herod heard, he was saying—He whom I beheaded—John, the same, hath been raised.
text.KJV: But when Herod heard thereof, he said, It is John, whom I beheaded: he is risen from the dead.
text.NABRE: But when Herod learned of it, he said, “It is John whom I beheaded. He has been raised up.” The Death of John the Baptist.
text.DRB: Which Herod hearing, said: John whom I beheaded, he is risen again from the dead.

text.NRSV: For Herod himself had sent men who arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, because Herod had married her.
text.Rotherham: For, Herod himself, had sent and secured John and bound him in prison, for the sake of Herodias the wife of Philip his brother,—for, her, had he married,
text.KJV: For Herod himself had sent forth and laid hold upon John, and bound him in prison for Herodias’ sake, his brother Philip’s wife: for he had married her.
text.NABRE: Herod was the one who had John arrested and bound in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip, whom he had married.
text.DRB: For the said Herod sent and apprehended John, and bound him in prison for Herodias the wife of Philippe his brother, because he had married her.

text.NRSV: For John had been telling Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”
text.Rotherham: For John had been saying to Herod—It is not allowed thee, to have, the wife of thy brother.
text.KJV: For John had said unto Herod, It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother’s wife.
text.NABRE: John had said to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”
text.DRB: For John said to Herod: It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother's wife,

text.NRSV: And Herodias had a grudge against him, and wanted to kill him. But she could not,
text.Rotherham: And, Herodias, was cherishing [a grudge] against him, and wishing, to slay him,—and could not;
text.KJV: Therefore Herodias had a quarrel against him, and would have killed him; but she could not:
text.NABRE: Herodias harbored a grudge against him and wanted to kill him but was unable to do so.
text.DRB: And Herodias lay in wait for him: and was desirous to kill him, and could not.

text.NRSV: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he protected him. When he heard him, he was greatly perplexed; and yet he liked to listen to him.
text.Rotherham: for, Herod, stood in fear of John, knowing him to be a man righteous and holy,—and was keeping him safe; and, when he heard him, he paid earnest heed, and, with pleasure, used to listen to him.
text.KJV: For Herod feared John, knowing that he was a just man and an holy, and observed him; and when he heard him, he did many things, and heard him gladly.
text.NABRE: Herod feared John, knowing him to be a righteous and holy man, and kept him in custody. When he heard him speak he was very much perplexed, yet he liked to listen to him.
text.DRB: For Herod feared John, knowing him to be a just and holy man: and he kept him, and by hearing him did many things: and he heard him gladly.

text.NRSV: But an opportunity came when Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his courtiers and officers and for the leaders of Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And, an opportune day arriving, when Herod on his birthday made a feast for his nobles, and for the rulers of thousands and for the first men of Galilee,
text.KJV: And when a convenient day was come, that Herod on his birthday made a supper to his lords, high captains, and chief estates of Galilee;
text.NABRE: She had an opportunity one day when Herod, on his birthday, gave a banquet for his courtiers, his military officers, and the leading men of Galilee.
text.DRB: And when a convenient day was fallen, Herod made the supper of his birth-day to the Princes, and the Tribunes, and the chief of Galilee.

text.NRSV: When his daughter Herodias came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests; and the king said to the girl, “Ask me for whatever you wish, and I will give it.”
text.Rotherham: when the daughter of this very Herodias came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those reclining together, and, the king, said unto the damsel—Ask me what thou wilt, and I will give it thee;
text.KJV: And when the daughter of the said Herodias came in, and danced, and pleased Herod and them that sat with him, the king said unto the damsel, Ask of me whatsoever thou wilt, and I will give it thee.
text.NABRE: Herodias’s own daughter came in and performed a dance that delighted Herod and his guests. The king said to the girl, “Ask of me whatever you wish and I will grant it to you.”
text.DRB: And when the daughter of the same Herodias came in, and had danced, and pleased Herod, and them that sat with him at the table, the king said to the damsel: Ask of me what thou wilt, and I will give it thee.

text.NRSV: And he solemnly swore to her, “Whatever you ask me, I will give you, even half of my kingdom.”
text.Rotherham: and he took an oath to her—Whatsoever thou shalt ask me, I will give thee, unto half my kingdom.
text.KJV: And he sware unto her, Whatsoever thou shalt ask of me, I will give it thee, unto the half of my kingdom.
text.NABRE: He even swore [many things] to her, “I will grant you whatever you ask of me, even to half of my kingdom.”
text.DRB: And he sware to her: That whatsoever thou shalt ask I will give thee, though the half of my kingdom.

text.NRSV: She went out and said to her mother, “What should I ask for?” She replied, “The head of John the baptizer.”
text.Rotherham: And, going out, she said unto her mother—What shall I ask? and she said—The head of John the Immerser;
text.KJV: And she went forth, and said unto her mother, What shall I ask? And she said, The head of John the Baptist.
text.NABRE: She went out and said to her mother, “What shall I ask for?” She replied, “The head of John the Baptist.”
text.DRB: Who when she was gone forth, said to her mother, what shall I ask? But she said: The head of John the Baptist.

text.NRSV: Immediately she rushed back to the king and requested, “I want you to give me at once the head of John the Baptist on a platter.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming in straightway, with hast, unto the king, she asked, saying—I desire, that, forthwith, thou give me, upon a charger, the head of John the Immerser.
text.KJV: And she came in straightway with haste unto the king, and asked, saying, I will that thou give me by and by in a charger the head of John the Baptist.
text.NABRE: The girl hurried back to the king’s presence and made her request, “I want you to give me at once on a platter the head of John the Baptist.”
text.DRB: And when she was gone in by and by with haste to the king, she asketh saying: I will that forthwith thou give me in a platter the head of John the Baptist.

text.NRSV: The king was deeply grieved; yet out of regard for his oaths and for the guests, he did not want to refuse her.
text.Rotherham: And, though the king was, very grieved, yet, by reason of the oaths, and of them who were reclining, he would not refuse her.
text.KJV: And the king was exceeding sorry; yet for his oath’s sake, and for their sakes which sat with him, he would not reject her.
text.NABRE: The king was deeply distressed, but because of his oaths and the guests he did not wish to break his word to her.
text.DRB: And the king was stroken sad. Because of his oath and for them that sat together at table he would not displease her:

text.NRSV: Immediately the king sent a soldier of the guard with orders to bring John’s head. He went and beheaded him in the prison,
text.Rotherham: And the king, straightway, sending off a guard, gave orders to bring his head.
text.KJV: And immediately the king sent an executioner, and commanded his head to be brought: and he went and beheaded him in the prison,
text.NABRE: So he promptly dispatched an executioner with orders to bring back his head. He went off and beheaded him in the prison.
text.DRB: but sending the hangman, commanded that his head should be brought in a platter.

text.NRSV: brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl. Then the girl gave it to her mother.
text.Rotherham: And, departing, he beheaded him in the prison, and brought his head upon a charger, and gave it unto the damsel—and, the damsel, gave it unto her mother.
text.KJV: And brought his head in a charger, and gave it to the damsel: and the damsel gave it to her mother.
text.NABRE: He brought in the head on a platter and gave it to the girl. The girl in turn gave it to her mother.
text.DRB: And he beheaded him in the prison, and brought his head in a platter: and gave it to the damsel, and the damsel gave it to her mother.

text.NRSV: When his disciples heard about it, they came and took his body, and laid it in a tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, hearing of it, his disciples went and took away his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
text.KJV: And when his disciples heard of it, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
text.NABRE: When his disciples heard about it, they came and took his body and laid it in a tomb.
text.DRB: Which his Disciples hearing came, and took his body; and they put it in a monument.

text.NRSV: The apostles gathered around Jesus, and told him all that they had done and taught.
text.Rotherham: And the apostles gather themselves together unto Jesus, and reported unto him all things, as many as they had done, and as many as they had taught.
text.KJV: And the apostles gathered themselves together unto Jesus, and told him all things, both what they had done, and what they had taught.
text.NABRE: The Return of the Twelve. The apostles gathered together with Jesus and reported all they had done and taught.
text.DRB: And the Apostles gathering together unto Jesus, made relation to him of all things that they had done and taught.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Come away to a deserted place all by yourselves and rest a while.” For many were coming and going, and they had no leisure even to eat.
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Come, ye yourselves, apart, into a desert place, and rest yourselves a little. For they who were coming and they who were going were many, and, not even to eat, were they finding fitting opportunity.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while: for there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Come away by yourselves to a deserted place and rest a while.” People were coming and going in great numbers, and they had no opportunity even to eat.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Come apart into the desert place, and rest a little. For there were that came and went, many: and they had not so much as space to eat.

text.NRSV: And they went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.
text.Rotherham: And they departed, in the boat, into a desert place, apart.
text.KJV: And they departed into a desert place by ship privately.
text.NABRE: So they went off in the boat by themselves to a deserted place.
text.DRB: And going up into the boat, they went into a desert place apart.

text.NRSV: Now many saw them going and recognized them, and they hurried there on foot from all the towns and arrived ahead of them.
text.Rotherham: And many saw them going away, and took note of it, and, afoot, from all the cities, ran they together thither,—and outwent them.
text.KJV: And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.
text.NABRE: People saw them leaving and many came to know about it. They hastened there on foot from all the towns and arrived at the place before them.
text.DRB: And they saw them going away, and many knew: and they ran flocking thither on foot from all cities, and prevented them.

text.NRSV: As he went ashore, he saw a great crowd; and he had compassion for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd; and he began to teach them many things.
text.Rotherham: And, coming forth, he saw, a great multitude, and was moved with compassion towards them, because they were like sheep having no shepherd, and he began to be teaching them many things.
text.KJV: And Jesus, when he came out, saw much people, and was moved with compassion toward them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd: and he began to teach them many things.
text.NABRE: The Feeding of the Five Thousand. When he disembarked and saw the vast crowd, his heart was moved with pity for them, for they were like sheep without a shepherd; and he began to teach them many things.
text.DRB: And going forth, Jesus saw a great multitude; and he had compassion on them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.

text.NRSV: When it grew late, his disciples came to him and said, “This is a deserted place, and the hour is now very late;
text.Rotherham: And, already, a late hour, having arrived, his disciples came unto him and were saying, A desert, is the place, and it is, already, a late hour:
text.KJV: And when the day was now far spent, his disciples came unto him, and said, This is a desert place, and now the time is far passed:
text.NABRE: By now it was already late and his disciples approached him and said, “This is a deserted place and it is already very late.
text.DRB: And when the day was now far spent, his Disciples came to him, saying: This is a desert place, and the hour is now past:

text.NRSV: send them away so that they may go into the surrounding country and villages and buy something for themselves to eat.”
text.Rotherham: dismiss them, that, departing into the surrounding hamlets and villages, they may buy themselves something to eat.
text.KJV: Send them away, that they may go into the country round about, and into the villages, and buy themselves bread: for they have nothing to eat.
text.NABRE: Dismiss them so that they can go to the surrounding farms and villages and buy themselves something to eat.”
text.DRB: dismiss them, that going out into the next villages and towns, they may buy themselves meats to eat.

text.NRSV: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They said to him, “Are we to go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give it to them to eat?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering said to them—Ye, give them, to eat. And they say unto him—Shall we depart and buy two hundred denaries’ worth of loaves, and give them to eat?
text.KJV: He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “Give them some food yourselves.” But they said to him, “Are we to buy two hundred days’ wages worth of food and give it to them to eat?”
text.DRB: And he answering said: Give ye them to eat. And they said to him: Let us go and buy bread for two hundred pence, and we will give them to eat.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “How many loaves have you? Go and see.” When they had found out, they said, “Five, and two fish.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, saith unto them—How many loaves, have ye? Go, see! And, getting to know, they say—Five, and, two fishes.
text.KJV: He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
text.NABRE: He asked them, “How many loaves do you have? Go and see.” And when they had found out they said, “Five loaves and two fish.”
text.DRB: And he saith to them: How many loaves have you? Go and see. And when they knew, they say: Five, and two fishes.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered them to get all the people to sit down in groups on the green grass.
text.Rotherham: And he gave them orders, that all should be made recline, in parties, upon, the green grass.
text.KJV: And he commanded them to make all sit down by companies upon the green grass.
text.NABRE: So he gave orders to have them sit down in groups on the green grass.
text.DRB: And he commanded them that they should make all sit down, by companies upon the green grass.

text.NRSV: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and of fifties.
text.Rotherham: And they fell back, in companies, by hundreds and by fifties.
text.KJV: And they sat down in ranks, by hundreds, and by fifties.
text.NABRE: The people took their places in rows by hundreds and by fifties.
text.DRB: And they sat down in ranks by hundreds and fifties.

text.NRSV: Taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people; and he divided the two fish among them all.
text.Rotherham: And, taking the five loaves, and the two fishes, looking up into heaven, he blessed, and brake up the loaves, and began giving unto the disciples, that they might set before them, and, the two fishes, divided he, unto all.
text.KJV: And when he had taken the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up to heaven, and blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before them; and the two fishes divided he among them all.
text.NABRE: Then, taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, he said the blessing, broke the loaves, and gave them to [his] disciples to set before the people; he also divided the two fish among them all.
text.DRB: And when he had taken the five loaves, and the two fishes: looking up unto Heaven, he blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave to his Disciples to set before them: and the two fishes he divided to all.

text.NRSV: And all ate and were filled;
text.Rotherham: And they did all eat, and were filled.
text.KJV: And they did all eat, and were filled.
text.NABRE: They all ate and were satisfied.
text.DRB: And all did eat, and had their fill.

text.NRSV: and they took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and of the fish.
text.Rotherham: And they took up broken pieces, twelve baskets, full measure, and from the fishes.
text.KJV: And they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments, and of the fishes.
text.NABRE: And they picked up twelve wicker baskets full of fragments and what was left of the fish.
text.DRB: And they took up the leavings, twelve full baskets of fragments, and of the fishes.

text.NRSV: Those who had eaten the loaves numbered five thousand men.
text.Rotherham: And they who did eat the loaves were, five thousand men.
text.KJV: And they that did eat of the loaves were about five thousand men.
text.NABRE: Those who ate [of the loaves] were five thousand men. The Walking on the Water.
text.DRB: And they that did eat, were five thousand men.

text.NRSV: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go on ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, constrained he his disciples to enter into the boat, and be going forward to the other side, unto Bethsaida,—while, he, was dismissing the multitude.
text.KJV: And straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship, and to go to the other side before unto Bethsaida, while he sent away the people.
text.NABRE: Then he made his disciples get into the boat and precede him to the other side toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
text.DRB: And immediately he compelled his Disciples to go up into the boat, that they might go before him beyond the strait to Bethsaida: whiles himself did dismiss the people.

text.NRSV: After saying farewell to them, he went up on the mountain to pray.
text.Rotherham: And, bidding them farewell, he departed into the mountain to pray.
text.KJV: And when he had sent them away, he departed into a mountain to pray.
text.NABRE: And when he had taken leave of them, he went off to the mountain to pray.
text.DRB: And when he had dismissed them, he went into the mountain to pray.

text.NRSV: When evening came, the boat was out on the sea, and he was alone on the land.
text.Rotherham: And, when, evening, came, the boat was in the midst of the sea, and, he alone, on the land.
text.KJV: And when even was come, the ship was in the midst of the sea, and he alone on the land.
text.NABRE: When it was evening, the boat was far out on the sea and he was alone on shore.
text.DRB: And when it was late, the boat was in the midst of the sea, and himself alone on the land.

text.NRSV: When he saw that they were straining at the oars against an adverse wind, he came towards them early in the morning, walking on the sea. He intended to pass them by.
text.Rotherham: And, seeing them distressed in the rowing, for the wind was against them, about the fourth watch of the night, he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea,—and was wishing to pass by them.
text.KJV: And he saw them toiling in rowing; for the wind was contrary unto them: and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea, and would have passed by them.
text.NABRE: Then he saw that they were tossed about while rowing, for the wind was against them. About the fourth watch of the night, he came toward them walking on the sea. He meant to pass by them.
text.DRB: And seeing them labouring in rowing (for the wind was against them) and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh to them walking upon the sea, and he would have passed by them.

text.NRSV: But when they saw him walking on the sea, they thought it was a ghost and cried out;
text.Rotherham: But, they, seeing him, upon the sea, walking, supposed that it was an apparition, and cried out aloud;
text.KJV: But when they saw him walking upon the sea, they supposed it had been a spirit, and cried out:
text.NABRE: But when they saw him walking on the sea, they thought it was a ghost and cried out.
text.DRB: But they seeing him walking upon the sea, thought it was a ghost, and cried out.

text.NRSV: for they all saw him and were terrified. But immediately he spoke to them and said, “Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.”
text.Rotherham: for, all, saw him, and were troubled. But, he, straightway, talked with them, and saith unto them—Take courage! it is, I—be not afraid!
text.KJV: For they all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.
text.NABRE: They had all seen him and were terrified. But at once he spoke with them, “Take courage, it is I, do not be afraid!”
text.DRB: For all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and said to them: Have confidence, it is I, fear ye not.

text.NRSV: Then he got into the boat with them and the wind ceased. And they were utterly astounded,
text.Rotherham: And he went up unto them, into the boat,—and the wind lulled. And, exceedingly, within themselves, were they astonished;
text.KJV: And he went up unto them into the ship; and the wind ceased: and they were sore amazed in themselves beyond measure, and wondered.
text.NABRE: He got into the boat with them and the wind died down. They were [completely] astounded.
text.DRB: And he went up to them into the ship, and the wind ceased: and they were far more astonished within themselves:

text.NRSV: for they did not understand about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.
text.Rotherham: for they understood not by the loaves,—but their, heart, had been, hardened.
text.KJV: For they considered not the miracle of the loaves: for their heart was hardened.
text.NABRE: They had not understood the incident of the loaves. On the contrary, their hearts were hardened.
text.DRB: for they understood not concerning the loaves; for their heart was blinded.

text.NRSV: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret and moored the boat.
text.Rotherham: And crossing over unto the land, they came unto Gennesaret, and cast anchor near.
text.KJV: And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Gennesaret, and drew to the shore.
text.NABRE: The Healings at Gennesaret. After making the crossing, they came to land at Gennesaret and tied up there.
text.DRB: And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Genesareth, and set to the shore.

text.NRSV: When they got out of the boat, people at once recognized him,
text.Rotherham: And, when they had, come forth, out of the boat, straightway, recognizing him,
text.KJV: And when they were come out of the ship, straightway they knew him,
text.NABRE: As they were leaving the boat, people immediately recognized him.
text.DRB: And when they were gone out of the boat, incontinent they knew him:

text.NRSV: and hurried about that whole region and began to bring the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.
text.Rotherham: the people ran round the whole of that country, and began to be carrying round, upon couches, them who were sick, wherever they heard that he was.
text.KJV: And ran through that whole region round about, and began to carry about in beds those that were sick, where they heard he was.
text.NABRE: They scurried about the surrounding country and began to bring in the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.
text.DRB: and running through that whole country they began to carry about in couches those that were ill at ease, where they heard he was.

text.NRSV: And wherever he went, into villages or cities or farms, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might touch even the fringe of his cloak; and all who touched it were healed.
text.Rotherham: And, wheresoever he was entering into villages, or into cities, or into hamlets, in the marketplaces, laid they the sick, and were beseeching him, that, only the fringe of his mantle, they might touch, and, as many soever as touched it, were being made well.
text.KJV: And whithersoever he entered, into villages, or cities, or country, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment: and as many as touched him were made whole.
text.NABRE: Whatever villages or towns or countryside he entered, they laid the sick in the marketplaces and begged him that they might touch only the tassel on his cloak; and as many as touched it were healed.
text.DRB: And whithersoever he entered into towns or into villages or cities, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch but the hem of his garment: and as many as touched him, were made whole.

text.KJV: Then came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 The Tradition of the Elders. Now when the Pharisees with some scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him,
text.NRSV: Now when the Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him,
text.DRB: And there assemble together unto him the Pharisees and certain of the Scribes, coming from Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And the Pharisees and certain of the Scribes who have come from Jerusalem gather themselves together unto him;

text.KJV: And when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is to say, with unwashen, hands, they found fault.
text.NABRE: they observed that some of his disciples ate their meals with unclean, that is, unwashed, hands.
text.NRSV: they noticed that some of his disciples were eating with defiled hands, that is, without washing them.
text.DRB: And when they had seen certain of his Disciples eat bread with common hands, that is, not washed, they blamed them.
text.Rotherham: and, observing certain of his disciples, that, with defiled hands, that is unwashed, they are eating bread,——

text.KJV: For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, except they wash their hands oft, eat not, holding the tradition of the elders.
text.NABRE: (For the Pharisees and, in fact, all Jews, do not eat without carefully washing their hands, keeping the tradition of the elders.
text.NRSV: (For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, do not eat unless they give their hands a ceremonial washing, keeping the tradition of the elders;
text.DRB: For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, unless they often wash their hands, eat not, holding the tradition of the Ancients:
text.Rotherham: For, the Pharisees, and all the Jews, unless with care they wash their hands, eat not, holding fast the tradition of the elders;

text.KJV: And when they come from the market, except they wash, they eat not. And many other things there be, which they have received to hold, as the washing of cups, and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.
text.NABRE: And on coming from the marketplace they do not eat without purifying themselves. And there are many other things that they have traditionally observed, the purification of cups and jugs and kettles [and beds].)
text.NRSV: and they do not eat anything from the market unless they wash it; and there are also many other traditions that they observe, the washing of cups, pots, and bronze kettles.)
text.DRB: And from the market, unless they be washed, they eat not: and many other things there be that were delivered unto them to observe, the washings of cups and cruses, and of brazen vessels & beds.
text.Rotherham: and coming from market, unless they sprinkle themselves, they eat not,—and, many other things, there are, which they have accepted to hold fast—immersions of cups and measures and copper vessels——

text.KJV: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked him, Why walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashen hands?
text.NABRE: So the Pharisees and scribes questioned him, “Why do your disciples not follow the tradition of the elders but instead eat a meal with unclean hands?”
text.NRSV: So the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why do your disciples not live according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?”
text.DRB: And the Pharisees and Scribes asked him: Why do not thy Disciples walk according to the tradition of the Ancients, but they eat bread with common hands?
text.Rotherham: and so the Pharisees and the Scribes, question, him—For what cause do thy disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but, with defiled hands, eat bread?

text.KJV: He answered and said unto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
text.NABRE: He responded, “Well did Isaiah prophesy about you hypocrites, as it is written: ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;
text.NRSV: He said to them, “Isaiah prophesied rightly about you hypocrites, as it is written, ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;
text.DRB: But he answering, said to them: Well did Esay Prophesy of you Hypocrites, as it is written: This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
