text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—Well, prophesied Isaiah concerning you, ye hypocrites, as it is written—This people, with the lips do honour me, while, their heart, far off, holdeth from me,—

text.KJV: Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
text.NABRE: In vain do they worship me, teaching as doctrines human precepts.’
text.NRSV: in vain do they worship me, teaching human precepts as doctrines.’
text.DRB: And in vain do they worship me, teaching doctrines precepts of men.
text.Rotherham: But, in vain, do they pay devotions unto me, teaching for teachings, the commandments of men;

text.KJV: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.
text.NABRE: You disregard God’s commandment but cling to human tradition.”
text.NRSV: You abandon the commandment of God and hold to human tradition.”
text.DRB: For leaving the commandment of God, you hold the traditions of men, the washings of cruses and cups: & many other things you do like to these.
text.Rotherham: Having, dismissed, the commandment, of God, ye, hold fast, the tradition, of men.

text.KJV: And he said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.
text.NABRE: He went on to say, “How well you have set aside the commandment of God in order to uphold your tradition!
text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to keep your tradition!
text.DRB: And he said to them, well do you frustrate the precept of God, that you may observe your own tradition.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying to them—Well, do ye set aside the commandment of God, that, your own tradition, ye may observe;

text.KJV: For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death:
text.NABRE: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and ‘Whoever curses father or mother shall die.’
text.NRSV: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and, ‘Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.’
text.DRB: For Moyses said: Honour thy father and thy mother; and, He that shall curse father or mother, dying let him die.
text.Rotherham: For, Moses, said—Honour thy father and thy mother, and—He that revileth father or mother, let him, surely die,—

text.KJV: But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free.
text.NABRE: Yet you say, ‘If a person says to father or mother, “Any support you might have had from me is qorban ”’ (meaning, dedicated to God),
text.NRSV: But you say that if anyone tells father or mother, ‘Whatever support you might have had from me is Corban’ (that is, an offering to God)—
text.DRB: But you say: If a man say to father or mother, Corban (which is a gift) whatsoever proceedeth from me, shall profit thee:
text.Rotherham: But, ye, say—If a man shall say to his father or his mother, Korban! that is, A gift, whatsoever, out of me, thou mightest be profited,

text.KJV: And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;
text.NABRE: you allow him to do nothing more for his father or mother.
text.NRSV: then you no longer permit doing anything for a father or mother,
text.DRB: And further you suffer him not to do ought for his father or mother,
text.Rotherham: no longer, do ye suffer him to do, aught, for his father or his mother,—

text.KJV: Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.
text.NABRE: You nullify the word of God in favor of your tradition that you have handed on. And you do many such things.”
text.NRSV: thus making void the word of God through your tradition that you have handed on. And you do many things like this.”
text.DRB: defeating the word of God for your own tradition which you have given forth. And many other things of this sort you do.
text.Rotherham: cancelling the word of God by your tradition which ye have delivered. And, many such similar things, are ye doing!

text.KJV: And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:
text.NABRE: He summoned the crowd again and said to them, “Hear me, all of you, and understand.
text.NRSV: Then he called the crowd again and said to them, “Listen to me, all of you, and understand:
text.DRB: And calling again the multitude unto him, he said to them: Hear me all you, and understand.
text.Rotherham: And, again calling near the multitude, he was saying unto them—Hearken to me, all! and understand:

text.KJV: There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that defile the man.
text.NABRE: Nothing that enters one from outside can defile that person; but the things that come out from within are what defile.”
text.NRSV: there is nothing outside a person that by going in can defile, but the things that come out are what defile.”
text.DRB: Nothing is without a man entering into him, that can defile him. But the things that proceed from a man those are they that make a man common.
text.Rotherham: Nothing, is there, from without the man, entering into him, which can defile him; but, the things which, out of the man, come forth, are the things that defile the man.

text.KJV: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.
text.DRB: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

text.KJV: And when he was entered into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.
text.NABRE: When he got home away from the crowd his disciples questioned him about the parable.
text.NRSV: When he had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about the parable.
text.DRB: And when he was entered into the house from the multitude, his Disciples asked him the parable.
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered into a house from the multitudes, his disciples began to question him as to the parable;

text.KJV: And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Are even you likewise without understanding? Do you not realize that everything that goes into a person from outside cannot defile,
text.NRSV: He said to them, “Then do you also fail to understand? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile,
text.DRB: And he saith to them: So are you also unskillful? Understand you not that every thing from without, entering into a man, cannot make him common:
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Thus, are, ye also, without discernment? Perceive ye not that, nothing, from without, entering into the man, can defile him;

text.KJV: Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
text.NABRE: since it enters not the heart but the stomach and passes out into the latrine?” (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
text.NRSV: since it enters, not the heart but the stomach, and goes out into the sewer?” (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
text.DRB: because it entereth not into his heart, but goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the privy, purging all the meats?
text.Rotherham: because it entereth not into his heart, but into his stomach, and, into the draught, is passed—purifying all foods?

text.KJV: And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.
text.NABRE: “But what comes out of a person, that is what defiles.
text.NRSV: And he said, “It is what comes out of a person that defiles.
text.DRB: But he said that the things which come forth from a man, they make a man common.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying—That which, out of the man, cometh forth, that, defileth the man;

text.KJV: For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,
text.NABRE: From within people, from their hearts, come evil thoughts, unchastity, theft, murder,
text.NRSV: For it is from within, from the human heart, that evil intentions come: fornication, theft, murder,
text.DRB: For from within out of the heart of men proceed evil cogitations, adulteries, fornications, murders,
text.Rotherham: For, from within, out of the heart of men, the base designs, come forth,—fornications, thefts,

text.KJV: Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:
text.NABRE: adultery, greed, malice, deceit, licentiousness, envy, blasphemy, arrogance, folly.
text.NRSV: adultery, avarice, wickedness, deceit, licentiousness, envy, slander, pride, folly.
text.DRB: thefts, avarices, wickedness, guile, impudicities, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness.
text.Rotherham: murders, adulteries, covetousnesses, knaveries, deceit, wantonness, an evil eye, profane speaking, foolishness,—

text.KJV: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.
text.NABRE: All these evils come from within and they defile.”
text.NRSV: All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person.”
text.DRB: All these evils proceed from within, and make a man common.
text.Rotherham: all these wicked things, from within, come forth, and defile, the man.

text.KJV: And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entered into an house, and would have no man know it: but he could not be hid.
text.NABRE: The Syrophoenician Woman’s Faith. From that place he went off to the district of Tyre. He entered a house and wanted no one to know about it, but he could not escape notice.
text.NRSV: From there he set out and went away to the region of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know he was there. Yet he could not escape notice,
text.DRB: And rising from thence he went into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon: and entering into a house, he would that no man should know, and he could not be hid.
text.Rotherham: And, from thence arising, he departed into the bounds of Tyre [and Zidon]. And, entering into a house, he was wishing, no one, to know it, and yet could not escape notice,—

text.KJV: For a certain woman, whose young daughter had an unclean spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feet:
text.NABRE: Soon a woman whose daughter had an unclean spirit heard about him. She came and fell at his feet.
text.NRSV: but a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately heard about him, and she came and bowed down at his feet.
text.DRB: For a woman immediately as she heard of him, whose daughter had an unclean spirit, entered in, and fell down at his feet.
text.Rotherham: but, straightway, a woman hearing about him, whose daughter had an impure spirit, she came and fell down at his feet.

text.KJV: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.
text.NABRE: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophoenician by birth, and she begged him to drive the demon out of her daughter.
text.NRSV: Now the woman was a Gentile, of Syrophoenician origin. She begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
text.DRB: For the woman was a Gentile, a Syrophænician born. And she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.
text.Rotherham: Now, the woman, was a Grecian, a Syrophoenician by race,—and she was requesting him that, the demon, he would cast forth out of her daughter.

text.KJV: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.
text.NABRE: He said to her, “Let the children be fed first. For it is not right to take the food of the children and throw it to the dogs.”
text.NRSV: He said to her, “Let the children be fed first, for it is not fair to take the children’s food and throw it to the dogs.”
text.DRB: Who said to her: Suffer first the children to be filled. For it is not good to take the children's bread, and cast it to the dogs.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying to her—Suffer, the children, first, to be fed; for it is not seemly to take the bread of the children, and, unto the little dogs, to cast it;

text.KJV: And she answered and said unto him, Yes, Lord: yet the dogs under the table eat of the children’s crumbs.
text.NABRE: She replied and said to him, “Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the children’s scraps.”
text.NRSV: But she answered him, “Sir, even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
text.DRB: But she answered, and said to him: Yea Lord; for the whelps also eat under the table of the crumbs of the children.
text.Rotherham: but she answered and saith to him—Yea, Lord! and yet, the little dogs under the table, do eat of the crumbs of the children;

text.KJV: And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.
text.NABRE: Then he said to her, “For saying this, you may go. The demon has gone out of your daughter.”
text.NRSV: Then he said to her, “For saying that, you may go—the demon has left your daughter.”
text.DRB: And he said to her: For this saying go thy way, the devil is gone out of thy daughter.
text.Rotherham: and he said to her—Because of this word, go thy way, the demon hath gone forth out of thy daughter;

text.KJV: And when she was come to her house, she found the devil gone out, and her daughter laid upon the bed.
text.NABRE: When the woman went home, she found the child lying in bed and the demon gone.
text.NRSV: So she went home, found the child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.
text.DRB: And when she was departed into her house, she found the maid lying upon the bed, and the devil gone out.
text.Rotherham: and, departing unto her house, she found the child laid prostrate on the couch, and the demon gone forth.

text.KJV: And again, departing from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.
text.NABRE: The Healing of a Deaf Man. Again he left the district of Tyre and went by way of Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, into the district of the Decapolis.
text.NRSV: Then he returned from the region of Tyre, and went by way of Sidon towards the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.
text.DRB: And again going out of the coasts of Tyre, he came by Sidon to the sea of Galilee through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.
text.Rotherham: And, again coming forth out of the bounds of Tyre, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the bounds of Decapolis.

text.KJV: And they bring unto him one that was deaf, and had an impediment in his speech; and they beseech him to put his hand upon him.
text.NABRE: And people brought to him a deaf man who had a speech impediment and begged him to lay his hand on him.
text.NRSV: They brought to him a deaf man who had an impediment in his speech; and they begged him to lay his hand on him.
text.DRB: And they bring to him one deaf and dumb; and they besought him that he would impose his hand upon him.
text.Rotherham: And they bring to him one deaf and stammering, and beseech him that he would lay upon him his hand.

text.KJV: And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;
text.NABRE: He took him off by himself away from the crowd. He put his finger into the man’s ears and, spitting, touched his tongue;
text.NRSV: He took him aside in private, away from the crowd, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat and touched his tongue.
text.DRB: And taking him from the multitude apart, he put his fingers into his ears, and spitting, touched his tongue;
text.Rotherham: And, taking him away from the multitude apart, he thrust his fingers into his ears, and, spitting, touched his tongue;

text.KJV: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.
text.NABRE: then he looked up to heaven and groaned, and said to him, “ Ephphatha!” (that is, “Be opened!”)
text.NRSV: Then looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, “Ephphatha,” that is, “Be opened.”
text.DRB: And looking up unto Heaven, he groaned, and said to him: Ephphetha, which is, Be thou opened.
text.Rotherham: and, looking up into the heaven, sighed deeply, and saith unto him—Ephphatha! that is, Be opened!

text.KJV: And straightway his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plain.
text.NABRE: And [immediately] the man’s ears were opened, his speech impediment was removed, and he spoke plainly.
text.NRSV: And immediately his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.
text.DRB: And immediately his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake right.
text.Rotherham: and his ears opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed,—and he was speaking correctly.

text.KJV: And he charged them that they should tell no man: but the more he charged them, so much the more a great deal they published it;
text.NABRE: He ordered them not to tell anyone. But the more he ordered them not to, the more they proclaimed it.
text.NRSV: Then Jesus ordered them to tell no one; but the more he ordered them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.
text.DRB: And he commanded them not to tell anybody. But how much he commanded them, so much the more a great deal did they publish it.
text.Rotherham: And he charged them that they should be telling, no one,—but, as much as to them he gave charge, they, exceeding more abundantly, were making proclamation.

text.KJV: And were beyond measure astonished, saying, He hath done all things well: he maketh both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.
text.NABRE: They were exceedingly astonished and they said, “He has done all things well. He makes the deaf hear and [the] mute speak.”
text.NRSV: They were astounded beyond measure, saying, “He has done everything well; he even makes the deaf to hear and the mute to speak.”
text.DRB: And so much the more did they wonder, saying: He hath done all things well; he hath made both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.
text.Rotherham: And, superabundantly, were they being struck with astonishment, saying—Well, hath he, all things, done,—both, the deaf, he causeth to hear, and, the dumb, to speak.

text.NRSV: In those days when there was again a great crowd without anything to eat, he called his disciples and said to them,
text.Rotherham: In those days, there being, again a large multitude, and they not having any thing to eat, calling near the disciples, he saith unto them—
text.KJV: In those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples unto him, and saith unto them,
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 The Feeding of the Four Thousand. In those days when there again was a great crowd without anything to eat, he summoned the disciples and said,
text.DRB: IN those days again when there was a great multitude, and had not what to eat: calling his Disciples together, he saith to them:

text.NRSV: “I have compassion for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat.
text.Rotherham: I am moved with compassion towards the multitude, because, already three days, abide they with me, and they have nothing to eat;
text.KJV: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
text.NABRE: “My heart is moved with pity for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat.
text.DRB: I have compassion upon the multitude, because lo three days they now endure with me, neither have what to eat.

text.NRSV: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they will faint on the way—and some of them have come from a great distance.”
text.Rotherham: and, if dismiss them fasting unto their home, they will be exhausted in the way,—and, certain of them, are, from afar.
text.KJV: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.
text.NABRE: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they will collapse on the way, and some of them have come a great distance.”
text.DRB: And if I dismiss them fasting into their home, they will faint in the way; for some of them came far off.

text.NRSV: His disciples replied, “How can one feed these people with bread here in the desert?”
text.Rotherham: And his disciples answered him—Whence shall any one be able, here, to fill, these, with bread, in a desert?
text.KJV: And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness?
text.NABRE: His disciples answered him, “Where can anyone get enough bread to satisfy them here in this deserted place?”
text.DRB: And his Disciples answered him: Whence may a man fill them here with bread in the wilderness?

text.NRSV: He asked them, “How many loaves have you?” They said, “Seven.”
text.Rotherham: And he was questioning them—How many loaves have ye? and they said—Seven.
text.KJV: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.
text.NABRE: Still he asked them, “How many loaves do you have?” “Seven,” they replied.
text.DRB: And he asked them: How many loaves have ye? Who said: Seven.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered the crowd to sit down on the ground; and he took the seven loaves, and after giving thanks he broke them and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they distributed them to the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And he sendeth word to the multitude, to fall back upon the ground. And, taking the seven loaves, giving thanks, he brake, and was giving unto his disciples, that they might be setting before them; and they set before the multitude.
text.KJV: And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before them; and they did set them before the people.
text.NABRE: He ordered the crowd to sit down on the ground. Then, taking the seven loaves he gave thanks, broke them, and gave them to his disciples to distribute, and they distributed them to the crowd.
text.DRB: And he commanded the multitude to sit down upon the ground. And taking the seven loaves, giving thanks he brake, and gave to his Disciples for to set before them, and they did set them before the multitude.

text.NRSV: They had also a few small fish; and after blessing them, he ordered that these too should be distributed.
text.Rotherham: And they had a few small fishes; and, blessing them, he bade them set, these also, before them.
text.KJV: And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before them.
text.NABRE: They also had a few fish. He said the blessing over them and ordered them distributed also.
text.DRB: And they had a few little fishes; and he blessed them, and commanded them to be set before them.

text.NRSV: They ate and were filled; and they took up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.
text.Rotherham: And they did eat, and were filled, and took up remainders of broken pieces, seven hampers.
text.KJV: So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets.
text.NABRE: They ate and were satisfied. They picked up the fragments left over—seven baskets.
text.DRB: And they did eat and were filled, and they took up that which was left of the fragments, seven maunds.

text.NRSV: Now there were about four thousand people. And he sent them away.
text.Rotherham: Now they were about four thousand; and he dismissed them.
text.KJV: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.
text.NABRE: There were about four thousand people. He dismissed them
text.DRB: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he dismissed them.

text.NRSV: And immediately he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, entering into the boat with his disciples, he came into the parts of Dalmanutha;
text.KJV: And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.
text.NABRE: and got into the boat with his disciples and came to the region of Dalmanutha.
text.DRB: And immediately going up into the boat with his Disciples, he came into the quarters of Dalmanutha.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees came and began to argue with him, asking him for a sign from heaven, to test him.
text.Rotherham: and forth came the Pharisees, and began discussing with him, seeking of him, a sign from the heaven, testing him.
text.KJV: And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a sign from heaven, tempting him.
text.NABRE: The Demand for a Sign. The Pharisees came forward and began to argue with him, seeking from him a sign from heaven to test him.
text.DRB: And the Pharisees went forth, and began to question with him, asking of him a sign from Heaven, tempting him.

text.NRSV: And he sighed deeply in his spirit and said, “Why does this generation ask for a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”
text.Rotherham: And, deeply sighing in his spirit, he saith—Why, doth this generation seek, a sign? Verily, I say—there shall, not be given, to this generation, a sign.
text.KJV: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.
text.NABRE: He sighed from the depth of his spirit and said, “Why does this generation seek a sign? Amen, I say to you, no sign will be given to this generation.”
text.DRB: And groaning in spirit, he saith: Why doth this Generation ask a sign? Amen I say to you, If a sign shall be given to this generation.

text.NRSV: And he left them, and getting into the boat again, he went across to the other side.
text.Rotherham: And leaving them, again, embarking, he departed unto the other side.
text.KJV: And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.
text.NABRE: Then he left them, got into the boat again, and went off to the other shore.
text.DRB: And leaving them, he went up again into the boat, and passed beyond the strait.

text.NRSV: Now the disciples had forgotten to bring any bread; and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text.Rotherham: And they forgot to take loaves, and, save one loaf, they had nothing with them in the boat.
text.KJV: Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.
text.NABRE: The Leaven of the Pharisees. They had forgotten to bring bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text.DRB: And they forgot to take bread: and they had but one loaf with them in the boat.

text.NRSV: And he cautioned them, saying, “Watch out—beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod.”
text.Rotherham: And he began charging them, saying—Mind! beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod!
text.KJV: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.
text.NABRE: He enjoined them, “Watch out, guard against the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod.”
text.DRB: And he commanded them, saying: Look well and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and the leaven of Herod.

text.NRSV: They said to one another, “It is because we have no bread.”
text.Rotherham: And they began deliberating one with another, because they had no loaves.
text.KJV: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have no bread.
text.NABRE: They concluded among themselves that it was because they had no bread.
text.DRB: And they reasoned among themselves saying: Because we have not bread.

text.NRSV: And becoming aware of it, Jesus said to them, “Why are you talking about having no bread? Do you still not perceive or understand? Are your hearts hardened?
text.Rotherham: And, taking note, he saith unto them—Why do ye deliberate, because ye have no, loaves? Not yet perceive ye, neither understand,—keep ye your hearts, hardened?
text.KJV: And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?
text.NABRE: When he became aware of this he said to them, “Why do you conclude that it is because you have no bread? Do you not yet understand or comprehend? Are your hearts hardened?
text.DRB: Which Jesus knowing, saith to them: Why do you reason, because you have not bread? do you not yet know nor understand? Yet have ye your heart blinded?

text.NRSV: Do you have eyes, and fail to see? Do you have ears, and fail to hear? And do you not remember?
text.Rotherham: Eyes having, see ye not? and ears having, hear ye not? and remember ye not
text.KJV: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
text.NABRE: Do you have eyes and not see, ears and not hear? And do you not remember,
text.DRB: having eyes see you not? and having ears hear you not? Neither do you remember?

text.NRSV: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?” They said to him, “Twelve.”
text.Rotherham: when, the five loaves, I brake unto the five thousand, how many baskets, full of broken pieces, ye took up? They say unto him—Twelve,—
text.KJV: When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.
text.NABRE: when I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many wicker baskets full of fragments you picked up?” They answered him, “Twelve.”
text.DRB: When I brake five loaves among five thousand; and how many baskets full of fragments took you up? They say to him, Twelve.

text.NRSV: “And the seven for the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?” And they said to him, “Seven.”
text.Rotherham: When the seven, unto the four thousand, how many hampers, full measure, of broken pieces, ye took up? And they say unto him—Seven. And he was saying unto them—
text.KJV: And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.
text.NABRE: “When I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many full baskets of fragments did you pick up?” They answered [him], “Seven.”
text.DRB: When also seven loaves among four thousand, how many maunds of fragments took you up? And they say to him, Seven.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Do you not yet understand?”
text.Rotherham: Not yet, do ye understand?
text.KJV: And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Do you still not understand?” The Blind Man of Bethsaida.
text.DRB: And he said to them: How do you not yet understand?

text.NRSV: They came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to him and begged him to touch him.
text.Rotherham: And they come into Bethsaida. And they bring unto him one blind, and beseech him that him, he would touch.
text.KJV: And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.
text.NABRE: When they arrived at Bethsaida, they brought to him a blind man and begged him to touch him.
text.DRB: And they come to Bethsaida; and they bring to him one blind, and desired him that he would touch him.

text.NRSV: He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village; and when he had put saliva on his eyes and laid his hands on him, he asked him, “Can you see anything?”
text.Rotherham: And, laying hold of the hand of the blind man, he brought him forth outside the village, and, spitting into his eyes, laying his hands upon him, he was asking him—Anything, seest thou?
text.KJV: And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.
text.NABRE: He took the blind man by the hand and led him outside the village. Putting spittle on his eyes he laid his hands on him and asked, “Do you see anything?”
text.DRB: And taking the hand of the blind, he led him forth out of the town; and spitting into his eyes, imposing his hands, he asked him if he saw any thing.

text.NRSV: And the man looked up and said, “I can see people, but they look like trees, walking.”
text.Rotherham: and, looking up, he was saying—I see men, because, like trees, I behold them walking
text.KJV: And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.
text.NABRE: Looking up he replied, “I see people looking like trees and walking.”
text.DRB: And looking up, he said: I see men as it were trees, walking.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus laid his hands on his eyes again; and he looked intently and his sight was restored, and he saw everything clearly.
text.Rotherham: Then again, put he his hands upon his eyes, and he saw clearly, and was restored, and was seeing distinctly, in broad splendour, all things together.
text.KJV: After that he put his hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw every man clearly.
text.NABRE: Then he laid hands on his eyes a second time and he saw clearly; his sight was restored and he could see everything distinctly.
text.DRB: After that again he imposed his hands upon his eyes, and he began to see, and was restored, so that he saw all things clearly.

text.NRSV: Then he sent him away to his home, saying, “Do not even go into the village.”
text.Rotherham: And he sent him away unto his house, saying—Not even into the village, mayest thou enter.
text.KJV: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.
text.NABRE: Then he sent him home and said, “Do not even go into the village.” III. The Mystery Begins to Be Revealed Peter’s Confession About Jesus.
text.DRB: And he sent him into his house, saying: Go into thy house; and if thou enter into the town, tell nobody.

text.NRSV: Jesus went on with his disciples to the villages of Caesarea Philippi; and on the way he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that I am?”
text.Rotherham: And forth went Jesus and his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea of Philip. And, in the way, he was questioning his disciples, saying unto them—Who, are men saying that I am?
text.KJV: And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Cesarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?
text.NABRE: Now Jesus and his disciples set out for the villages of Caesarea Philippi. Along the way he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that I am?”
text.DRB: And Jesus went forth and his Disciples into the towns of Cæsarea-Philippi; and in the way he asked his Disciples, saying to them: Whom do men say that I am?

text.NRSV: And they answered him, “John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets.”
text.Rotherham: And they answered him, saying—John the Immerser, and, others, Elijah,—and, others, One of the prophets.
text.KJV: And they answered, John the Baptist: but some say, Elias; and others, One of the prophets.
text.NABRE: They said in reply, “John the Baptist, others Elijah, still others one of the prophets.”
text.DRB: Who answered him, saying: John the Baptist, some Elias, & other some as it were one of the Prophets.

text.NRSV: He asked them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered him, “You are the Messiah.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, went on to question them—But, who, say, ye, that I am? Peter, answering, saith unto him—Thou, art, the Christ.
text.KJV: And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.
text.NABRE: And he asked them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter said to him in reply, “You are the Messiah.”
text.DRB: Then he saith to them: But whom do you say that I am? Peter answering said to him: Thou art Christ.

text.NRSV: And he sternly ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
text.Rotherham: And he straitly charged them, that, no one, they should tell concerning him.
text.KJV: And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.
text.NABRE: Then he warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text.DRB: And he threatened them that they should not tell any man of him.

text.NRSV: Then he began to teach them that the Son of Man must undergo great suffering, and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
text.Rotherham: And he began to be teaching them—The Son of Man, must needs suffer many things, and be rejected by the Elders and the High-priests and the Scribes,—and be slain; and, after three days, arise.
text.KJV: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and of the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
text.NABRE: The First Prediction of the Passion. He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer greatly and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and rise after three days.
text.DRB: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the Ancients and of the high Priests & the Scribes, and be killed: and after three days rise again.

text.NRSV: He said all this quite openly. And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
text.Rotherham: And, openly, was he speaking the word. And Peter, taking him aside, began to rebuke him.
text.KJV: And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.
text.NABRE: He spoke this openly. Then Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
text.DRB: And he spake the word openly. And Peter taking him, began to rebuke him.

text.NRSV: But turning and looking at his disciples, he rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind me, Satan! For you are setting your mind not on divine things but on human things.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, turning round and looking upon his disciples, rebuked Peter, and saith—Withdraw behind me, Satan! because thou art not regarding the things, of God, but the things, of men.
text.KJV: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.
text.NABRE: At this he turned around and, looking at his disciples, rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind me, Satan. You are thinking not as God does, but as human beings do.”
text.DRB: Who turning, and seeing his Disciples, threatened Peter, saying: Go behind me Satan, because thou savourest not the things that are of God, but that are of men.

text.NRSV: He called the crowd with his disciples, and said to them, “If any want to become my followers, let them deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.
text.Rotherham: And, calling near the multitude with his disciples, he said unto them—If anyone willeth, after me, to come, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and be following me.
text.KJV: And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
text.NABRE: The Conditions of Discipleship. He summoned the crowd with his disciples and said to them, “Whoever wishes to come after me must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
text.DRB: And calling the multitude together with his Disciples, he said to them: If any man will follow me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

text.NRSV: For those who want to save their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake, and for the sake of the gospel, will find it.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever willeth, his own life, to save, shall lose it, but, whosoever shall lose his life for the sake [of me and] of the glad-message, shall save it;
text.KJV: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel’s, the same shall save it.
text.NABRE: For whoever wishes to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake and that of the gospel will save it.
text.DRB: For he that will save his life, shall lose it; and he that shall lose his life for me and the Gospel, shall save it.

text.NRSV: For what will it profit them to gain the whole world and forfeit their life?
text.Rotherham: For, what doth it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and be made to forfeit his life?
text.KJV: For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
text.NABRE: What profit is there for one to gain the whole world and forfeit his life?
text.DRB: For what shall it profit a man, if he gain the whole world, & suffer damage of his soul?

text.NRSV: Indeed, what can they give in return for their life?
text.Rotherham: For what can a man, give, in exchange for his life?
text.KJV: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
text.NABRE: What could one give in exchange for his life?
text.DRB: Or what permutation shall a man give for his soul?

text.NRSV: Those who are ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, of them the Son of Man will also be ashamed when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.”
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man also, will be ashamed, of him, whensoever he shall come, in the glory of his Father, with the holy messengers.
text.KJV: Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
text.NABRE: Whoever is ashamed of me and of my words in this faithless and sinful generation, the Son of Man will be ashamed of when he comes in his Father’s glory with the holy angels.”
text.DRB: For he that shall be ashamed of me, and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of man also will be ashamed of him, when he shall come in the glory of his Father with the holy Angels.
And he said to them: Amen I say to you, that there be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, until they see the Kingdom of God coming in power.

text.KJV: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That there be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 He also said to them, “Amen, I say to you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see that the kingdom of God has come in power.” The Transfiguration of Jesus.
text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see that the kingdom of God has come with power.”
text.DRB: And after six days Jesus taketh Peter and James and John, and bringeth them alone into a high mountain apart, and was transfigured before them.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying to them: Verily, say unto you—There, are, certain of those here standing, who shall in nowise taste of death, until they see the kingdom of God, already come in power.

text.KJV: And after six days Jesus taketh with him Peter, and James, and John, and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves: and he was transfigured before them.
text.NABRE: After six days Jesus took Peter, James, and John and led them up a high mountain apart by themselves. And he was transfigured before them,
text.NRSV: Six days later, Jesus took with him Peter and James and John, and led them up a high mountain apart, by themselves. And he was transfigured before them,
text.DRB: And his garments were made glistering and white exceedingly as snow, the like whereof a fuller cannot make white upon the earth.
text.Rotherham: And, after six days, Jesus taketh with him Peter and James and John, and bringeth them up into a high mountain, apart, alone,—and he was transformed before them;

text.KJV: And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow; so as no fuller on earth can white them.
text.NABRE: and his clothes became dazzling white, such as no fuller on earth could bleach them.
text.NRSV: and his clothes became dazzling white, such as no one on earth could bleach them.
text.DRB: And there appeared to them Elias with Moyses; and they were talking with Jesus.
text.Rotherham: and his garments became brilliant, exceeding white, such as, no fuller on the earth, is able, so, to whiten.

text.KJV: And there appeared unto them Elias with Moses: and they were talking with Jesus.
text.NABRE: Then Elijah appeared to them along with Moses, and they were conversing with Jesus.
text.NRSV: And there appeared to them Elijah with Moses, who were talking with Jesus.
text.DRB: And Peter answering, said to Jesus: Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles, one for thee, and one for Moyses, and one for Elias.
text.Rotherham: And there appeared unto them Elijah, with Moses,—and they were conversing with Jesus.

text.KJV: And Peter answered and said to Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
text.NABRE: Then Peter said to Jesus in reply, “Rabbi, it is good that we are here! Let us make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text.NRSV: Then Peter said to Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; let us make three dwellings, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text.DRB: For he knew not what he said; for they were frighted with fear:
text.Rotherham: And Peter, answering, saith unto Jesus—Rabbi! it is, delightful, for us to be, here,—let us therefore make three tents, for thee, one, and, for Moses, one, and, for Elijah, one;

text.KJV: For he wist not what to say; for they were sore afraid.
text.NABRE: He hardly knew what to say, they were so terrified.
text.NRSV: He did not know what to say, for they were terrified.
text.DRB: And there was a cloud overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, saying: This is my Son most dear: hear ye him.
text.Rotherham: for he knew not what he should answer, for, greatly affrighted, had they become.

text.KJV: And there was a cloud that overshadowed them: and a voice came out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.
text.NABRE: Then a cloud came, casting a shadow over them; then from the cloud came a voice, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him.”
text.NRSV: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and from the cloud there came a voice, “This is my Son, the Beloved; listen to him!”
text.DRB: And immediately looking about, they saw no man any more but Jesus only with them.
text.Rotherham: And there came a cloud, overshadowing them, and there came a voice, out of the cloud,—This is, my Son, the Beloved, Be hearkening unto him.

text.KJV: And suddenly, when they had looked round about, they saw no man any more, save Jesus only with themselves.
text.NABRE: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone but Jesus alone with them. The Coming of Elijah.
text.NRSV: Suddenly when they looked around, they saw no one with them any more, but only Jesus.
text.DRB: And as they descended from the mountain, he commanded them that they should not tell any man what things they had seen, but when the Son of man shall be risen again from the dead.
text.Rotherham: And, suddenly looking round, no longer, saw they, anyone, with them, save, Jesus only.

text.KJV: And as they came down from the mountain, he charged them that they should tell no man what things they had seen, till the Son of man were risen from the dead.
text.NABRE: As they were coming down from the mountain, he charged them not to relate what they had seen to anyone, except when the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
text.NRSV: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them to tell no one about what they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
text.DRB: And they kept in the word with themselves; questioning together what that should be, when he shall be risen from the dead.
text.Rotherham: And, as they were coming down out of the mountain, he charged them, that, unto no one, they should narrate what they had seen, save whenever the Son of Man, from among the dead should arise.

text.KJV: And they kept that saying with themselves, questioning one with another what the rising from the dead should mean.
text.NABRE: So they kept the matter to themselves, questioning what rising from the dead meant.
text.NRSV: So they kept the matter to themselves, questioning what this rising from the dead could mean.
text.DRB: And they asked him, saying: What say the Pharisees then and the Scribes, that Elias must come first?
text.Rotherham: And, the word, they held fast unto themselves, discussing what was, the rising from among the dead.

text.KJV: And they asked him, saying, Why say the scribes that Elias must first come?
text.NABRE: Then they asked him, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
text.NRSV: Then they asked him, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
text.DRB: Who answering said to them: Elias when he cometh first, shall restore all things: and how it is written of the Son of man, that he shall suffer many things and be contemned.
text.Rotherham: And they began to question him, saying—The Scribes, say, that, Elijah, must needs come, first?

text.KJV: And he answered and told them, Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.
text.NABRE: He told them, “Elijah will indeed come first and restore all things, yet how is it written regarding the Son of Man that he must suffer greatly and be treated with contempt?
text.NRSV: He said to them, “Elijah is indeed coming first to restore all things. How then is it written about the Son of Man, that he is to go through many sufferings and be treated with contempt?
text.DRB: But I say to you that Elias also is come (and they have done to him whatsoever they would) as it is written of him.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Elijah, indeed, coming first, restoreth all things; and yet, how is it written, regarding the Son of Man, that, many things, he must suffer, and be set at nought?

text.KJV: But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.
text.NABRE: But I tell you that Elijah has come and they did to him whatever they pleased, as it is written of him.” The Healing of a Boy with a Demon.
text.NRSV: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they did to him whatever they pleased, as it is written of him.”
text.DRB: And coming to his Disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the Scribes questioning with them.
text.Rotherham: But I say unto you—Elijah also, hath come, and they have done with him, whatsoever they pleased,—according as it is written regarding him.

text.KJV: And when he came to his disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the scribes questioning with them.
text.NABRE: When they came to the disciples, they saw a large crowd around them and scribes arguing with them.
text.NRSV: When they came to the disciples, they saw a great crowd around them, and some scribes arguing with them.
text.DRB: And forthwith all the people seeing Jesus, was astonished, and much afraid: and running to him, saluted him.
text.Rotherham: And, coming unto the disciples, they saw a large multitude around them, and Scribes discussing with them.

text.KJV: And straightway all the people, when they beheld him, were greatly amazed, and running to him saluted him.
text.NABRE: Immediately on seeing him, the whole crowd was utterly amazed. They ran up to him and greeted him.
text.NRSV: When the whole crowd saw him, they were immediately overcome with awe, and they ran forward to greet him.
text.DRB: And he asked them, what do you question of among you?
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, all the multitude, seeing him, were greatly amazed, and, running near, began saluting him.

text.KJV: And he asked the scribes, What question ye with them?
text.NABRE: He asked them, “What are you arguing about with them?”
text.NRSV: He asked them, “What are you arguing about with them?”
text.DRB: And one of the multitude answering, said: Master, I have brought my son to thee, having a dumb spirit,
text.Rotherham: And he questioned them—What are ye discussing with them?

text.KJV: And one of the multitude answered and said, Master, I have brought unto thee my son, which hath a dumb spirit;
text.NABRE: Someone from the crowd answered him, “Teacher, I have brought to you my son possessed by a mute spirit.
text.NRSV: Someone from the crowd answered him, “Teacher, I brought you my son; he has a spirit that makes him unable to speak;
text.DRB: Who, wheresoever he taketh him, dasheth him, and he foameth, and gnasheth with the teeth, and withereth: and I spake to thy Disciples to cast him out, and they could not.
text.Rotherham: And one out of the multitude answered him—Teacher! I brought my son unto thee, having a dumb spirit;

text.KJV: And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
text.NABRE: Wherever it seizes him, it throws him down; he foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and becomes rigid. I asked your disciples to drive it out, but they were unable to do so.”
text.NRSV: and whenever it seizes him, it dashes him down; and he foams and grinds his teeth and becomes rigid; and I asked your disciples to cast it out, but they could not do so.”
text.DRB: Who answering them, said: O incredulous generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? Bring him unto me.
text.Rotherham: and, wheresoever it seizeth him, it teareth him, and he foameth, and grindeth his teeth, and weareth himself out;—and I spake to thy disciples, that they should cast it out, and they could not.

text.KJV: He answereth him, and saith, O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “O faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I endure you? Bring him to me.”
text.NRSV: He answered them, “You faithless generation, how much longer must I be among you? How much longer must I put up with you? Bring him to me.”
text.DRB: And they brought him. And when he had seen him, immediately the spirit troubled him; and being thrown upon the ground, he tumbled foaming.
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, saith—O faithless generation! how long, shall I be with you? how long, shall I bear with you? Bring him unto me!

text.KJV: And they brought him unto him: and when he saw him, straightway the spirit tare him; and he fell on the ground, and wallowed foaming.
text.NABRE: They brought the boy to him. And when he saw him, the spirit immediately threw the boy into convulsions. As he fell to the ground, he began to roll around and foam at the mouth.
text.NRSV: And they brought the boy to him. When the spirit saw him, immediately it convulsed the boy, and he fell on the ground and rolled about, foaming at the mouth.
text.DRB: And he asked his father: How long time is it since this hath chanced unto him? But he said: From his infancy:
text.Rotherham: And they brought him unto him. And, seeing him, the spirit, straightway tare him,—and, falling upon the ground, he wallowed, foaming.

text.KJV: And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.
text.NABRE: Then he questioned his father, “How long has this been happening to him?” He replied, “Since childhood.
text.NRSV: Jesus asked the father, “How long has this been happening to him?” And he said, “From childhood.
text.DRB: And oftentimes hath he cast him into fire and into waters, to destroy him. But if thou canst any thing, help us, having compassion on us.
text.Rotherham: And he questioned his father—How long a time, is it, that, this, hath befallen him? and he said—From childhood;

text.KJV: And ofttimes it hath cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him: but if thou canst do any thing, have compassion on us, and help us.
text.NABRE: It has often thrown him into fire and into water to kill him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us and help us.”
text.NRSV: It has often cast him into the fire and into the water, to destroy him; but if you are able to do anything, have pity on us and help us.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.
text.Rotherham: and, many times, hath it cast him, both into fire, and into waters, that it might destroy him; but, if anything be possible to thee, help us, have compassion upon us.

text.KJV: Jesus said unto him, If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “‘If you can!’ Everything is possible to one who has faith.”
text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “‘If you are able!’—All things can be done for the one who believes.”
text.DRB: And incontinent the father of the boy crying out, with tears said: I do believe Lord; help my incredulity.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—As for this, If it be possible to thee, all things, are possible, to him who hath faith.

text.KJV: And straightway the father of the child cried out, and said with tears, Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.
text.NABRE: Then the boy’s father cried out, “I do believe, help my unbelief!”
text.NRSV: Immediately the father of the child cried out, “I believe; help my unbelief!”
text.DRB: And when Jesus saw the people running together, he threatened the unclean spirit, saying to him: Deaf and dumb Spirit, I command thee, go out of him, and enter not any more into him.
text.Rotherham: Straightway crying out, the father of the child was saying—I have faith! help my, want, of faith.

text.KJV: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foul spirit, saying unto him, Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.
text.NABRE: Jesus, on seeing a crowd rapidly gathering, rebuked the unclean spirit and said to it, “Mute and deaf spirit, I command you: come out of him and never enter him again!”
text.NRSV: When Jesus saw that a crowd came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, “You spirit that keeps this boy from speaking and hearing, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!”
text.DRB: And crying out; and greatly tearing him, he went out of him, and he became as dead, so that many said, that he is dead.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, seeing that a multitude is running together, rebuked the impure spirit, saying unto it—Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I, give orders unto thee: Come forth out of him, and do not, any more, enter into him.

text.KJV: And the spirit cried, and rent him sore, and came out of him: and he was as one dead; insomuch that many said, He is dead.
text.NABRE: Shouting and throwing the boy into convulsions, it came out. He became like a corpse, which caused many to say, “He is dead!”
text.NRSV: After crying out and convulsing him terribly, it came out, and the boy was like a corpse, so that most of them said, “He is dead.”
text.DRB: But Jesus holding his hand, lifted him up; and he rose.
text.Rotherham: And, crying out, and greatly mangling him, it came forth; and he became as if dead, so that, the more part, were saying—He is dead!

text.KJV: But Jesus took him by the hand, and lifted him up; and he arose.
text.NABRE: But Jesus took him by the hand, raised him, and he stood up.
text.NRSV: But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he was able to stand.
text.DRB: And when he was entered into the house, his Disciples secretly asked him, why could not we cast him out?
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, grasping his hand, raised him, and he stood up.

text.KJV: And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why could not we cast him out?
text.NABRE: When he entered the house, his disciples asked him in private, “Why could we not drive it out?”
text.NRSV: When he had entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why could we not cast it out?”
text.DRB: And he said to them: This kind can go out by nothing but by prayer and fasting.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had gone into a house, his disciples, privately, were questioning him—Why, were, we, not able to cast it out?

text.KJV: And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “This kind can only come out through prayer.”
text.NRSV: He said to them, “This kind can come out only through prayer.”
text.DRB: And departing thence they passed by Galilee, neither would he that any man should know.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—This kind, by nothing, can come out, save, by prayer.

text.KJV: And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.
text.NABRE: The Second Prediction of the Passion. They left from there and began a journey through Galilee, but he did not wish anyone to know about it.
text.NRSV: They went on from there and passed through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know it;
text.DRB: And he taught his Disciples, and said to them: That the Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men, and they shall kill him, and being killed the third day he shall rise again.
text.Rotherham: And, from thence, going forth, they were journeying through Galilee,—and he was not wishing that any should get to know it;

text.KJV: For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, The Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.
text.NABRE: He was teaching his disciples and telling them, “The Son of Man is to be handed over to men and they will kill him, and three days after his death he will rise.”
text.NRSV: for he was teaching his disciples, saying to them, “The Son of Man is to be betrayed into human hands, and they will kill him, and three days after being killed, he will rise again.”
text.DRB: But they knew not the word: and they were afraid to ask him.
text.Rotherham: for he was teaching his disciples and saying [unto them], The Son of Man, is to be delivered up, into the hands of men, and they will slay him,—and, being slain, after three days, will he arise.

text.KJV: But they understood not that saying, and were afraid to ask him.
text.NABRE: But they did not understand the saying, and they were afraid to question him. IV. The Full Revelation of the Mystery The Greatest in the Kingdom.
text.NRSV: But they did not understand what he was saying and were afraid to ask him.
text.DRB: And they came to Capharnaum. Who, when he was in the house, asked them, what did you treat of in the way?
text.Rotherham: But they were not understanding the declaration, and feared, to question, him.

text.KJV: And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?
text.NABRE: They came to Capernaum and, once inside the house, he began to ask them, “What were you arguing about on the way?”
text.NRSV: Then they came to Capernaum; and when he was in the house he asked them, “What were you arguing about on the way?”
text.DRB: But they held their peace, for in the way they had disputed among themselves, which of them should be the greater.
text.Rotherham: And they came into Capernaum. And, happening to be, in the house, he was questioning them—What, in the way, were ye discussing?

text.KJV: But they held their peace: for by the way they had disputed among themselves, who should be the greatest.
text.NABRE: But they remained silent. They had been discussing among themselves on the way who was the greatest.
text.NRSV: But they were silent, for on the way they had argued with one another who was the greatest.
text.DRB: And sitting down, he called the Twelve, and saith to them: If any man will be first, he shall be last of all, and the minister of all.
text.Rotherham: And they were silent; for, with one another, they had discussed in the way, who should be greatest.

text.KJV: And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and servant of all.
text.NABRE: Then he sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, “If anyone wishes to be first, he shall be the last of all and the servant of all.”
text.NRSV: He sat down, called the twelve, and said to them, “Whoever wants to be first must be last of all and servant of all.”
text.DRB: And taking a child, he set him in the midst of them. Whom when he had embraced, he said to them:
text.Rotherham: And, taking a seat, he addressed the twelve, and saith unto them—If anyone willeth to be, first, he shall be, least of all, and, minister of all.

text.KJV: And he took a child, and set him in the midst of them: and when he had taken him in his arms, he said unto them,
text.NABRE: Taking a child he placed it in their midst, and putting his arms around it he said to them,
text.NRSV: Then he took a little child and put it among them; and taking it in his arms, he said to them,
text.DRB: Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me. And Whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.
text.Rotherham: And, taking a child, he set it in the midst of them,—and, folding it in his arms, said unto them—

text.KJV: Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.
text.NABRE: “Whoever receives one child such as this in my name, receives me; and whoever receives me, receives not me but the One who sent me.” Another Exorcist.
text.NRSV: “Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes not me but the one who sent me.”
text.DRB: John answered him, saying: Master we saw one casting out devils in thy name, who followeth not us, and we prohibited him.
text.Rotherham: Whosoever, unto, [one of] these children, shall give welcome, upon my name, unto me, giveth welcome; and, whosoever, unto me, giveth welcome, not, unto me, giveth welcome, but, unto him that sent me.

text.KJV: And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.
text.NABRE: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone driving out demons in your name, and we tried to prevent him because he does not follow us.”
text.NRSV: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he was not following us.”
text.DRB: But Jesus said: Do not prohibit him. For there is no man that doth a miracle in my name, and can soon speak ill of me.
text.Rotherham: John said unto him—Teacher! we saw someone, in thy name, casting out demons,—and we forbade him, because he was not following with us.

text.KJV: But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.
text.NABRE: Jesus replied, “Do not prevent him. There is no one who performs a mighty deed in my name who can at the same time speak ill of me.
text.NRSV: But Jesus said, “Do not stop him; for no one who does a deed of power in my name will be able soon afterward to speak evil of me.
text.DRB: For he that is not against you, is for you.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said—Do not forbid him! For, no one, is there, who shall do a work of power in my name, and have power, soon, to revile me;

text.KJV: For he that is not against us is on our part.
text.NABRE: For whoever is not against us is for us.
text.NRSV: Whoever is not against us is for us.
text.DRB: For Whosoever shall give you to drink a cup of water in my name, because you are Christ's; amen I say to you, he shall not lose his reward.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever is not, against, us, is, for, us;

text.KJV: For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.
text.NABRE: Anyone who gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ, amen, I say to you, will surely not lose his reward.
text.NRSV: For truly I tell you, whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you bear the name of Christ will by no means lose the reward.
text.DRB: And whosoever shall scandalize one of these little ones believing in me; it is good for him rather if a millstone were put about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever shall give you to drink a cup of water, in name that, Christ’s, ye are, Verily! I say unto you, in nowise, shall lose his reward.

text.KJV: And whosoever shall offend one of these little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.
text.NABRE: Temptations to Sin. “Whoever causes one of these little ones who believe [in me] to sin, it would be better for him if a great millstone were put around his neck and he were thrown into the sea.
text.NRSV: “If any of you put a stumbling block before one of these little ones who believe in me, it would be better for you if a great millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea.
text.DRB: And if thy hand scandalize thee, cut it off. It is good for thee to enter into life, maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire unquenchable,
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall cause to stumble one of these little ones that believe, it is, seemly, for him, rather, if there is hung a large millstone about his neck, and he is cast into the sea.

text.KJV: And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
text.NABRE: If your hand causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life maimed than with two hands to go into Gehenna, into the unquenchable fire.
text.NRSV: If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life maimed than to have two hands and to go to hell, to the unquenchable fire.
text.DRB: where their worm dieth not, and the fire quencheth not.
text.Rotherham: And, if thy hand shall cause thee to stumble, cut it off,—it is, seemly, for thee, maimed, to enter into life, rather than having, the two hands, to depart into the gehenna, into the fire that is not quenched;

text.KJV: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
text.DRB: And if thy foot scandalize thee, chop it off. It is good for thee to enter into life everlasting, lame, rather than having two feet, to be cast into the hell of unquenchable fire,

text.KJV: And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
text.NABRE: And if your foot causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life crippled than with two feet to be thrown into Gehenna.
text.NRSV: And if your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life lame than to have two feet and to be thrown into hell.
text.DRB: where their worm dieth not, and the fire quencheth not.
text.Rotherham: And, if thy foot be causing thee to stumble, cut it off,—it is, seemly, for thee, to enter into life, lame, rather than having, the two feet, to be cast into the gehenna;

text.KJV: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
text.DRB: And if thine eye scandalize thee, cast it out. It is good for thee with one eye to enter into the Kingdom of God, rather than having two eyes, to be cast into the hell of fire,

text.KJV: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:
text.NABRE: And if your eye causes you to sin, pluck it out. Better for you to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye than with two eyes to be thrown into Gehenna,
text.NRSV: And if your eye causes you to stumble, tear it out; it is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and to be thrown into hell,
text.DRB: where their worm dieth not, and the fire quencheth not.
text.Rotherham: And, if thine eye be causing thee to stumble, thrust it out,—it is, seemly, for thee, one-eye, to enter into the kingdom of God, rather than having, two eyes, to be cast into gehenna,

text.KJV: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
text.NABRE: where ‘their worm does not die, and the fire is not quenched.’
text.NRSV: where their worm never dies, and the fire is never quenched.
text.DRB: For every one shall be salted with fire: and every victim shall be salted with salt.
text.Rotherham: Where, their worm, dieth not, and, the fire, is not quenched,

text.KJV: For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.
text.NABRE: The Simile of Salt. “Everyone will be salted with fire.
text.NRSV: “For everyone will be salted with fire.
text.DRB: Salt is good. But if the salt shall be unseasoned; wherewith will you season it? Have salt in you, and have peace among you.
text.Rotherham: For, everyone, with fire, shall be salted.

text.KJV: Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.
text.NABRE: Salt is good, but if salt becomes insipid, with what will you restore its flavor? Keep salt in yourselves and you will have peace with one another.”
text.NRSV: Salt is good; but if salt has lost its saltiness, how can you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”
text.Rotherham: Salt is, good: but, if, salt, become, saltless, wherewith will ye, prepare, it? Have, within yourselves, salt, and be at peace, one with another.

text.NRSV: He left that place and went to the region of Judea and beyond the Jordan. And crowds again gathered around him; and, as was his custom, he again taught them.
text.Rotherham: And, from thence, arising, he cometh into the bounds of Judaea and beyond the Jordan,—and there come together again, multitudes unto him, and, as he had been wont, again, was he teaching them.
text.KJV: And he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judea by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 Marriage and Divorce. He set out from there and went into the district of Judea [and] across the Jordan. Again crowds gathered around him and, as was his custom, he again taught them.
text.DRB: And rising up thence, he cometh into the coasts of Jewry beyond Jordan: and the multitudes assemble again unto him. And as he was accustomed, again he taught them.

text.NRSV: Some Pharisees came, and to test him they asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”
text.Rotherham: And Pharisees coming near were questioning him—whether it is allowed a husband to divorce a wife, testing him.
text.KJV: And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.
text.NABRE: The Pharisees approached and asked, “Is it lawful for a husband to divorce his wife?” They were testing him.
text.DRB: And the Pharisees coming near, asked him: Is it lawful for a man to dismiss his wife? Tempting him.

text.NRSV: He answered them, “What did Moses command you?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—What unto you did, Moses, command?
text.KJV: And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “What did Moses command you?”
text.DRB: But he answering, said to them: What did Moyses command you?

text.NRSV: They said, “Moses allowed a man to write a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Moses permitted, to write, a roll of dismissal, and to divorce.
text.KJV: And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put her away.
text.NABRE: They replied, “Moses permitted him to write a bill of divorce and dismiss her.”
text.DRB: Who said: Moyses permitted to write a bill of divorce, and to dismiss her.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to them, “Because of your hardness of heart he wrote this commandment for you.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them—In view of your hardness of heart, wrote he for you this commandment;
text.KJV: And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.
text.NABRE: But Jesus told them, “Because of the hardness of your hearts he wrote you this commandment.
text.DRB: To whom Jesus answering, said: For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.

text.NRSV: But from the beginning of creation, ‘God made them male and female.’
text.Rotherham: But, from the beginning of creation, male and female, made he [them];
text.KJV: But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.
text.NABRE: But from the beginning of creation, ‘God made them male and female.
text.DRB: But from the beginning of the creation God made them male & female.

text.NRSV: ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, shall a man leave behind his father and mother,
text.KJV: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;
text.NABRE: For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother [and be joined to his wife],
text.DRB: For this cause, man shall leave his father & mother; and shall cleave to his wife.

text.NRSV: and the two shall become one flesh.’ So they are no longer two, but one flesh.
text.Rotherham: and, the two, shall become, one flesh; so that, no longer, are they two, but, one flesh.
text.KJV: And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.
text.NABRE: and the two shall become one flesh.’ So they are no longer two but one flesh.
text.DRB: And they two shall be in one flesh. Therefore now they are not two, but one flesh.

text.NRSV: Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
text.Rotherham: What then, God, hath yoked together, let, a man, not put asunder.
text.KJV: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
text.NABRE: Therefore what God has joined together, no human being must separate.”
text.DRB: That therefore which God hath joined together, let no man separate.

text.NRSV: Then in the house the disciples asked him again about this matter.
text.Rotherham: And, [coming] into the house again, the disciples, concerning this, were questioning him;
text.KJV: And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.
text.NABRE: In the house the disciples again questioned him about this.
text.DRB: And in the house again his Disciples asked him of the same thing.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her;
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Whosoever shall divorce his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her;
text.KJV: And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.
