text.NABRE: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her;
text.DRB: And he saith to them: Whosoever dismisseth his wife and marrieth another, committeth adultery upon her.

text.NRSV: and if she divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.”
text.Rotherham: And, if, she, divorcing her husband, marry another, she committeth adultery.
text.KJV: And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.
text.NABRE: and if she divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.”
text.DRB: And if the wife dismiss her husband, and marry another, she committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: People were bringing little children to him in order that he might touch them; and the disciples spoke sternly to them.
text.Rotherham: And they were bringing unto him children, that he might, touch, them,—the disciples, however, were rebuking them.
text.KJV: And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.
text.NABRE: Blessing of the Children. And people were bringing children to him that he might touch them, but the disciples rebuked them.
text.DRB: And they offered to him young children, that he might touch them. And the Disciples threatened those that offered them.

text.NRSV: But when Jesus saw this, he was indignant and said to them, “Let the little children come to me; do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of God belongs.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, seeing it, was greatly displeased, and said unto them—Suffer the children to come unto me,—do not hinder them; for, of such, is the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: When Jesus saw this he became indignant and said to them, “Let the children come to me; do not prevent them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
text.DRB: Whom when Jesus saw, he took it ill, and said to them: Suffer the little children to come unto me, and prohibit them not. For the Kingdom of God is for such.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will never enter it.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Whosoever shall not welcome the kingdom of God, as a child, in nowise shall enter thereinto.
text.KJV: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, whoever does not accept the kingdom of God like a child will not enter it.”
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, whosoever receiveth not the Kingdom of God as a little child, shall not enter into it.

text.NRSV: And he took them up in his arms, laid his hands on them, and blessed them.
text.Rotherham: And, folding them in his arms, he was blessing them, having laid his hands upon them.
text.KJV: And he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and blessed them.
text.NABRE: Then he embraced them and blessed them, placing his hands on them.
text.DRB: And embracing them, and imposing hands upon them, he blessed them.

text.NRSV: As he was setting out on a journey, a man ran up and knelt before him, and asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was going forth into a road, one, running, and kneeling before him, was questioning him—Good Teacher! what shall I do that, life age—abiding, I may inherit?
text.KJV: And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?
text.NABRE: The Rich Man. As he was setting out on a journey, a man ran up, knelt down before him, and asked him, “Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.DRB: And when he was gone forth in the way, a certain man running forth & kneeling before him asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may receive life everlasting?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Why callest thou me, good? None, is good, save one—God.
text.KJV: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him, why callest thou me good? None is good but one, God.

text.NRSV: You know the commandments: ‘You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; You shall not defraud; Honor your father and mother.’”
text.Rotherham: The commandments, thou knowest,—Do not commit murder, Do not commit adultery, Do not steal; Do not bear false witness, Do not defraud, Honour thy father and mother.
text.KJV: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.
text.NABRE: You know the commandments: ‘You shall not kill; you shall not commit adultery; you shall not steal; you shall not bear false witness; you shall not defraud; honor your father and your mother.’”
text.DRB: Thou knowest the commandments, Commit not adultery, Kill not, Steal not, Bear not false witness, do no fraud, Honour thy father and mother.

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Teacher, I have kept all these since my youth.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—Teacher! all these things, have I kept, from my youth.
text.KJV: And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.
text.NABRE: He replied and said to him, “Teacher, all of these I have observed from my youth.”
text.DRB: But he answering, said to him: Master all these things I have observed from my youth.

text.NRSV: Jesus, looking at him, loved him and said, “You lack one thing; go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, looking at him, loved him, and said unto him—One thing, unto thee, is wanting;—Withdraw! whatsoever thou hast, sell, and give unto [the] destitute,—and thou shalt have treasure in heaven; and come! be following me.
text.KJV: Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.
text.NABRE: Jesus, looking at him, loved him and said to him, “You are lacking in one thing. Go, sell what you have, and give to [the] poor and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.DRB: And Jesus beholding him, loved him, and said to him: One thing is wanting unto thee: go, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven; and come, follow me.

text.NRSV: When he heard this, he was shocked and went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
text.Rotherham: And, he, becoming gloomy because of the word, departed sorrowing, for he was holding, many possessions.
text.KJV: And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.
text.NABRE: At that statement his face fell, and he went away sad, for he had many possessions.
text.DRB: Who being stroken sad at the word, went away sorrowful. For he had many possessions.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “How hard it will be for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!”
text.Rotherham: And, looking around, Jesus saith unto his disciples—With what difficulty, shall, they who have money, enter, into the kingdom of God!
text.KJV: And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
text.NABRE: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!”
text.DRB: And Jesus looking about, saith to his Disciples: How hardly shall they that have money, enter into the Kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: And the disciples were perplexed at these words. But Jesus said to them again, “Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
text.Rotherham: And, the disciples, were in amazement, because of the words. But, Jesus, again answering, saith unto them—Children! how difficult, it is, to enter, into the kingdom of God!
text.KJV: And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!
text.NABRE: The disciples were amazed at his words. So Jesus again said to them in reply, “Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
text.DRB: And the Disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus again answering, saith to them: Children, how hard is it for them that trust in money, to enter into the Kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: It is, easier, for a camel, through the eye of a needle, to pass, than for, a rich man, into the kingdom of God, to enter.
text.KJV: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: It is easier for a camel to pass through [the] eye of [a] needle than for one who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: It is easier for a camel to pass through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: They were greatly astounded and said to one another, “Then who can be saved?”
text.Rotherham: And, they, were being exceedingly struck with astonishment, saying unto him—Who, then, can, be saved?
text.KJV: And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?
text.NABRE: They were exceedingly astonished and said among themselves, “Then who can be saved?”
text.DRB: Who marveled more, saying to themselves: And who can be saved?

text.NRSV: Jesus looked at them and said, “For mortals it is impossible, but not for God; for God all things are possible.”
text.Rotherham: Looking at them, Jesus saith—With men, impossible, but not, with God; for, all things, are possible, with God
text.KJV: And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.
text.NABRE: Jesus looked at them and said, “For human beings it is impossible, but not for God. All things are possible for God.”
text.DRB: And Jesus beholding them saith: With men it is impossible; but not with God. For all things are possible with God.

text.NRSV: Peter began to say to him, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”
text.Rotherham: Peter began to be saying unto him—Lo! we, have left all, and followed thee!
text.KJV: Then Peter began to say unto him, Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.
text.NABRE: Peter began to say to him, “We have given up everything and followed you.”
text.DRB: And Peter began to say unto him: Behold, we have left all things, and have followed thee.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields, for my sake and for the sake of the good news,
text.Rotherham: Jesus said—Verily, I say unto you—There is, no one, who hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or mother, or father, or children, or lands, for the sake of me, and [for the sake of] the glad-message,
text.KJV: And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel’s,
text.NABRE: Jesus said, “Amen, I say to you, there is no one who has given up house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or lands for my sake and for the sake of the gospel
text.DRB: Jesus answering, said: Amen I say to you, there is no man which hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or children, or lands for me and for the Gospel,

text.NRSV: who will not receive a hundredfold now in this age—houses, brothers and sisters, mothers and children, and fields, with persecutions—and in the age to come eternal life.
text.Rotherham: who shall not receive a hundredfold, now, in this season, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands,—with persecutions, and, in the age that is coming, life age-abiding.
text.KJV: But he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.
text.NABRE: who will not receive a hundred times more now in this present age: houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and lands, with persecutions, and eternal life in the age to come.
text.DRB: that shall not receive an hundred times so much now in this time; houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions: and in the world to come life everlasting.

text.NRSV: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.”
text.Rotherham: But, many, shall be—first-last, and [the] Last-first.
text.KJV: But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.
text.NABRE: But many that are first will be last, and [the] last will be first.”
text.DRB: But many that are first, shall be last; and the last, first.

text.NRSV: They were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them; they were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. He took the twelve aside again and began to tell them what was to happen to him,
text.Rotherham: Now they were in the way, going up unto Jerusalem, and Jesus was going on before them,—and they were in amazement; and, they who followed, were in fear. And, taking unto himself, again, the twelve, he began to be saying unto them, as to the things about to befall him—
text.KJV: And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus went before them: and they were amazed; and as they followed, they were afraid. And he took again the twelve, and began to tell them what things should happen unto him,
text.NABRE: The Third Prediction of the Passion. They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus went ahead of them. They were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he began to tell them what was going to happen to him.
text.DRB: And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem: and Jesus went before them, and they were astonished; and following were afraid. And taking again the Twelve, he began to tell them the things that should befall him.

text.NRSV: saying, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death; then they will hand him over to the Gentiles;
text.Rotherham: Lo! we are going up unto Jerusalem; and, the Son of Man, will be delivered up, unto the Chief-priests and the Scribes,—and they will condemn him to death, and deliver him up unto the nations;
text.KJV: Saying, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
text.NABRE: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles
text.DRB: That, behold we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man shall be betrayed to the chief Priests, and to the Scribes and Ancients, and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: they will mock him, and spit upon him, and flog him, and kill him; and after three days he will rise again.”
text.Rotherham: And they will mock him, and spit upon him, and scourge him, and, slay;—and, after three days, will he, arise.
text.KJV: And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.
text.NABRE: who will mock him, spit upon him, scourge him, and put him to death, but after three days he will rise.”
text.DRB: and they shall mock him, and spit on him, and scourge him, and kill him, and the third day he shall rise again.

text.NRSV: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came forward to him and said to him, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask of you.”
text.Rotherham: And there come near unto him, James and John, the [two] sons of Zebedee,—saying unto him—Teacher! we desire, that, whatsoever we shall ask thee, thou wilt do for us.
text.KJV: And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, come unto him, saying, Master, we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we shall desire.
text.NABRE: Ambition of James and John. Then James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to him and said to him, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask of you.”
text.DRB: And there came to him James and John the sons of Zebedee, saying: Master, we will that what thing soever we shall ask, thou do it to us.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “What is it you want me to do for you?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—What desire ye, I should do for you?
text.KJV: And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?
text.NABRE: He replied, “What do you wish [me] to do for you?”
text.DRB: But he said to them: What will you that I do to you?

text.NRSV: And they said to him, “Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your glory.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—Grant us, that, one on thy right hand, and one on thy left, we may sit, in thy glory.
text.KJV: They said unto him, Grant unto us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.
text.NABRE: They answered him, “Grant that in your glory we may sit one at your right and the other at your left.”
text.DRB: And they said: Grant to us, that we may sit, one on thy right hand & the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to them, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, or be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them—Ye know not what ye are asking: Are ye able to drink the cup that, I, am to drink? or to be immersed, with the immersion wherewith, I, am to be immersed?
text.KJV: But Jesus said unto them, Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “You do not know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup that I drink or be baptized with the baptism with which I am baptized?”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: You wot not what you ask. Can you drink the cup that I drink; or be baptized with the baptism wherewith I am baptized?

text.NRSV: They replied, “We are able.” Then Jesus said to them, “The cup that I drink you will drink; and with the baptism with which I am baptized, you will be baptized;
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—We are able. And, Jesus, said unto them—The cup which, I, am to drink, ye shall drink, and, with the immersion wherewith, I, am to be immersed, shall ye be immersed;
text.KJV: And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:
text.NABRE: They said to him, “We can.” Jesus said to them, “The cup that I drink, you will drink, and with the baptism with which I am baptized, you will be baptized;
text.DRB: But they said to him, we can. And Jesus said to them: The cup indeed which I drink, you shall drink; and with the Baptism wherewith I am baptized, shall you be baptized:

text.NRSV: but to sit at my right hand or at my left is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared.”
text.Rotherham: But, to sit on my right hand and on my left, is not mine to give, except unto them for whom it hath been prepared.
text.KJV: But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared.
text.NABRE: but to sit at my right or at my left is not mine to give but is for those for whom it has been prepared.”
text.DRB: but to sit on my right hand or on my left, is not mine to give unto you, but to whom it is prepared.

text.NRSV: When the ten heard this, they began to be angry with James and John.
text.Rotherham: And the ten, hearing, began to be sorely displeased, concerning James and John.
text.KJV: And when the ten heard it, they began to be much displeased with James and John.
text.NABRE: When the ten heard this, they became indignant at James and John.
text.DRB: And the ten hearing, began to be displeased at James and John.

text.NRSV: So Jesus called them and said to them, “You know that among the Gentiles those whom they recognize as their rulers lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, calling them near, saith unto them—Ye know that, they who think to rule the nations, lord it over them, and, their great ones, wield authority over them;
text.KJV: But Jesus called them to him, and saith unto them, Ye know that they which are accounted to rule over the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and their great ones exercise authority upon them.
text.NABRE: Jesus summoned them and said to them, “You know that those who are recognized as rulers over the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones make their authority over them felt.
text.DRB: And Jesus calling them, saith to them: You know that they which seem to rule over the Gentiles, overrule them: and their Princes have power over them.

text.NRSV: But it is not so among you; but whoever wishes to become great among you must be your servant,
text.Rotherham: But, not so, it is, among you. But, whosoever desireth to become, great, among you, shall be, your minister,
text.KJV: But so shall it not be among you: but whosoever will be great among you, shall be your minister:
text.NABRE: But it shall not be so among you. Rather, whoever wishes to be great among you will be your servant;
text.DRB: But it is not so in you. But whosoever will be greater, shall be your minister;

text.NRSV: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be slave of all.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever desireth, among you, to be, first, shall be, servant of all;
text.KJV: And whosoever of you will be the chiefest, shall be servant of all.
text.NABRE: whoever wishes to be first among you will be the slave of all.
text.DRB: and whosoever will be first among you, shall be the servant of all.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
text.Rotherham: For, even the Son of Man, came not to be ministered unto, but minister,—and to give his life, a ransom instead of many.
text.KJV: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.
text.NABRE: For the Son of Man did not come to be served but to serve and to give his life as a ransom for many.” The Blind Bartimaeus.
text.DRB: For the Son of man also is not come to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a redemption for many.

text.NRSV: They came to Jericho. As he and his disciples and a large crowd were leaving Jericho, Bartimaeus son of Timaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the roadside.
text.Rotherham: And they come into Jericho. And, as he was journeying forth from Jericho, and his disciples, and a considerable multitude, the son of Timaeus, blind Bar-Timaeus, a beggar, was sitting beside the road.
text.KJV: And they came to Jericho: and as he went out of Jericho with his disciples and a great number of people, blind Bartimeus, the son of Timeus, sat by the highway side begging.
text.NABRE: They came to Jericho. And as he was leaving Jericho with his disciples and a sizable crowd, Bartimaeus, a blind man, the son of Timaeus, sat by the roadside begging.
text.DRB: And they come to Jericho: and when he departed from Jericho, and his Disciples, and a very great multitude, the son of Timæus, Bar-timæus the blind man, sat by the way side begging.

text.NRSV: When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout out and say, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing that it was, Jesus the Nazarene, he began to be crying aloud, and saying—O Son of David! Jesus! have mercy on me.
text.KJV: And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, and say, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
text.NABRE: On hearing that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out and say, “Jesus, son of David, have pity on me.”
text.DRB: Who when he had heard, that it is Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry, and to say: Jesus, son of David, have mercy upon me.

text.NRSV: Many sternly ordered him to be quiet, but he cried out even more loudly, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And many were rebuking him, that he might hold his peace; but, he, so much the more, was crying aloud, O Son of David, have mercy on me.
text.KJV: And many charged him that he should hold his peace: but he cried the more a great deal, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
text.NABRE: And many rebuked him, telling him to be silent. But he kept calling out all the more, “Son of David, have pity on me.”
text.DRB: And many threatened him, to hold his peace. But he cried much more, Son of David, have mercy upon me.

text.NRSV: Jesus stood still and said, “Call him here.” And they called the blind man, saying to him, “Take heart; get up, he is calling you.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming to a stand, Jesus said—Call him! And they call the blind man, saying unto him—Take courage! rise! he calleth thee!
text.KJV: And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying unto him, Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee.
text.NABRE: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him.” So they called the blind man, saying to him, “Take courage; get up, he is calling you.”
text.DRB: And Jesus standing still commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying to him: Be of better comfort, arise, he calleth thee.

text.NRSV: So throwing off his cloak, he sprang up and came to Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, he, throwing off his mantle—springing to his feet, came unto Jesus.
text.KJV: And he, casting away his garment, rose, and came to Jesus.
text.NABRE: He threw aside his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus.
text.DRB: Who casting off his garment leapt up, and came to him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man said to him, “My teacher, let me see again.”
text.Rotherham: And, answering him, Jesus said—What desirest thou, I should do for thee? And, the blind man, said unto him—Rabboni! that may recover sight.
text.KJV: And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him in reply, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man replied to him, “Master, I want to see.”
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to him: What wilt thou that I do unto thee? And the blind man said to him: Rabboni, that I may see.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Go; your faith has made you well.” Immediately he regained his sight and followed him on the way.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Go thy way! thy faith, hath saved thee. And, straightway, he recovered sight, and was following him in the road.
text.KJV: And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
text.NABRE: Jesus told him, “Go your way; your faith has saved you.” Immediately he received his sight and followed him on the way.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Go thy ways, thy faith hath made thee safe. And forthwith he saw, and followed him in the way.

text.KJV: And when they came nigh to Jerusalem, unto Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount of Olives, he sendeth forth two of his disciples,
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 The Entry into Jerusalem. When they drew near to Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples
text.NRSV: When they were approaching Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples
text.DRB: And when they came nigh unto Jerusalem and Bethania to Mount-olivet, he sendeth two of his Disciples,
text.Rotherham: And, when they are drawing near unto Jerusalem, unto Bethphage and Bethany, towards the Mount of Olives, he sendeth forth two of his disciples,—

text.KJV: And saith unto them, Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him.
text.NABRE: and said to them, “Go into the village opposite you, and immediately on entering it, you will find a colt tethered on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it here.
text.NRSV: and said to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately as you enter it, you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden; untie it and bring it.
text.DRB: and saith to them: Go into the town that is against you, and immediately entering in thither, you shall find a colt tied, upon which no man yet hath sitten: loose him, and bring him.
text.Rotherham: and saith unto them—Go your way into the village that is over against you, and, straightway, as ye are entering it, ye shall find a colt tied, upon which, no, man, yet, hath sat: loose him, and bring him.

text.KJV: And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.
text.NABRE: If anyone should say to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ reply, ‘The Master has need of it and will send it back here at once.’”
text.NRSV: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ just say this, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here immediately.’”
text.DRB: And if any man shall say to you, what do you? Say that he is needful for our Lord: and incontinent he will send him hither.
text.Rotherham: And, if anyone unto you should say—Why are ye doing this? say—The Lord, of him hath need; and, straightway, he sendeth him off again here.

text.KJV: And they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door without in a place where two ways met; and they loose him.
text.NABRE: So they went off and found a colt tethered at a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
text.NRSV: They went away and found a colt tied near a door, outside in the street. As they were untying it,
text.DRB: And going their ways, they found the colt tied before the gate without in the meeting of two ways: and they loose him.
text.Rotherham: And they departed, and found a colt, tied unto a door outside, on the street,—and they are loosing him.

text.KJV: And certain of them that stood there said unto them, What do ye, loosing the colt?
text.NABRE: Some of the bystanders said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
text.NRSV: some of the bystanders said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
text.DRB: And certain of them that stood there, said to them: What do you loosing the colt?
text.Rotherham: And, certain of them that were there standing, were saying unto them—What are ye doing, loosing the colt?

text.KJV: And they said unto them even as Jesus had commanded: and they let them go.
text.NABRE: They answered them just as Jesus had told them to, and they permitted them to do it.
text.NRSV: They told them what Jesus had said; and they allowed them to take it.
text.DRB: Who said to them as Jesus had commanded them; and they did let him go with them.
text.Rotherham: and they said unto them as, Jesus, said,—and they let them go.

text.KJV: And they brought the colt to Jesus, and cast their garments on him; and he sat upon him.
text.NABRE: So they brought the colt to Jesus and put their cloaks over it. And he sat on it.
text.NRSV: Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks on it; and he sat on it.
text.DRB: And they brought the colt to Jesus; and they lay their garments upon him, and he sat upon him.
text.Rotherham: And they bring the colt unto Jesus, and throw upon him their mantles,—and he took his seat upon him.

text.KJV: And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut down branches off the trees, and strawed them in the way.
text.NABRE: Many people spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut from the fields.
text.NRSV: Many people spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut in the fields.
text.DRB: And many spread their garments in the way: and others did cut boughs from the trees, and strawed them in the way.
text.Rotherham: And, many, spread out, their mantles, along the way, and, others, young branches,—cutting them out of the fields.

text.KJV: And they that went before, and they that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord:
text.NABRE: Those preceding him as well as those following kept crying out: “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!
text.NRSV: Then those who went ahead and those who followed were shouting, “Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
text.DRB: And they that went before and they that followed, cried saying: Hosanna, blessed is he that cometh in the name of our Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, they who were going before, and they who were following after, were crying aloud—Hosanna! Blessed, is he that is coming in the name of the Lord!

text.KJV: Blessed be the kingdom of our father David, that cometh in the name of the Lord: Hosanna in the highest.
text.NABRE: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is to come! Hosanna in the highest!”
text.NRSV: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest heaven!”
text.DRB: Blessed is the Kingdom of our father David that cometh, Hosanna in the highest.
text.Rotherham: Blessed, is the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in the highest!

text.KJV: And Jesus entered into Jerusalem, and into the temple: and when he had looked round about upon all things, and now the eventide was come, he went out unto Bethany with the twelve.
text.NABRE: He entered Jerusalem and went into the temple area. He looked around at everything and, since it was already late, went out to Bethany with the Twelve. Jesus Curses a Fig Tree.
text.NRSV: Then he entered Jerusalem and went into the temple; and when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
text.DRB: And he entered Jerusalem into the temple; and having viewed all things round about, when now the evening hour was come, he went forth into Bethania with the Twelve.
text.Rotherham: And he entered into Jerusalem, into the temple, and, looking round on all things, late already being the hour, he went out into Bethany, with the twelve.

text.KJV: And on the morrow, when they were come from Bethany, he was hungry:
text.NABRE: The next day as they were leaving Bethany he was hungry.
text.NRSV: On the following day, when they came from Bethany, he was hungry.
text.DRB: And the next day when they departed from Bethania, he was an hungered.
text.Rotherham: And, on the morrow, when they came forth from Bethany, he hungered;

text.KJV: And seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came, if haply he might find any thing thereon: and when he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for the time of figs was not yet.
text.NABRE: Seeing from a distance a fig tree in leaf, he went over to see if he could find anything on it. When he reached it he found nothing but leaves; it was not the time for figs.
text.NRSV: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to see whether perhaps he would find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
text.DRB: And when he had seen a far off a figtree having leaves, he came if happily he could find any thing on it. And when he was come to it, he found nothing but leaves. For it was not the time for figs.
text.Rotherham: and, seeing a fig-tree from afar, having leaves, he came… if, therefore, he should find aught in it, and, coming up to it, nothing, found he, save, leaves; for it was not, the season, of figs.

text.KJV: And Jesus answered and said unto it, No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever. And his disciples heard it.
text.NABRE: And he said to it in reply, “May no one ever eat of your fruit again!” And his disciples heard it. Cleansing of the Temple.
text.NRSV: He said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard it.
text.DRB: And answering he said to it: Now no man eat fruit of thee any more for ever. And his Disciples heard it.
text.Rotherham: And, answering, he said unto it—No more, unto times age-abiding, let anyone of thee, eat fruit. And his disciples were listening.

text.KJV: And they come to Jerusalem: and Jesus went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves;
text.NABRE: They came to Jerusalem, and on entering the temple area he began to drive out those selling and buying there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves.
text.NRSV: Then they came to Jerusalem. And he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling and those who were buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves;
text.DRB: And they come to Jerusalem. And when he was entered into the temple, he began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and the tables of the bankers, and the chairs of them that sold pigeons he overthrew.
text.Rotherham: And they come into Jerusalem. And, entering into the temple, he began to be casting out them who were selling and them who were buying in the temple, and, the tables of the money-changers, and the seats of them who were selling doves, he overthrew,—

text.KJV: And would not suffer that any man should carry any vessel through the temple.
text.NABRE: He did not permit anyone to carry anything through the temple area.
text.NRSV: and he would not allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
text.DRB: And he suffered not that any man should carry a vessel through the temple;
text.Rotherham: and was not suffering that anyone should carry a vessel through the temple;

text.KJV: And he taught, saying unto them, Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.
text.NABRE: Then he taught them saying, “Is it not written: ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all peoples’? But you have made it a den of thieves.”
text.NRSV: He was teaching them and saying, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations’? But you have made it a den of robbers.”
text.DRB: And he taught, saying to them; Is it not written, That my house shall be called the house of prayer to all nations? But you have made it a den of thieves.
text.Rotherham: and was teaching, and saying—Is it not written, My house, a house of prayer, shall be called, for all the nations? but, ye, have made it, A den of robbers.

text.KJV: And the scribes and chief priests heard it, and sought how they might destroy him: for they feared him, because all the people was astonished at his doctrine.
text.NABRE: The chief priests and the scribes came to hear of it and were seeking a way to put him to death, yet they feared him because the whole crowd was astonished at his teaching.
text.NRSV: And when the chief priests and the scribes heard it, they kept looking for a way to kill him; for they were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was spellbound by his teaching.
text.DRB: Which when the chief Priests and the Scribes had heard, they sought how they might destroy him. For they were afraid of him, because the whole multitude was in admiration upon his doctrine.
text.Rotherham: And the High-priests and the Scribes, heard, it, and began seeking how they might, destroy, him,—for they were fearing him; for, all the multitude, were being struck with astonishment at his teaching.

text.KJV: And when even was come, he went out of the city.
text.NABRE: When evening came, they went out of the city.
text.NRSV: And when evening came, Jesus and his disciples went out of the city.
text.DRB: And when evening was come, he went forth out of the city.
text.Rotherham: And, as soon as it became late, they were going forth outside the city.

text.KJV: And in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.
text.NABRE: The Withered Fig Tree. Early in the morning, as they were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered to its roots.
text.NRSV: In the morning as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered away to its roots.
text.DRB: And when they passed by in the morning, they saw the figtree withered from the roots.
text.Rotherham: And, passing by early, they saw the fig-tree, withered from its roots;

text.KJV: And Peter calling to remembrance saith unto him, Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away.
text.NABRE: Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
text.NRSV: Then Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
text.DRB: And Peter remembering, said to him: Rabbi, behold the figtree that thou didst curse, is withered.
text.Rotherham: and, put in mind, Peter saith unto him—Rabbi! see! the fig-tree which thou didst curse, is withered.

text.KJV: And Jesus answering saith unto them, Have faith in God.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “Have faith in God.
text.NRSV: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering saith to them: Have faith of God.
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus saith unto them—Have faith in God.

text.KJV: For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart but believes that what he says will happen, it shall be done for him.
text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, if you say to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ and do not doubt in your heart, but believe that what you say will come to pass, it will be done for you.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, that whosoever shall say to this mountain, Be taken up and be cast into the sea, and shall not stagger in his heart, but believe that whatsoever he saith shall be done; it shall be done unto him.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be lifted up, and cast into the sea, and shall not doubt in his heart but shall believe that, what he speaketh, cometh to pass, it shall be his.

text.KJV: Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.
text.NABRE: Therefore I tell you, all that you ask for in prayer, believe that you will receive it and it shall be yours.
text.NRSV: So I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
text.DRB: Therefore I say to you, all things, whatsoever you ask, praying, believe that you shall receive, and they shall come unto you.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, I say unto you—All things, whatsoever ye are praying for and asking, believe that ye have received, and they shall be yours.

text.KJV: And when ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.
text.NABRE: When you stand to pray, forgive anyone against whom you have a grievance, so that your heavenly Father may in turn forgive you your transgressions.”
text.NRSV: “Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.”
text.DRB: And when you shall stand to pray, forgive if you have ought against any man; that also your Father which is in Heaven, may forgive you your sins.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye stand praying, forgive, if aught ye have against any,—that, your Father also, who is in the heavens, may forgive you your offences.

text.NABRE: [ ] The Authority of Jesus Questioned.
text.KJV: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.
text.DRB: If so be that you will not forgive, neither will your Father that is in Heaven, forgive you your sins.

text.KJV: And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,
text.NABRE: They returned once more to Jerusalem. As he was walking in the temple area, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders approached him
text.NRSV: Again they came to Jerusalem. As he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him
text.DRB: And they come again to Jerusalem. And when he walked in the temple, there come to him the chief Priests & the Scribes and the Ancients,
text.Rotherham: And they come again into Jerusalem. And, as he is walking in the temple, the High-priests and the Scribes and the Elders come unto him,

text.KJV: And say unto him, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority to do these things?
text.NABRE: and said to him, “By what authority are you doing these things? Or who gave you this authority to do them?”
text.NRSV: and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them?”
text.DRB: and they say to him: In what power doest thou these things? and who hath given thee this power, that thou shouldest do these things?
text.Rotherham: and were saying unto him—By what authority, these things, art thou doing? And who to thee hath given this authority that, these things, thou shouldst be doing?

text.KJV: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “I shall ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said to them: I also will ask you one word, and answer you me, and I will tell you in what power I do these things.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them—I will question you, as to one matter, and answer me,—and I will tell you, by what authority, these things, I am doing:

text.KJV: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.
text.NABRE: Was John’s baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.”
text.NRSV: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin? Answer me.”
text.DRB: The baptism of John was it from Heaven, or from men? Answer me.
text.Rotherham: The immersion by John, Of heaven, was it, or, of men? Answer me!

text.KJV: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then did ye not believe him?
text.NABRE: They discussed this among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘Of heavenly origin,’ he will say, ‘[Then] why did you not believe him?’
text.NRSV: They argued with one another, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
text.DRB: But they thought with themselves, saying: If we say, from Heaven; he will say, why then did you not believe him?
text.Rotherham: And they were deliberating among themselves, saying—If we say, Of heaven, he will say, Wherefore [then] did ye not believe him?

text.KJV: But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all men counted John, that he was a prophet indeed.
text.NABRE: But shall we say, ‘Of human origin’?”—they feared the crowd, for they all thought John really was a prophet.
text.NRSV: But shall we say, ‘Of human origin’?”—they were afraid of the crowd, for all regarded John as actually a prophet.
text.DRB: If we say, from men, we fear the people. For all accounted John that he was indeed a Prophet.
text.Rotherham: But, should we say, Of men…, they were in fear of the multitude, for, one and all, were holding John, in very deed, that he was, a prophet.

text.KJV: And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.
text.NABRE: So they said to Jesus in reply, “We do not know.” Then Jesus said to them, “Neither shall I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
text.NRSV: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
text.DRB: And they answering say to Jesus: We know not. And Jesus answering saith to them: Neither do I tell you in what power I do these things.
text.Rotherham: And, making answer unto Jesus, they say—We know not; and Jesus saith unto them—Neither do, I, tell you, by what authority, these things, I am doing.

text.NRSV: Then he began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the wine press, and built a watchtower; then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.
text.Rotherham: And he began to be speaking unto them, in parables,—A man planted, a vineyard, and put round it a wall, and digged a wine-vat, and built a tower,—and let it out to husbandmen; and left home.
text.KJV: And he began to speak unto them by parables. A certain man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the winefat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Parable of the Tenants. He began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a wine press, and built a tower. Then he leased it to tenant farmers and left on a journey.
text.DRB: And he began to speak to them in parables: A man planted a vineyard and made a hedge about it, and digged a trough, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen; and went forth into a strange country.

text.NRSV: When the season came, he sent a slave to the tenants to collect from them his share of the produce of the vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth, unto the husbandmen, in the season, a servant, that, from the husbandmen, he might receive of the fruits of the vineyard;
text.KJV: And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.
text.NABRE: At the proper time he sent a servant to the tenants to obtain from them some of the produce of the vineyard.
text.DRB: And he sent to the husbandmen in season a servant, to receive of the husbandmen, of the fruit of the vineyard.

text.NRSV: But they seized him, and beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: and, taking him, they beat him, and sent him away, empty.
text.KJV: And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.
text.NABRE: But they seized him, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
text.DRB: Who apprehending him, beat him; and sent him away empty.

text.NRSV: And again he sent another slave to them; this one they beat over the head and insulted.
text.Rotherham: And, again, sent he forth unto them, another servant; and, him, they smote on the head, and dishonoured.
text.KJV: And again he sent unto them another servant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.
text.NABRE: Again he sent them another servant. And that one they beat over the head and treated shamefully.
text.DRB: And again he sent to them another servant; and him they wounded in the head, and used him reproachfully.

text.NRSV: Then he sent another, and that one they killed. And so it was with many others; some they beat, and others they killed.
text.Rotherham: And another sent he forth; and, him, they slew. And many others; some, indeed beating, and, others, slaying.
text.KJV: And again he sent another; and him they killed, and many others; beating some, and killing some.
text.NABRE: He sent yet another whom they killed. So, too, many others; some they beat, others they killed.
text.DRB: And again he sent another, & him they killed: and many other, beating certain, and killing others.

text.NRSV: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
text.Rotherham: Yet one, had he, a son beloved: he sent him forth last unto them, saying—They will pay deference unto my son!
text.KJV: Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.
text.NABRE: He had one other to send, a beloved son. He sent him to them last of all, thinking, ‘They will respect my son.’
text.DRB: Therefore having yet one son most dear; him also he sent unto them last saying: That they will reverence my son.

text.NRSV: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
text.Rotherham: But, those husbandmen, unto themselves, said—This, is the heir: Come! let us slay him, and, ours, shall be, the inheritance.
text.KJV: But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.
text.NABRE: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
text.DRB: But the husbandmen said one to another. This is the heir; come, let us kill him; and the inheritance shall be ours.

text.NRSV: So they seized him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And, taking, they slew him, and cast him forth outside the vineyard.
text.KJV: And they took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
text.NABRE: So they seized him and killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
text.DRB: And apprehending him, they killed him, and cast him forth out of the vineyard.

text.NRSV: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
text.Rotherham: What will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come, and destroy the husbandmen, and let the vineyard unto others.
text.KJV: What shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others.
text.NABRE: What [then] will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come, put the tenants to death, and give the vineyard to others.
text.DRB: What therefore will the Lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the husbandmen; and will give the vineyard to others.

text.NRSV: Have you not read this scripture: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
text.Rotherham: Have ye not, this scripture, read—A stone which the builders rejected, the same, hath become head of the corner!
text.KJV: And have ye not read this scripture; The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner:
text.NABRE: Have you not read this scripture passage: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
text.DRB: Neither have you read this scripture, The stone which the builders rejected; the same is made the head of the corner:

text.NRSV: this was the Lord’s doing, and it is amazing in our eyes’?”
text.Rotherham: From the Lord, hath this come to pass, and is marvellous in our eyes?
text.KJV: This was the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?
text.NABRE: by the Lord has this been done, and it is wonderful in our eyes’?”
text.DRB: By our Lord was this done, and it is marvelous in our eyes?

text.NRSV: When they realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowd. So they left him and went away.
text.Rotherham: And they were seeking, to secure, him,—and were in fear of the multitude; for they perceived that, against them, the parable he had spoken. And, leaving him, they departed.
text.KJV: And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
text.NABRE: They were seeking to arrest him, but they feared the crowd, for they realized that he had addressed the parable to them. So they left him and went away.
text.DRB: And they sought to lay hands on him, and they feared the multitude. For they knew that he spake this parable to them. And leaving him they went their way.

text.NRSV: Then they sent to him some Pharisees and some Herodians to trap him in what he said.
text.Rotherham: And they send forth unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, that they might, catch, him, in discourse.
text.KJV: And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.
text.NABRE: Paying Taxes to the Emperor. They sent some Pharisees and Herodians to him to ensnare him in his speech.
text.DRB: And they send to him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians; that they should entrap him in his word.

text.NRSV: And they came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?
text.Rotherham: And, coming, they say unto him—Teacher! we know that, true, thou art, and it concerneth thee not about anyone,—for thou lookest not unto the face of men; but, in truth, the way of God, dost teach:—Is it allowable to give tax unto Caesar, or not? Should we give, or should we not give?
text.KJV: And when they were come, they say unto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Cesar, or not?
text.NABRE: They came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are a truthful man and that you are not concerned with anyone’s opinion. You do not regard a person’s status but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. Is it lawful to pay the census tax to Caesar or not? Should we pay or should we not pay?”
text.DRB: Who coming, say to him: Master, we know that thou art a true speaker, and carest not for any man; for thou doest not look upon the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to give tribute to Cæsar; or shall we not give it?

text.NRSV: Should we pay them, or should we not?” But knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why are you putting me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me see it.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them—Why are ye, tempting, me? Bring me a denary, that I may see it.
text.KJV: Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, Why tempt ye me? bring me a penny, that I may see it.
text.NABRE: Knowing their hypocrisy he said to them, “Why are you testing me? Bring me a denarius to look at.”
text.DRB: Who knowing their subtlety, said to them: Why tempt you me? Bring me a penny that I may see it.

text.NRSV: And they brought one. Then he said to them, “Whose head is this, and whose title?” They answered, “The emperor’s.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, brought one. And he saith unto them—Whose, is this image, and the inscription? And, they, said unto him—Caesar’s.
text.KJV: And they brought it. And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription? And they said unto him, Cesar’s.
text.NABRE: They brought one to him and he said to them, “Whose image and inscription is this?” They replied to him, “Caesar’s.”
text.DRB: But they brought it him. And he saith to them: Whose is this image, and inscription? They say to him, Cæsar's.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Give to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” And they were utterly amazed at him.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said—the things of Caesar, render, unto Caesar, and, the things of God, unto God. And they were marvelling at him.
text.KJV: And Jesus answering said unto them, Render to Cesar the things that are Cesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s. And they marvelled at him.
text.NABRE: So Jesus said to them, “Repay to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.” They were utterly amazed at him. The Question About the Resurrection.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to them: Render therefore the things that are Cæsar's, to Cæsar; and that are God's, to God. And they marveled at him.

text.NRSV: Some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him and asked him a question, saying,
text.Rotherham: And there come Sadducees unto him,—who, indeed say—Resurrection, there is none! and they were questioning him, saying—
text.KJV: Then come unto him the Sadducees, which say there is no resurrection; and they asked him, saying,
text.NABRE: Some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him and put this question to him,
text.DRB: And there came to him the Saducees that say there is no resurrection; and they asked him saying: Master,

text.NRSV: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no child, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
text.Rotherham: Teacher! Moses, wrote for us, that—If one’s brother die, and leave behind a wife, and leave no child, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
text.KJV: Master, Moses wrote unto us, If a man’s brother die, and leave his wife behind him, and leave no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
text.NABRE: saying, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us, ‘If someone’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no child, his brother must take the wife and raise up descendants for his brother.’
text.DRB: Moyses wrote unto us, that if any man's brother die, and leave his wife, and leave no children, his brother shall take his wife and raise up seed to his brother.

text.NRSV: There were seven brothers; the first married and died childless;
text.Rotherham: Seven brethren, there were: and, the first, took a wife, and, dying, left no seed,—
text.KJV: Now there were seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and dying left no seed.
text.NABRE: Now there were seven brothers. The first married a woman and died, leaving no descendants.
text.DRB: There were therefore seven brethren; and the first took a wife, and died leaving no issue.

text.NRSV: and the second married her and died, leaving no child; and the third likewise;
text.Rotherham: And, the second, took her, and died, not leaving behind seed,—and, the third, likewise,—
text.KJV: And the second took her, and died, neither left he any seed: and the third likewise.
text.NABRE: So the second married her and died, leaving no descendants, and the third likewise.
text.DRB: And the second took her, and died: and neither this left issue. And the third in like manner.

text.NRSV: none of the seven left a child. Last of all the woman herself died.
text.Rotherham: And, the seven, left no seed; last of all, the woman also, died:—
text.KJV: And the seven had her, and left no seed: last of all the woman died also.
text.NABRE: And the seven left no descendants. Last of all the woman also died.
text.DRB: And the seven took her in like sort; and did not leave issue. Last of all the woman also died.

text.NRSV: In the resurrection whose wife will she be? For the seven had married her.”
text.Rotherham: In the resurrection,—of, which, of them shall she be, wife? For, the seven, had her to wife.
text.KJV: In the resurrection therefore, when they shall rise, whose wife shall she be of them? for the seven had her to wife.
text.NABRE: At the resurrection [when they arise] whose wife will she be? For all seven had been married to her.”
text.DRB: In the resurrection therefore when they shall rise again, whose wife shall she be of these? For the seven had her to wife.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Is not this the reason you are wrong, that you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God?
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Are ye not, for this cause, deceiving yourselves, knowing neither the Scriptures, nor the power of God?
text.KJV: And Jesus answering said unto them, Do ye not therefore err, because ye know not the scriptures, neither the power of God?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “Are you not misled because you do not know the scriptures or the power of God?
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to them: Do ye not therefore err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God?

text.NRSV: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
text.Rotherham: For, when, from among the dead, they rise, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like messengers in the heavens.
text.KJV: For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage; but are as the angels which are in heaven.
text.NABRE: When they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but they are like the angels in heaven.
text.DRB: For when they shall rise again from the dead, they shall neither marry, nor be married, but are as the Angels in Heaven.

text.NRSV: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the story about the bush, how God said to him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
text.Rotherham: But, as touching the dead, that they, do rise,—Have ye not read in the book of Moses, at the Bush, how God spake unto him, saying—I [am] the God of Abraham, and God of Isaac, and God of Jacob:—
text.KJV: And as touching the dead, that they rise: have ye not read in the book of Moses, how in the bush God spake unto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?
text.NABRE: As for the dead being raised, have you not read in the Book of Moses, in the passage about the bush, how God told him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, [the] God of Isaac, and [the] God of Jacob’?
text.DRB: And as concerning the dead, that they do rise again, have you not read in the book of Moyses, how in the bush God spake to him, saying: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?

text.NRSV: He is God not of the dead, but of the living; you are quite wrong.”
text.Rotherham: He is not a God of, dead, men, but of, living. Greatly, are ye deceiving yourselves.
text.KJV: He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living: ye therefore do greatly err.
text.NABRE: He is not God of the dead but of the living. You are greatly misled.” The Greatest Commandment.
text.DRB: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You therefore are much deceived.

text.NRSV: One of the scribes came near and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, he asked him, “Which commandment is the first of all?”
text.Rotherham: And one of the Scribes, coming near, hearing them discussing, seeing that, well, he had answered them, began to question him—Which is the chief commandment of all?
text.KJV: And one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, and perceiving that he had answered them well, asked him, Which is the first commandment of all?
text.NABRE: One of the scribes, when he came forward and heard them disputing and saw how well he had answered them, asked him, “Which is the first of all the commandments?”
text.DRB: And there came one of the Scribes that had heard them questioning, and seeing that he had well answered them, asked him which was the first commandment of all.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “The first is, ‘Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one;
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—The chief is: Hear! O Israel,—The Lord our God, is, one Lord;
text.KJV: And Jesus answered him, The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord:
text.NABRE: Jesus replied, “The first is this: ‘Hear, O Israel! The Lord our God is Lord alone!
text.DRB: And Jesus answered him: That the first commandment of all is, Hear Israel: the Lord thy God, is one God.

text.NRSV: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.’
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou love the Lord thy God, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul,—and with all thy mind; and with all thy strength.
text.KJV: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength: this is the first commandment.
text.NABRE: You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.’
text.DRB: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God from thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with thy whole mind, and with thy whole power. This is the first commandment.

text.NRSV: The second is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”
text.Rotherham: The second, is, this—Thou shalt love, thy neighbour, as thyself. Greater than these, other commandment, is there none.
text.KJV: And the second is like, namely this, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. There is none other commandment greater than these.
text.NABRE: The second is this: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”
text.DRB: And the second is like to it: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. Another commandment greater than these there is not.

text.NRSV: Then the scribe said to him, “You are right, Teacher; you have truly said that ‘he is one, and besides him there is no other’;
text.Rotherham: The Scribe said unto him—Well, Teacher! in truth, hast thou said—He is, One, and there is none other, than he;
text.KJV: And the scribe said unto him, Well, Master, thou hast said the truth: for there is one God; and there is none other but he:
text.NABRE: The scribe said to him, “Well said, teacher. You are right in saying, ‘He is One and there is no other than he.’
text.DRB: And the Scribe said to him: Well Master, thou hast said in truth, that there is one God, and there is none other besides him.

text.NRSV: and ‘to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the strength,’ and ‘to love one’s neighbor as oneself,’—this is much more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
text.Rotherham: And, to love him, with all the heart, and with all the understanding,—and with all the might; and to love one’s neighbour as one’s self, is, abundantly more, than all the whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
text.KJV: And to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbour as himself, is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
text.NABRE: And ‘to love him with all your heart, with all your understanding, with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself’ is worth more than all burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
text.DRB: And that he be loved from the whole heart, and with the whole understanding, and with the whole soul, and with the whole strength: and to love his neighbour as himself, is a greater thing than all holocausts and sacrifices.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” After that no one dared to ask him any more questions.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, seeing him, that, with intelligence he answered, said unto him—Not far, [art thou] from the kingdom of God! And, no one, any longer, was daring to, question, him.
text.KJV: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, Thou art not far from the kingdom of God. And no man after that durst ask him any question.
text.NABRE: And when Jesus saw that [he] answered with understanding, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” And no one dared to ask him any more questions. The Question About David’s Son.
text.DRB: And Jesus seeing that he had answered wisely, said to him: Thou art not far from the Kingdom of God. And no man now durst ask him.

text.NRSV: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, “How can the scribes say that the Messiah is the son of David?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, was saying, as he taught in the temple—How say the Scribes that, the Christ, is, Son of David?
text.KJV: And Jesus answered and said, while he taught in the temple, How say the scribes that Christ is the Son of David?
text.NABRE: As Jesus was teaching in the temple area he said, “How do the scribes claim that the Messiah is the son of David?
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said, teaching in the temple: How do the Scribes say, that Christ is the son of David?

text.NRSV: David himself, by the Holy Spirit, declared, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.”’
text.Rotherham: David himself, hath said, by the Holy Spirit,—The Lord, hath said unto, my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, until I put thy foes beneath thy feet.
text.KJV: For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
text.NABRE: David himself, inspired by the holy Spirit, said: ‘The Lord said to my lord, “Sit at my right hand until I place your enemies under your feet.”’
text.DRB: For David himself saith in the Holy Ghost: Our Lord said to my Lord, sit on my right hand, until I put thine enemies the foot-stool of thy feet.

text.NRSV: David himself calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?” And the large crowd was listening to him with delight.
text.Rotherham: David himself, calleth him, Lord: whence, then, is he, his own son? And, the great multitude, was hearing him gladly.
text.KJV: David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.
text.NABRE: David himself calls him ‘lord’; so how is he his son?” [The] great crowd heard this with delight. Denunciation of the Scribes.
text.DRB: David therefore himself calleth him Lord, and whence is he his son? And a great multitude heard him gladly.

text.NRSV: As he taught, he said, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces,
text.Rotherham: And, in his teaching, he was saying—Beware of the Scribes, who desire, in robes, to be walking about, and salutations in the markets,
text.KJV: And he said unto them in his doctrine, Beware of the scribes, which love to go in long clothing, and love salutations in the marketplaces,
text.NABRE: In the course of his teaching he said, “Beware of the scribes, who like to go around in long robes and accept greetings in the marketplaces,
text.DRB: And he said to them in his doctrine: Take heed of the Scribes that will walk in long robes, and be saluted in the market-place,

text.NRSV: and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets!
text.Rotherham: And first seats in the synagogues, and first couches in the chief meals,—
text.KJV: And the chief seats in the synagogues, and the uppermost rooms at feasts:
text.NABRE: seats of honor in synagogues, and places of honor at banquets.
text.DRB: And sit in the first chairs in the Synagogues, and love the highest places at suppers:

text.NRSV: They devour widows’ houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
text.Rotherham: Who devour widows’ houses, and, for a pretence, are long in prayer: these, shall receive a more surpassing judgment.
text.KJV: Which devour widows’ houses, and for a pretence make long prayers: these shall receive greater damnation.
text.NABRE: They devour the houses of widows and, as a pretext, recite lengthy prayers. They will receive a very severe condemnation.” The Poor Widow’s Contribution.
text.DRB: Which devour widows' houses under the pretence of long prayer: these shall receive larger judgement.

text.NRSV: He sat down opposite the treasury, and watched the crowd putting money into the treasury. Many rich people put in large sums.
text.Rotherham: And, taking his seat over against the treasury, he was observing how, the multitude, was casting in copper into the treasury, and, man rich, were casting in, much.
text.KJV: And Jesus sat over against the treasury, and beheld how the people cast money into the treasury: and many that were rich cast in much.
text.NABRE: He sat down opposite the treasury and observed how the crowd put money into the treasury. Many rich people put in large sums.
text.DRB: And Jesus sitting over against the treasury, beheld how the multitude did cast money into the treasury, and many rich men did cast in much.

text.NRSV: A poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which are worth a penny.
text.Rotherham: And there came, one destitute, widow, and cast in two mites, which are, a farthing.
text.KJV: And there came a certain poor widow, and she threw in two mites, which make a farthing.
text.NABRE: A poor widow also came and put in two small coins worth a few cents.
text.DRB: And when there came a certain poor widow, she cast in two mites, which is a farthing.

text.NRSV: Then he called his disciples and said to them, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the treasury.
text.Rotherham: And, calling near his disciples, he said to them—Verily, I say unto you, this destitute widow, more than they all, hath cast in, of those casting into the treasury;
text.KJV: And he called unto him his disciples, and saith unto them, Verily I say unto you, That this poor widow hath cast more in, than all they which have cast into the treasury:
text.NABRE: Calling his disciples to himself, he said to them, “Amen, I say to you, this poor widow put in more than all the other contributors to the treasury.
text.DRB: And calling his Disciples together, he saith to them, Amen I say to you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than all that have cast into the treasury.

text.NRSV: For all of them have contributed out of their abundance; but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had, all she had to live on.”
text.Rotherham: For, they all, out of their surplus, cast in, but, she, out of her deficiency, all, as much as she had, cast in,—the whole of her living.
text.KJV: For all they did cast in of their abundance; but she of her want did cast in all that she had, even all her living.
text.NABRE: For they have all contributed from their surplus wealth, but she, from her poverty, has contributed all she had, her whole livelihood.”
