text.DRB: For all they of their aboundance have cast in; but she, of her penury hath cast in all that she had, her whole living.

text.NRSV: As he came out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Look, Teacher, what large stones and what large buildings!”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was going forth out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him—Teacher! see what manner of stones, and what manner of buildings!
text.KJV: And as he went out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him, Master, see what manner of stones and what buildings are here!
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 The Destruction of the Temple Foretold. As he was making his way out of the temple area one of his disciples said to him, “Look, teacher, what stones and what buildings!”
text.DRB: And when he went out of the Temple, one of his Disciples saith to him: Master, behold what manner of stones, and what kind of buildings.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus asked him, “Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left here upon another; all will be thrown down.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Art thou beholding these great buildings? In nowise, shall there be left here, stone upon stone, which shall, in any wise, not be thrown down.
text.KJV: And Jesus answering said unto him, Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be one stone left upon another that will not be thrown down.”
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to him: Seest thou all these great buildings? There shall not be left a stone upon a stone, that shall not be destroyed.

text.NRSV: When he was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
text.Rotherham: And, as he was sitting within the mount of Olives, over against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew were questioning him, privately—
text.KJV: And as he sat upon the mount of Olives over against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,
text.NABRE: The Signs of the End. As he was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple area, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
text.DRB: And when he sat in Mount-olivet against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him apart:

text.NRSV: “Tell us, when will this be, and what will be the sign that all these things are about to be accomplished?”
text.Rotherham: Tell us, When, these things, shall be, and, what the sign, when all these things shall be about to be concluded.
text.KJV: Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled?
text.NABRE: “Tell us, when will this happen, and what sign will there be when all these things are about to come to an end?”
text.DRB: Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign when all these things shall begin to be consummate?

text.NRSV: Then Jesus began to say to them, “Beware that no one leads you astray.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, began to be saying unto them—Beware, lest anyone, deceive, you;
text.KJV: And Jesus answering them began to say, Take heed lest any man deceive you:
text.NABRE: Jesus began to say to them, “See that no one deceives you.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering began to say to them, See, that no man seduce you.

text.NRSV: Many will come in my name and say, ‘I am he!’ and they will lead many astray.
text.Rotherham: For, many, will come on my name, saying, I, am he! and, will deceive, many.
text.KJV: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.
text.NABRE: Many will come in my name saying, ‘I am he,’ and they will deceive many.
text.DRB: For many shall come in my name saying that I am he; and they shall seduce many.

text.NRSV: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed; this must take place, but the end is not yet.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye shall hear of wars, and rumours of wars, be not alarmed—it must needs come to pass, but, not yet, is, the end.
text.KJV: And when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars, be ye not troubled: for such things must needs be; but the end shall not be yet.
text.NABRE: When you hear of wars and reports of wars do not be alarmed; such things must happen, but it will not yet be the end.
text.DRB: And when you shall hear of wars and bruits of wars, fear not. For these things must be, but the end is not yet.

text.NRSV: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. This is but the beginning of the birthpangs.
text.Rotherham: For there will arise—Nation against nation, and, kingdom against kingdom,—there will be earthquakes in places, there will be famines:—
text.KJV: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be earthquakes in divers places, and there shall be famines and troubles: these are the beginnings of sorrows.
text.NABRE: Nation will rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes from place to place and there will be famines. These are the beginnings of the labor pains.
text.DRB: For nation shall rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom, and there shall be earth-quakes in places, & famines. These things are the beginning of sorrows.

text.NRSV: “As for yourselves, beware; for they will hand you over to councils; and you will be beaten in synagogues; and you will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them.
text.Rotherham: A beginning of birth-pangs, are these things. But be, ye, taking heed, unto yourselves: they will deliver you up into high-councils, and, in synagogues, shall ye be beaten,—and, before governors and kings, shall ye be set, for my sake, for a witness unto them.
text.KJV: But take heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.
text.NABRE: The Coming Persecution. “Watch out for yourselves. They will hand you over to the courts. You will be beaten in synagogues. You will be arraigned before governors and kings because of me, as a witness before them.
text.DRB: But look to yourselves. For they shall deliver you up in Councils, and in Synagogues shall you be beaten, and you shall stand before Presidents and kings for my sake, for a testimony unto them.

text.NRSV: And the good news must first be proclaimed to all nations.
text.Rotherham: And, unto all the nations, first, must needs be proclaimed, the glad-message.
text.KJV: And the gospel must first be published among all nations.
text.NABRE: But the gospel must first be preached to all nations.
text.DRB: And into all nations first the Gospel must be preached.

text.NRSV: When they bring you to trial and deliver you up, do not worry beforehand about what you are to say; but say whatever is given you at that time, for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: And, when they are leading you, as they are delivering you up, be not beforehand anxious, what ye shall speak; but, whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, the same, speak,—for, ye, are not the speakers, but the Holy Spirit.
text.KJV: But when they shall lead you, and deliver you up, take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak, neither do ye premeditate: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye: for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: When they lead you away and hand you over, do not worry beforehand about what you are to say. But say whatever will be given to you at that hour. For it will not be you who are speaking but the holy Spirit.
text.DRB: And when they shall lead you and deliver you, be not careful beforehand what you shall speak; but that which shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye. For it is not you that speak, but the Holy Ghost.

text.NRSV: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death;
text.Rotherham: And, brother, will deliver up, brother, unto death, and, father, child, and, children, will rise up, against parents, and put them to death;
text.KJV: Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the son; and children shall rise up against their parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.
text.NABRE: Brother will hand over brother to death, and the father his child; children will rise up against parents and have them put to death.
text.DRB: And brother shall deliver brother unto death, and the father his Son; and the children shall arise against the parents, and shall work their death.

text.NRSV: and you will be hated by all because of my name. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
text.Rotherham: And ye will be men hated by all, because of my name; but, he that hath endured throughout, the same, shall be saved.
text.KJV: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.
text.NABRE: You will be hated by all because of my name. But the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.
text.DRB: And you shall be odious to all men for my name. But he that shall endure unto the end, he shall be saved.

text.NRSV: “But when you see the desolating sacrilege set up where it ought not to be (let the reader understand), then those in Judea must flee to the mountains;
text.Rotherham: But, when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, standing where it ought not, he that readeth, let him, think, then, they who are in Judaea, let them flee into the mountains;
text.KJV: But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not, (let him that readeth understand,) then let them that be in Judea flee to the mountains:
text.NABRE: The Great Tribulation. “When you see the desolating abomination standing where he should not (let the reader understand), then those in Judea must flee to the mountains,
text.DRB: And when you shall see the abomination of desolation, standing where it ought not (he that readeth, let him understand) then they that are in Jewry, let them flee unto the mountains:

text.NRSV: the one on the housetop must not go down or enter the house to take anything away;
text.Rotherham: He that is on the house-top, let him not come down, neither let him enter, to take away anything out of his louse;
text.KJV: And let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house, neither enter therein, to take any thing out of his house:
text.NABRE: [and] a person on a housetop must not go down or enter to get anything out of his house,
text.DRB: and he that is on the house-top, let him not go down into the house, nor enter in to take any thing out of his house:

text.NRSV: the one in the field must not turn back to get a coat.
text.Rotherham: And, he that hath gone into the field, let him not turn back unto the things behind, to take away his mantle.
text.KJV: And let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment.
text.NABRE: and a person in a field must not return to get his cloak.
text.DRB: and he that shall be in the field, let him not return back to take his garment.

text.NRSV: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days!
text.Rotherham: But alas for the women with child, and for them who are giving suck,—in those days.
text.KJV: But woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!
text.NABRE: Woe to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days.
text.DRB: And woe to them that are with child and that give suck in those days.

text.NRSV: Pray that it may not be in winter.
text.Rotherham: But be praying, that it may not happen in winter.
text.KJV: And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter.
text.NABRE: Pray that this does not happen in winter.
text.DRB: But pray that the things chance not in the winter.

text.NRSV: For in those days there will be suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, no, and never will be.
text.Rotherham: For in those days shall be a tribulation—such, that there hath not happened, the like, from the beginning of creation which God created, until the present time, and shall in nowise happen.
text.KJV: For in those days shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created unto this time, neither shall be.
text.NABRE: For those times will have tribulation such as has not been since the beginning of God’s creation until now, nor ever will be.
text.DRB: For those days shall be such tribulations as were not from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, neither shall be.

text.NRSV: And if the Lord had not cut short those days, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect, whom he chose, he has cut short those days.
text.Rotherham: And, save that the Lord hath shortened the days, no flesh should be saved; but, for the sake of the chosen of whom he hath made choice, he hath shortened the days.
text.KJV: And except that the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect’s sake, whom he hath chosen, he hath shortened the days.
text.NABRE: If the Lord had not shortened those days, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect whom he chose, he did shorten the days.
text.DRB: And unless the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect which he hath elected, he hath shortened the days.

text.NRSV: And if anyone says to you at that time, ‘Look! Here is the Messiah!’ or ‘Look! There he is!’—do not believe it.
text.Rotherham: And, then, if any, unto you, say—See! Here, is the Christ! See! there, do not believe it;
text.KJV: And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ; or, lo, he is there; believe him not:
text.NABRE: If anyone says to you then, ‘Look, here is the Messiah! Look, there he is!’ do not believe it.
text.DRB: And then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, lo, there: do not believe.

text.NRSV: False messiahs and false prophets will appear and produce signs and omens, to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
text.Rotherham: For there will arise, false Christs, and false prophets; and they will show signs and wonders, so as to deceive, if possible, the chosen.
text.KJV: For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce, if it were possible, even the elect.
text.NABRE: False messiahs and false prophets will arise and will perform signs and wonders in order to mislead, if that were possible, the elect.
text.DRB: For there shall rise up false-Christs and false-Prophets, and they shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce (if it be possible) the elect also.

text.NRSV: But be on your guard; I have foretold everything to you.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, beware: I have foretold you, all things.
text.KJV: But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.
text.NABRE: Be watchful! I have told it all to you beforehand.
text.DRB: You therefore take heed; behold I have foretold you all things.

text.NRSV: “But in those days, after that suffering, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light,
text.Rotherham: But, in those days, after that tribulation, the sun, shall be darkened, and, the moon, will not give her brightness,
text.KJV: But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,
text.NABRE: The Coming of the Son of Man. “But in those days after that tribulation the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light,
text.DRB: But in those days after that tribulation the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,

text.NRSV: and the stars will be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
text.Rotherham: And, the stars, will, out of the heavens, be falling,—and, the powers which are in the heavens, will be shaken;
text.KJV: And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.
text.NABRE: and the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
text.DRB: and the stars of Heaven shall be falling down, and the powers that are in Heaven, shall be moved.

text.NRSV: Then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in clouds’ with great power and glory.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will they see the Son of Man—coming in clouds, with great power and glory.
text.KJV: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
text.NABRE: And then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in the clouds’ with great power and glory,
text.DRB: And then they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds, with much power and glory.

text.NRSV: Then he will send out the angels, and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will he send forth the messengers, and they will gather together his chosen—out of the four winds, from utmost bound of earth, unto utmost bound of heaven.
text.KJV: And then shall he send his angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven.
text.NABRE: and then he will send out the angels and gather [his] elect from the four winds, from the end of the earth to the end of the sky.
text.DRB: And then shall he send his Angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of Heaven.

text.NRSV: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.
text.Rotherham: Now, from the fig-tree, learn ye, the parable:—When, already, her young branch, becometh tender, and, the leaves, are sprouting, ye observe that, near, is, the summer:
text.KJV: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:
text.NABRE: The Lesson of the Fig Tree. “Learn a lesson from the fig tree. When its branch becomes tender and sprouts leaves, you know that summer is near.
text.DRB: And of the figtree learn ye a parable. When now the bough thereof is tender, and the leaves come forth, you know that summer is very nigh.

text.NRSV: So also, when you see these things happening, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
text.Rotherham: Thus, ye also, when ye shall see these things coming to pass, observe ye, that, near, he is, at the doors.
text.KJV: So ye in like manner, when ye shall see these things come to pass, know that it is nigh, even at the doors.
text.NABRE: In the same way, when you see these things happening, know that he is near, at the gates.
text.DRB: So you also when you shall see these things come to pass, know ye that it is very nigh, at the doors.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—In nowise, shall this generation pass away, until all these things, shall happen:
text.KJV: Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, that this generation shall not pass, until all these things be done.

text.NRSV: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.Rotherham: The heaven and the earth, shall pass away, but, my words, shall not pass away.
text.KJV: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.
text.NABRE: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.DRB: Heaven & earth shall pass, but my words shall not pass.

text.NRSV: “But about that day or hour no one knows, neither the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning that day or hour, no one, knoweth, neither the messengers in heaven, nor the Son,—save the Father.
text.KJV: But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.
text.NABRE: Need for Watchfulness. “But of that day or hour, no one knows, neither the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
text.DRB: But of that day or hour no man knoweth, neither the Angels in Heaven, nor the Son, but the Father.

text.NRSV: Beware, keep alert; for you do not know when the time will come.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed, be watching, for ye know not, when, the season [is]:—
text.KJV: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.
text.NABRE: Be watchful! Be alert! You do not know when the time will come.
text.DRB: Take heed, watch, and pray. For you know not when the time is.

text.NRSV: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his slaves in charge, each with his particular task, and commands the doorkeeper to be on the watch.
text.Rotherham: As a man from home—having left his house, and given his servants the authority, to each one, his work,—and, unto the porter, hath given command, that he should watch:—
text.KJV: For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.
text.NABRE: It is like a man traveling abroad. He leaves home and places his servants in charge, each with his work, and orders the gatekeeper to be on the watch.
text.DRB: Even as a man who being gone into a strange country, left his house; and gave his servants authority over each work, and commanded the porter to watch.

text.NRSV: Therefore, keep awake—for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening, or at midnight, or at cockcrow, or at dawn,
text.Rotherham: Be watching, therefore, for ye know not, when, the master of the house, is coming,—whether at even, or at midnight, or at cock-crowing, or at early morn;—
text.KJV: Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:
text.NABRE: Watch, therefore; you do not know when the lord of the house is coming, whether in the evening, or at midnight, or at cockcrow, or in the morning.
text.DRB: Watch ye therefore (for you know not when the Lord of the house cometh: at even, or at midnight, or at the cock crowing, or in the morning)

text.NRSV: or else he may find you asleep when he comes suddenly.
text.Rotherham: Lest, coming suddenly, he find you, sleeping.
text.KJV: Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.
text.NABRE: May he not come suddenly and find you sleeping.
text.DRB: lest coming upon a sudden, he find you sleeping.

text.NRSV: And what I say to you I say to all: Keep awake.”
text.Rotherham: But, what, unto you, I say, unto all, I say:—Be watching.
text.KJV: And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.
text.NABRE: What I say to you, I say to all: ‘Watch!’”
text.DRB: And that which I say to you, I say to all, Watch.

text.NRSV: It was two days before the Passover and the festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him;
text.Rotherham: Now it was the Passover and the Unleavened [cakes], after two days. And the High-priests and Scribes were seeking, how, with guile, they might secure, and lay him;
text.KJV: After two days was the feast of the passover, and of unleavened bread: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put him to death.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 The Conspiracy Against Jesus. The Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread were to take place in two days’ time. So the chief priests and the scribes were seeking a way to arrest him by treachery and put him to death.
text.DRB: And the Pasch was, and the Azymes after two days: and the chief Priests and the Scribes sought how they might by some wile lay hands on him, and kill him.

text.NRSV: for they said, “Not during the festival, or there may be a riot among the people.”
text.Rotherham: for they were saying—Not in the feast, lest there be an uproar of the people.
text.KJV: But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar of the people.
text.NABRE: They said, “Not during the festival, for fear that there may be a riot among the people.” The Anointing at Bethany.
text.DRB: But they said: Not on the festival day, lest there might be a tumult of the people.

text.NRSV: While he was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came with an alabaster jar of very costly ointment of nard, and she broke open the jar and poured the ointment on his head.
text.Rotherham: And, he being in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he was reclining, there came a woman, holding an alabaster-jar of perfume, pure spikenard, very costly; and breaking the jar she was pouring down [the perfume], upon his head.
text.KJV: And being in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious; and she brake the box, and poured it on his head.
text.NABRE: When he was in Bethany reclining at table in the house of Simon the leper, a woman came with an alabaster jar of perfumed oil, costly genuine spikenard. She broke the alabaster jar and poured it on his head.
text.DRB: And when he was at Bethania in the house of Simon the Leper, and sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment, of precious spike-nard; and breaking the alabaster-box, she poured it out upon his head.

text.NRSV: But some were there who said to one another in anger, “Why was the ointment wasted in this way?
text.Rotherham: But there were some much displeased among themselves—To what end hath, this waste of the perfume happened?
text.KJV: And there were some that had indignation within themselves, and said, Why was this waste of the ointment made?
text.NABRE: There were some who were indignant. “Why has there been this waste of perfumed oil?
text.DRB: But there were certain that had indignation within themselves, and said: Whereto is this waste of the ointment made?

text.NRSV: For this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii, and the money given to the poor.” And they scolded her.
text.Rotherham: For this perfume could have been sold for above three hundred denaries, and given unto the destitute! and they were indignant with her.
text.KJV: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor. And they murmured against her.
text.NABRE: It could have been sold for more than three hundred days’ wages and the money given to the poor.” They were infuriated with her.
text.DRB: For this ointment might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and given to the poor. And they murmured against her.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said, “Let her alone; why do you trouble her? She has performed a good service for me.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said—Let her alone! Why are ye reproaching, her? A seemly work, hath she wrought, in me;
text.KJV: And Jesus said, Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.
text.NABRE: Jesus said, “Let her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
text.DRB: But Jesus said: Let her alone, why do you molest her? She hath wrought a good work upon me.

text.NRSV: For you always have the poor with you, and you can show kindness to them whenever you wish; but you will not always have me.
text.Rotherham: For, always, have ye, the destitute, with you, and whensoever ye please, ye can, unto them, [at any time] do well! But, me, not, always, have ye.
text.KJV: For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.
text.NABRE: The poor you will always have with you, and whenever you wish you can do good to them, but you will not always have me.
text.DRB: For the poor you have always with you; and when you will, you may do them good; but me you have not always.

text.NRSV: She has done what she could; she has anointed my body beforehand for its burial.
text.Rotherham: What she had, she used,—She took it beforehand to anoint my body for the burial;
text.KJV: She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.
text.NABRE: She has done what she could. She has anticipated anointing my body for burial.
text.DRB: That which she had, she hath done; she hath prevented to anoint my body to the burial.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, wherever the good news is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in remembrance of her.”
text.Rotherham: And, verily, say unto you—Wheresoever the glad-message shall be proclaimed throughout the whole world,—also what she did, will be told for a memorial of her.
text.KJV: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, wherever the gospel is proclaimed to the whole world, what she has done will be told in memory of her.”
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, wheresoever this Gospel shall be preached in the whole world, that also which she hath done, shall be told for a memory of her.

text.NRSV: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went to the chief priests in order to betray him to them.
text.Rotherham: And ,Judas Iscariot, who was one of he twelve, went away unto the High-priests, that, him, he might deliver up unto them.
text.KJV: And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them.
text.NABRE: The Betrayal by Judas. Then Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went off to the chief priests to hand him over to them.
text.DRB: And Judas Iscariote one of the Twelve went his way to the chief Priests, for to betray him to them.

text.NRSV: When they heard it, they were greatly pleased, and promised to give him money. So he began to look for an opportunity to betray him.
text.Rotherham: Now, when they heard, they rejoiced, and promised to give him, silver; and he was seeking how, at a favourable opportunity, he might, deliver him up.
text.KJV: And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. And he sought how he might conveniently betray him.
text.NABRE: When they heard him they were pleased and promised to pay him money. Then he looked for an opportunity to hand him over.
text.DRB: Who hearing it, were glad; and they promised him that they would give him money. And he sought how he might betray him conveniently.

text.NRSV: On the first day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb is sacrificed, his disciples said to him, “Where do you want us to go and make the preparations for you to eat the Passover?”
text.Rotherham: And, on the first day of the unleavened cakes, when, the passover, they were slaying, his disciples say unto him—Where wilt thou, we depart and make ready, that thou mayest eat the passover?
text.KJV: And the first day of unleavened bread, when they killed the passover, his disciples said unto him, Where wilt thou that we go and prepare that thou mayest eat the passover?
text.NABRE: Preparations for the Passover. On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, when they sacrificed the Passover lamb, his disciples said to him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare for you to eat the Passover?”
text.DRB: And the first day of the Azymes when they sacrificed the Pasch, the Disciples say to him: Whither wilt thou that we go, and prepare for thee to eat the Pasch?

text.NRSV: So he sent two of his disciples, saying to them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him,
text.Rotherham: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them—Go your way into the city, and there will meet you a man, a jar of water, bearing,—follow him;
text.KJV: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them, Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.
text.NABRE: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, “Go into the city and a man will meet you, carrying a jar of water. Follow him.
text.DRB: And he sendeth two of his Disciples, and saith to them: Go ye into the city; and there shall meet you a man carrying a pitcher of water, follow him;

text.NRSV: and wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher asks, Where is my guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?’
text.Rotherham: and, wheresoever he shall enter, say ye unto the householder—The teacher, saith, Where is my lodging, where, the passover, with my disciples, I may eat?
text.KJV: And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
text.NABRE: Wherever he enters, say to the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is my guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
text.DRB: and whithersoever he entereth, say to the Master of the house, that the Master saith, where is my refectory, where I may eat the Pasch with my Disciples?

text.NRSV: He will show you a large room upstairs, furnished and ready. Make preparations for us there.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, unto you, will shew, a large upper-room, spread ready, and, there, make ye ready for us.
text.KJV: And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.
text.NABRE: Then he will show you a large upper room furnished and ready. Make the preparations for us there.”
text.DRB: And he will shew you a great chamber, adorned; and there prepare for us.

text.NRSV: So the disciples set out and went to the city, and found everything as he had told them; and they prepared the Passover meal.
text.Rotherham: And the disciples went forth, and came into the city, and found, according as he had said unto them,—and they made ready the passover.
text.KJV: And his disciples went forth, and came into the city, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
text.NABRE: The disciples then went off, entered the city, and found it just as he had told them; and they prepared the Passover.
text.DRB: And his Disciples went their ways, and came into the city; and they found as he had told them, and they prepared the Pasch.

text.NRSV: When it was evening, he came with the twelve.
text.Rotherham: And, when evening arrived, he cometh, with the twelve.
text.KJV: And in the evening he cometh with the twelve.
text.NABRE: The Betrayer. When it was evening, he came with the Twelve.
text.DRB: And when even was come, he cometh with the Twelve.

text.NRSV: And when they had taken their places and were eating, Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me, one who is eating with me.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were reclining and eating, Jesus said—Verily, I say unto you—One from among you, will deliver me up, he that is eating with me.
text.KJV: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said, Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.
text.NABRE: And as they reclined at table and were eating, Jesus said, “Amen, I say to you, one of you will betray me, one who is eating with me.”
text.DRB: And when they were sitting at the table and eating, Jesus said: Amen I say to you, that one of you shall betray me, he that eateth with me.

text.NRSV: They began to be distressed and to say to him one after another, “Surely, not I?”
text.Rotherham: They began to be grieved, and to be saying unto him, one by one,—Can it be, I?
text.KJV: And they began to be sorrowful, and to say unto him one by one, Is it I? and another said, Is it I?
text.NABRE: They began to be distressed and to say to him, one by one, “Surely it is not I?”
text.DRB: But they began to be sad, and to say to him severally, Is it I?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “It is one of the twelve, one who is dipping bread into the bowl with me.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—One of the twelve, he that is dipping with me into the [one] bowl:
text.KJV: And he answered and said unto them, It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “One of the Twelve, the one who dips with me into the dish.
text.DRB: Who said to them: One of the Twelve, he that dippeth with me his hand in the dish.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that one by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that one not to have been born.”
text.Rotherham: Because, the Son of Man, indeed, goeth his way,—according as it is written concerning him; but alas! for that man, through whom the Son of Man is being delivered up,—Well for him, if that man, had not been born!
text.KJV: The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.
text.NABRE: For the Son of Man indeed goes, as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed. It would be better for that man if he had never been born.”
text.DRB: And the Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of man shall be betrayed. It were good for him, if that man had not been born.

text.NRSV: While they were eating, he took a loaf of bread, and after blessing it he broke it, gave it to them, and said, “Take; this is my body.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were eating, taking a loaf, he blessed and brake, and gave unto them, and said—Take! this, is, my body;
text.KJV: And as they did eat, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake it, and gave to them, and said, Take, eat: this is my body.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Supper. While they were eating, he took bread, said the blessing, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, “Take it; this is my body.”
text.DRB: And whiles they were eating, Jesus took bread: and blessing brake, and gave to them, and said: Take, This is my Body.

text.NRSV: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he gave it to them, and all of them drank from it.
text.Rotherham: And, taking a cup, he offered thanks, and gave unto them; and they, all, drank of it.
text.KJV: And he took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave it to them: and they all drank of it.
text.NABRE: Then he took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
text.DRB: And taking the chalice, giving thanks he gave to them. And they all drank of it.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—This, is, my blood of the covenant, that is to be poured out in behalf of many.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, This is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “This is my blood of the covenant, which will be shed for many.
text.DRB: And he said to them: This is my Blood of The New Testament, That Shall be Shed For Many.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, I will never again drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—No more, will I in anywise drink of the fruit of the vine, until that day when I shall be drinking it new, in the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: Verily I say unto you, I will drink no more of the fruit of the vine, until that day that I drink it new in the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, I shall not drink again the fruit of the vine until the day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, that now I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I shall drink it new in the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: When they had sung the hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
text.Rotherham: And, having sung praise, they went forth unto the Mount of Olives.
text.KJV: And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.
text.NABRE: Then, after singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter’s Denial Foretold.
text.DRB: And an hymn being said, they went forth into Mount-olivet.

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “You will all become deserters; for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto them—Ye, all, will find cause of stumbling, because it is written,—I will smite the shepherd, and, the sheep, will be scattered abroad;
text.KJV: And Jesus saith unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to them, “All of you will have your faith shaken, for it is written: ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be dispersed.’
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to them: You shall all be scandalized in me in this night; for it is written, I will strike the Pastor, & the sheep shall be dispersed.

text.NRSV: But after I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.”
text.Rotherham: But, after my arising, I will go before you into Galilee.
text.KJV: But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
text.NABRE: But after I have been raised up, I shall go before you to Galilee.”
text.DRB: But after that I shall be risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

text.NRSV: Peter said to him, “Even though all become deserters, I will not.”
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, said unto him—Even if, all, shall find cause of stumbling, certainly not, I.
text.KJV: But Peter said unto him, Although all shall be offended, yet will not I.
text.NABRE: Peter said to him, “Even though all should have their faith shaken, mine will not be.”
text.DRB: And Peter said to him: Although all shall be scandalized, yet not I.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Truly I tell you, this day, this very night, before the cock crows twice, you will deny me three times.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus saith unto him—Verily, I say unto thee, thou, this day, in this night, before that twice a cock crow, thrice, will deny me.
text.KJV: And Jesus saith unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to him, “Amen, I say to you, this very night before the cock crows twice you will deny me three times.”
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to him: Amen I say to thee, that thou this day in this night, before the cock crow twice, shalt thrice deny me.

text.NRSV: But he said vehemently, “Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you.” And all of them said the same.
text.Rotherham: But he, most vehemently, was saying—Even though I must needs die with thee, in nowise, will I, deny thee. Likewise, indeed, were, all besides, saying.
text.KJV: But he spake the more vehemently, If I should die with thee, I will not deny thee in any wise. Likewise also said they all.
text.NABRE: But he vehemently replied, “Even though I should have to die with you, I will not deny you.” And they all spoke similarly.
text.DRB: But he spake more vehemently: Although I should die together with thee, I will not deny thee. And in like manner also said they all.

text.NRSV: They went to a place called Gethsemane; and he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
text.Rotherham: And they come into an estate the name of which is Gethsemane, and he saith unto his disciples—Sit ye here, while I pray.
text.KJV: And they came to a place which was named Gethsemane: and he saith to his disciples, Sit ye here, while I shall pray.
text.NABRE: The Agony in the Garden. Then they came to a place named Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
text.DRB: And they come into a farm-place called Gethsemani. And he saith to his Disciples: Sit you here, until I pray.

text.NRSV: He took with him Peter and James and John, and began to be distressed and agitated.
text.Rotherham: And he taketh Peter and James and John with him, and began to be exceedingly amazed, and in great distress;
text.KJV: And he taketh with him Peter and James and John, and began to be sore amazed, and to be very heavy;
text.NABRE: He took with him Peter, James, and John, and began to be troubled and distressed.
text.DRB: And he taketh Peter and James and John with him; and he began to fear and to be heavy.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “I am deeply grieved, even to death; remain here, and keep awake.”
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Encompassed with grief is my soul, unto death: Abide ye here, and be watching.
text.KJV: And saith unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful unto death: tarry ye here, and watch.
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “My soul is sorrowful even to death. Remain here and keep watch.”
text.DRB: And he saith to them: My soul is sorrowful even unto death; stay here, and watch.

text.NRSV: And going a little farther, he threw himself on the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
text.Rotherham: And, going forward a little, he fell upon the ground, and was praying that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him;
text.KJV: And he went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
text.NABRE: He advanced a little and fell to the ground and prayed that if it were possible the hour might pass by him;
text.DRB: And when he was gone forward a little, he fell flat upon the ground; and he prayed that if it might be, the hour might pass from him;

text.NRSV: He said, “Abba, Father, for you all things are possible; remove this cup from me; yet, not what I want, but what you want.”
text.Rotherham: and was saying—Abba! O Father! All things, are possible to thee: Bear aside this cup from me; but not what, I, will, but what, thou, [wilt] .
text.KJV: And he said, Abba, Father, all things are possible unto thee; take away this cup from me: nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.
text.NABRE: he said, “Abba, Father, all things are possible to you. Take this cup away from me, but not what I will but what you will.”
text.DRB: and he said: Abba, Father, all things are possible to thee, transfer this chalice from me, but not that which I will, but that which thou.

text.NRSV: He came and found them sleeping; and he said to Peter, “Simon, are you asleep? Could you not keep awake one hour?
text.Rotherham: And he cometh and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter—Simon! art thou sleeping? Hadst thou not strength, one hour, to watch?
text.KJV: And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, Simon, sleepest thou? couldest not thou watch one hour?
text.NABRE: When he returned he found them asleep. He said to Peter, “Simon, are you asleep? Could you not keep watch for one hour?
text.DRB: And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping. And he saith to Peter: Simon, sleepest thou? couldst thou not watch one hour?

text.NRSV: Keep awake and pray that you may not come into the time of trial; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
text.Rotherham: Be watching and praying, that ye enter not into temptation: the, spirit, indeed, is willing, but, the flesh, weak.
text.KJV: Watch ye and pray, lest ye enter into temptation. The spirit truly is ready, but the flesh is weak.
text.NABRE: Watch and pray that you may not undergo the test. The spirit is willing but the flesh is weak.”
text.DRB: Watch ye, and pray that you enter not into tentation. The spirit indeed is prompt, but the flesh infirm.

text.NRSV: And again he went away and prayed, saying the same words.
text.Rotherham: And, again, departing, he prayed, [the same thing, saying].
text.KJV: And again he went away, and prayed, and spake the same words.
text.NABRE: Withdrawing again, he prayed, saying the same thing.
text.DRB: And going away again, he prayed, saying the self same word.

text.NRSV: And once more he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy; and they did not know what to say to him.
text.Rotherham: And, again, coming, he found them sleeping, for their eyes were being, weighed down,—and they knew not what to answer him.
text.KJV: And when he returned, he found them asleep again, (for their eyes were heavy,) neither wist they what to answer him.
text.NABRE: Then he returned once more and found them asleep, for they could not keep their eyes open and did not know what to answer him.
text.DRB: And returning, again he found them asleep (for their eyes were heavy) and they wist not what they should answer him.

text.NRSV: He came a third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? Enough! The hour has come; the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
text.Rotherham: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them—Ye are sleeping the remaining time and taking your rest:—It is enough! the hour hath come! Lo! the Son of Man is being delivered up into the hands of sinners.
text.KJV: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: it is enough, the hour is come; behold, the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
text.NABRE: He returned a third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? It is enough. The hour has come. Behold, the Son of Man is to be handed over to sinners.
text.DRB: And he cometh the third time, and saith to them: Sleep ye now, and take rest. It sufficeth: the hour is come; behold the Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of sinners.

text.NRSV: Get up, let us be going. See, my betrayer is at hand.”
text.Rotherham: Be rousing yourselves, let us be leading on! Lo! he that is delivering me up, hath drawn near.
text.KJV: Rise up, let us go; lo, he that betrayeth me is at hand.
text.NABRE: Get up, let us go. See, my betrayer is at hand.”
text.DRB: Arise, let us go. Behold, he that shall betray me, is at hand.

text.NRSV: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, arrived; and with him there was a crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, while yet he is speaking, Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, cometh near, and, with him, a multitude, with swords and clubs, from the High-priests and the Scribes and the Elders.
text.KJV: And immediately, while he yet spake, cometh Judas, one of the twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
text.NABRE: The Betrayal and Arrest of Jesus. Then, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived, accompanied by a crowd with swords and clubs who had come from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.
text.DRB: And as he was yet speaking, cometh Judas Iscariote one of the Twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief Priests and the Scribes and the Ancients.

text.NRSV: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, “The one I kiss is the man; arrest him and lead him away under guard.”
text.Rotherham: He that is delivering him up hath given an agreed sign unto them, saying—Whomsoever I shall kiss, he, it is! Secure him, and be leading away, safely.
text.KJV: And he that betrayed him had given them a token, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he; take him, and lead him away safely.
text.NABRE: His betrayer had arranged a signal with them, saying, “The man I shall kiss is the one; arrest him and lead him away securely.”
text.DRB: And the betrayer of him had given them a sign, saying: Whomsoever I shall kiss, it is he, lay hold on him, and lead him warily.

text.NRSV: So when he came, he went up to him at once and said, “Rabbi!” and kissed him.
text.Rotherham: And, coming, straightway, stepping forward to him, he saith—Rabbi! and openly kissed him.
text.KJV: And as soon as he was come, he goeth straightway to him, and saith, Master, master; and kissed him.
text.NABRE: He came and immediately went over to him and said, “Rabbi.” And he kissed him.
text.DRB: And when he was come, immediately going to him, he saith: Rabbi, and he kissed him.

text.NRSV: Then they laid hands on him and arrested him.
text.Rotherham: And, they, thrust their hands on him, and secured him.
text.KJV: And they laid their hands on him, and took him.
text.NABRE: At this they laid hands on him and arrested him.
text.DRB: But they laid hands upon him, and held him.

text.NRSV: But one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the slave of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
text.Rotherham: [A certain] one of the by-standers, drawing a sword, smote the servant of the High priest, and took off his, ear.
text.KJV: And one of them that stood by drew a sword, and smote a servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
text.NABRE: One of the bystanders drew his sword, struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his ear.
text.DRB: And one certain man of the standers about, drawing out a sword, smote the servant of the chief Priest, and cut off his ear.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me as though I were a robber?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, said unto them—As against a robber, came ye forth, with swords and clubs, to arrest me?
text.KJV: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs, to seize me?
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to them: As to a thief you come out with swords and clubs to apprehend me?

text.NRSV: Day after day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.”
text.Rotherham: Daily, was I with you, in the temple, teaching; and ye secured me not. But, that, the Scriptures, may be fulfilled…
text.KJV: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: Day after day I was with you teaching in the temple area, yet you did not arrest me; but that the scriptures may be fulfilled.”
text.DRB: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you did not lay hands on me. But, that the Scriptures may be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: All of them deserted him and fled.
text.Rotherham: And they, all, forsook, him, and fled.
text.KJV: And they all forsook him, and fled.
text.NABRE: And they all left him and fled.
text.DRB: Then his Disciples leaving him, all fled.

text.NRSV: A certain young man was following him, wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They caught hold of him,
text.Rotherham: And, a certain young man, was following with him, having cast about himself a fine Indian cloth, over his naked body,—and they are securing him;
text.KJV: And there followed him a certain young man, having a linen cloth cast about his naked body; and the young men laid hold on him:
text.NABRE: Now a young man followed him wearing nothing but a linen cloth about his body. They seized him,
text.DRB: And a certain young man followed him clothed with sindon upon the bare; and they took him.

text.NRSV: but he left the linen cloth and ran off naked.
text.Rotherham: but, he, leaving behind the cloth, fled naked.
text.KJV: And he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.
text.NABRE: but he left the cloth behind and ran off naked.
text.DRB: But he casting off the sindon, fled from them naked.

text.NRSV: They took Jesus to the high priest; and all the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes were assembled.
text.Rotherham: And they led away Jesus unto the High-priest; and all the High-priests and the Elders and the Scribes gather together.
text.KJV: And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.
text.NABRE: Jesus Before the Sanhedrin. They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes came together.
text.DRB: And they brought Jesus to the chief Priest; and all the Priests and the Scribes and the Ancients assembled together.

text.NRSV: Peter had followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest; and he was sitting with the guards, warming himself at the fire.
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, afar of, followed him, as far as within, into the court of the High-priest; and was sitting with the attendants, and warming himself by the light.
text.KJV: And Peter followed him afar off, even into the palace of the high priest: and he sat with the servants, and warmed himself at the fire.
text.NABRE: Peter followed him at a distance into the high priest’s courtyard and was seated with the guards, warming himself at the fire.
text.DRB: And Peter followed him a far off even in unto the court of the high Priest; and he sat with the servants at the fire, and warmed himself.

text.NRSV: Now the chief priests and the whole council were looking for testimony against Jesus to put him to death; but they found none.
text.Rotherham: Now, the High-priest, and all the High-council, were seeking, against Jesus, testimony, with the intent to put him to death,—and were not finding any;
text.KJV: And the chief priests and all the council sought for witness against Jesus to put him to death; and found none.
text.NABRE: The chief priests and the entire Sanhedrin kept trying to obtain testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they found none.
text.DRB: And the chief Priests and all the Council sought testimony against Jesus, that they might put him to death, neither found they.

text.NRSV: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony did not agree.
text.Rotherham: for, many, were bearing false-witness against him, and the testimonies were not, agreed.
text.KJV: For many bare false witness against him, but their witness agreed not together.
text.NABRE: Many gave false witness against him, but their testimony did not agree.
text.DRB: For many spake false witness against him; and the testimonies were not convenient.

text.NRSV: Some stood up and gave false testimony against him, saying,
text.Rotherham: And some, standing up, were bearing false-witness against him, saying—
text.KJV: And there arose certain, and bare false witness against him, saying,
text.NABRE: Some took the stand and testified falsely against him, alleging,
text.DRB: And certain rising up, bare false witness against him, saying:

text.NRSV: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another, not made with hands.’”
text.Rotherham: We, heard him saying, I, will pull down this shrine, the one made by hand, and, in three days, another, not made by hand, will I raise.
text.KJV: We heard him say, I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and within three days I will build another made without hands.
text.NABRE: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple made with hands and within three days I will build another not made with hands.’”
text.DRB: That we heard him say, I will dissolve this temple made with hand, and in three days will I build another not made with hand.

text.NRSV: But even on this point their testimony did not agree.
text.Rotherham: And, not even so, was the testimony, agreed.
text.KJV: But neither so did their witness agree together.
text.NABRE: Even so their testimony did not agree.
text.DRB: And their testimony was not convenient.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest stood up before them and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer? What is it that they testify against you?”
text.Rotherham: And, the High-priest rising up into the midst, questioned Jesus, saying—Answerest thou, nothing? What are these, against thee, witnessing?
text.KJV: And the high priest stood up in the midst, and asked Jesus, saying, Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?
text.NABRE: The high priest rose before the assembly and questioned Jesus, saying, “Have you no answer? What are these men testifying against you?”
text.DRB: And the high Priest rising up into the midst, asked Jesus, saying: Answerest thou nothing to these things that are objected to thee of these?

text.NRSV: But he was silent and did not answer. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, was silent, and answered, nothing. Again, the High-priest was questioning him, and saith unto him—Art, thou, the Christ the Son of the Blessed?
text.KJV: But he held his peace, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, and said unto him, Art thou the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?
text.NABRE: But he was silent and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him and said to him, “Are you the Messiah, the son of the Blessed One?”
text.DRB: But he held his peace and answered nothing. Again the high Priest asked him, and said to him: Art thou Christ the Son of the blessed God?

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “I am; and ‘you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Power,’ and ‘coming with the clouds of heaven.’”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said—I, am; and ye shall see the Son of Man—sitting, on the right hand, of Power, and coming with the clouds of heaven.
text.KJV: And Jesus said, I am: and ye shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus answered, “I am; and ‘you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Power and coming with the clouds of heaven.’”
text.DRB: And Jesus saith to him: I am. And you shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming with the clouds of Heaven.

text.NRSV: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, “Why do we still need witnesses?
text.Rotherham: And, the High-priest, having rent asunder his inner-garments, saith—What, further, need have we, of witnesses?
text.KJV: Then the high priest rent his clothes, and saith, What need we any further witnesses?
text.NABRE: At that the high priest tore his garments and said, “What further need have we of witnesses?
text.DRB: And the high Priest renting his garments, saith: What need we witnesses any further?

text.NRSV: You have heard his blasphemy. What is your decision?” All of them condemned him as deserving death.
text.Rotherham: Heard ye the profanity? What, to you, doth it appear? And they, all, condemned him to be, worthy, of, death.
text.KJV: Ye have heard the blasphemy: what think ye? And they all condemned him to be guilty of death.
text.NABRE: You have heard the blasphemy. What do you think?” They all condemned him as deserving to die.
text.DRB: You have heard blasphemy. How think you? Who all condemned him to be guilty of death.

text.NRSV: Some began to spit on him, and to blindfold him, and to strike him, saying to him, “Prophesy!” The guards also took him over and slapped him.
text.Rotherham: And some began to be spitting at him, and, covering up his face, and to be buffeting him, and saying to him—Prophesy! And, the attendants, with smart blows, took him.
text.KJV: And some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to buffet him, and to say unto him, Prophesy: and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands.
text.NABRE: Some began to spit on him. They blindfolded him and struck him and said to him, “Prophesy!” And the guards greeted him with blows.
text.DRB: And certain began to spit upon him, and to cover his face, and to beat him with buffets, and to say unto him, Prophesy; and the servants gave him blows.

text.NRSV: While Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant-girls of the high priest came by.
text.Rotherham: And, Peter, being, below in the court, there cometh one of the maid-servants of the High-priest;
text.KJV: And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:
text.NABRE: Peter’s Denial of Jesus. While Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the high priest’s maids came along.
text.DRB: And when Peter was in the court beneath, there cometh one of the woman-servants of the high Priest.

text.NRSV: When she saw Peter warming himself, she stared at him and said, “You also were with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.”
text.Rotherham: and, seeing Peter warming himself, having looked at him, saith—And, thou, wast, with the Nazarene—with Jesus.
text.KJV: And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked upon him, and said, And thou also wast with Jesus of Nazareth.
text.NABRE: Seeing Peter warming himself, she looked intently at him and said, “You too were with the Nazarene, Jesus.”
text.DRB: And when she had seen Peter warming himself, beholding him she saith: And thou wast with Jesus of Nazareth.

text.NRSV: But he denied it, saying, “I do not know or understand what you are talking about.” And he went out into the forecourt. Then the cock crowed.
text.Rotherham: But, he, denied, saying—I neither know [him], nor do I well understand what, thou, sayest; and went out into the porch.
text.KJV: But he denied, saying, I know not, neither understand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch; and the cock crew.
text.NABRE: But he denied it saying, “I neither know nor understand what you are talking about.” So he went out into the outer court. [Then the cock crowed.]
text.DRB: But he denied, saying: Neither know I, neither wot I what thou sayest. And he went before the court; and the cock crew.

text.NRSV: And the servant-girl, on seeing him, began again to say to the bystanders, “This man is one of them.”
text.Rotherham: And, the maid-servant, seeing him, began, again, to be saying unto the by-standers—This one, is, from among them.
text.KJV: And a maid saw him again, and began to say to them that stood by, This is one of them.
text.NABRE: The maid saw him and began again to say to the bystanders, “This man is one of them.”
text.DRB: And again a wench seeing him, began to say to the standers about, That this fellow is of them.

text.NRSV: But again he denied it. Then after a little while the bystanders again said to Peter, “Certainly you are one of them; for you are a Galilean.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, again, was denying. And, after a little again, the by-standers, were saying unto Peter—Truly, thou, art, from among them; for thou art, a Galilaean, also.
text.KJV: And he denied it again. And a little after, they that stood by said again to Peter, Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilean, and thy speech agreeth thereto.
text.NABRE: Once again he denied it. A little later the bystanders said to Peter once more, “Surely you are one of them; for you too are a Galilean.”
text.DRB: But he denied again. And after a while again they that stood by, said to Peter: Verily thou art of them; for thou art also a Galilæan.

text.NRSV: But he began to curse, and he swore an oath, “I do not know this man you are talking about.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, began to be cursing and swearing—I know not this man, of whom ye are speaking!
text.KJV: But he began to curse and to swear, saying, I know not this man of whom ye speak.
text.NABRE: He began to curse and to swear, “I do not know this man about whom you are talking.”
text.DRB: But he began to curse and to swear, That I know not this man whom you speak of.

text.NRSV: At that moment the cock crowed for the second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had said to him, “Before the cock crows twice, you will deny me three times.” And he broke down and wept.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, a second time, a cock, crowed; and Peter was reminded of the declaration, how Jesus had said to him—Before a cock, twice, crow, thrice, wilt thou deny me. And, when he thought thereon, he began to weep.
text.KJV: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.
text.NABRE: And immediately a cock crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered the word that Jesus had said to him, “Before the cock crows twice you will deny me three times.” He broke down and wept.
text.DRB: And immediately the cock crew again. And Peter remembered the word that Jesus had said unto him: Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt thrice deny me. And he began to weep.

text.KJV: And straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him to Pilate.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Jesus Before Pilate. As soon as morning came, the chief priests with the elders and the scribes, that is, the whole Sanhedrin, held a council. They bound Jesus, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.
text.NRSV: As soon as it was morning, the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council. They bound Jesus, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.
text.DRB: And forthwith in the morning the chief Priests with the Ancients and the Scribes & the whole Council, consulting together, binding Jesus led & delivered him to Pilate.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, early, when they had made, a council, the High-priest, with the Elders, and Scribes, and all the High-council, binding Jesus, led him away, and delivered him up unto Pilate.

text.KJV: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest it.
text.NABRE: Pilate questioned him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” He said to him in reply, “You say so.”
text.NRSV: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “You say so.”
text.DRB: And Pilate asked him: Art thou the King of the Jews? but he answering, said to him: Thou sayest,
text.Rotherham: And Pilate questioned him—Art, thou, the king of the Jews? And, he, answering him, saith—Thou, sayest.

text.KJV: And the chief priests accused him of many things: but he answered nothing.
text.NABRE: The chief priests accused him of many things.
text.NRSV: Then the chief priests accused him of many things.
text.DRB: And the chief Priests accused him in many things.
text.Rotherham: And the High-priests began to accuse him, of many things.

text.KJV: And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witness against thee.
text.NABRE: Again Pilate questioned him, “Have you no answer? See how many things they accuse you of.”
text.NRSV: Pilate asked him again, “Have you no answer? See how many charges they bring against you.”
text.DRB: And Pilate again asked him, saying: Answerest thou nothing? See in how many things they accuse thee.
text.Rotherham: And, Pilate, again, was questioning him, [saying]—Answerest thou, nothing? See! of how many things, they are accusing thee!

text.KJV: But Jesus yet answered nothing; so that Pilate marvelled.
text.NABRE: Jesus gave him no further answer, so that Pilate was amazed. The Sentence of Death.
text.NRSV: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate was amazed.
text.DRB: But Jesus answered nothing more; so that Pilate marveled.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, no further, answered, anything, so that Pilate began to marvel.

text.KJV: Now at that feast he released unto them one prisoner, whomsoever they desired.
text.NABRE: Now on the occasion of the feast he used to release to them one prisoner whom they requested.
text.NRSV: Now at the festival he used to release a prisoner for them, anyone for whom they asked.
text.DRB: And upon the festival day he was wont to release unto them one of the prisoners whomsoever they had demanded.
text.Rotherham: Now, at feast time, he was wont to release unto them one prisoner, whom they were claiming.

text.KJV: And there was one named Barabbas, which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.
text.NABRE: A man called Barabbas was then in prison along with the rebels who had committed murder in a rebellion.
text.NRSV: Now a man called Barabbas was in prison with the rebels who had committed murder during the insurrection.
text.DRB: And there was one called Barabbas, which was put in prison with seditious persons, who in a sedition had committed murder.
text.Rotherham: And there was the so-called Barabbas, with the rebels, bound, even with them who, in the rebellion, had committed, murder.

text.KJV: And the multitude crying aloud began to desire him to do as he had ever done unto them.
text.NABRE: The crowd came forward and began to ask him to do for them as he was accustomed.
text.NRSV: So the crowd came and began to ask Pilate to do for them according to his custom.
text.DRB: And when the multitude was come up, they began to require, according as always he did unto them.
text.Rotherham: And, going up, the multitude began to be claiming—according as he was wont to do for them.

text.KJV: But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
text.NABRE: Pilate answered, “Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews?”
text.NRSV: Then he answered them, “Do you want me to release for you the King of the Jews?”
text.DRB: And Pilate answered them, and said: Will you that I release to you the King of the Jews?
text.Rotherham: But, Pilate, answered them, saying—Will ye, I release unto you, the King of the Jews?

text.KJV: For he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy.
text.NABRE: For he knew that it was out of envy that the chief priests had handed him over.
text.NRSV: For he realized that it was out of jealousy that the chief priests had handed him over.
text.DRB: For he knew that the chief Priests for envy had delivered him.
text.Rotherham: For he was getting to know that, for envy, had [the High-priests] delivered him up.

text.KJV: But the chief priests moved the people, that he should rather release Barabbas unto them.
text.NABRE: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas for them instead.
text.NRSV: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas for them instead.
text.DRB: But the chief Priests moved the people, that he should release Barabbas rather to them.
text.Rotherham: But, the High-priests, stirred up the multitude, that, rather Barabbas, he should release unto them.

text.KJV: And Pilate answered and said again unto them, What will ye then that I shall do unto him whom ye call the King of the Jews?
text.NABRE: Pilate again said to them in reply, “Then what [do you want] me to do with [the man you call] the king of the Jews?”
text.NRSV: Pilate spoke to them again, “Then what do you wish me to do with the man you call the King of the Jews?”
text.DRB: And Pilate again answering, said to them: What will you then that I do to the King of the Jews?
text.Rotherham: But, Pilate, again answering, was saying unto them—What then shall I do with him whom ye call, the King of the Jews?

text.KJV: And they cried out again, Crucify him.
text.NABRE: They shouted again, “Crucify him.”
text.NRSV: They shouted back, “Crucify him!”
text.DRB: But they again cried: Crucify him.
text.Rotherham: And, they, again, cried out—Crucify him!

text.KJV: Then Pilate said unto them, Why, what evil hath he done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucify him.
text.NABRE: Pilate said to them, “Why? What evil has he done?” They only shouted the louder, “Crucify him.”
text.NRSV: Pilate asked them, “Why, what evil has he done?” But they shouted all the more, “Crucify him!”
text.DRB: And Pilate saith to them: Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried the more: Crucify him.
text.Rotherham: But, Pilate, was saying unto them—Why! what, evil, hath he done? And, they, vehemently, cried out—Crucify him!

text.KJV: And so Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them, and delivered Jesus, when he had scourged him, to be crucified.
text.NABRE: So Pilate, wishing to satisfy the crowd, released Barabbas to them and, after he had Jesus scourged, handed him over to be crucified.
text.NRSV: So Pilate, wishing to satisfy the crowd, released Barabbas for them; and after flogging Jesus, he handed him over to be crucified.
text.DRB: And Pilate willing to satisfy the people, released to them Barabbas, and delivered Jesus, having whipped him, for to be crucified.
text.Rotherham: And Pilate, being minded to do what would satisfy, the multitude, released unto them Barabbas, and delivered up Jesus, having scourged him, that he should be crucified.

text.KJV: And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Pretorium; and they call together the whole band.
text.NABRE: Mockery by the Soldiers. The soldiers led him away inside the palace, that is, the praetorium, and assembled the whole cohort.
text.NRSV: Then the soldiers led him into the courtyard of the palace (that is, the governor’s headquarters); and they called together the whole cohort.
text.DRB: And the soldiers led him into the court of the Palace, and they call together the whole band:
text.Rotherham: And, the soldiers, led him away, inside the court, which is a judgment-hall,—and called together the whole band;

text.KJV: And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crown of thorns, and put it about his head,
text.NABRE: They clothed him in purple and, weaving a crown of thorns, placed it on him.
text.NRSV: And they clothed him in a purple cloak; and after twisting some thorns into a crown, they put it on him.
text.DRB: and they clothe him in purple, and platting a crown of thorns, they put it upon him.
text.Rotherham: and they array him in purple, and set upon him, when they have plaited it; a crown of thorns,—

text.KJV: And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!
text.NABRE: They began to salute him with, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
text.NRSV: And they began saluting him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
text.DRB: And they began to salute him: Hail King of the Jews.
text.Rotherham: and began to be saluting him—Joy to thee! King of the Jews!

text.KJV: And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing their knees worshipped him.
text.NABRE: and kept striking his head with a reed and spitting upon him. They knelt before him in homage.
text.NRSV: They struck his head with a reed, spat upon him, and knelt down in homage to him.
text.DRB: And they smote his head with a reed: and they did spit on him. And bowing the knees, they adored him.
text.Rotherham: and were striking him on the head with a reed, and were spitting at him,—and, bowing their knees, were doing him homage.

text.KJV: And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple from him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him out to crucify him.
text.NABRE: And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the purple cloak, dressed him in his own clothes, and led him out to crucify him.
text.NRSV: After mocking him, they stripped him of the purple cloak and put his own clothes on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
text.DRB: And after they had mocked him, they stripped him of the purple, and put on him his own garments, and they lead him forth to crucify him.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had mocked him, they put off him, the purple, and put on him, his own garments. And they lead him forth, that they may crucify him.

text.KJV: And they compel one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, coming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to bear his cross.
text.NABRE: The Way of the Cross. They pressed into service a passer-by, Simon, a Cyrenian, who was coming in from the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to carry his cross.
