text.NRSV: They compelled a passer-by, who was coming in from the country, to carry his cross; it was Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus.
text.DRB: And they forced a certain man that passed by, Simon a Cyrenean coming from the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to take up his cross.
text.Rotherham: And they impress a certain passer-by, Simon a Cyrenian, coming from a field, the father of Alexander and Rufus,—that he may carry his cross.

text.KJV: And they bring him unto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a skull.
text.NABRE: The Crucifixion. They brought him to the place of Golgotha (which is translated Place of the Skull).
text.NRSV: Then they brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha (which means the place of a skull).
text.DRB: And they bring him into the place Golgotha, which being interpreted is, The place of Calvary.
text.Rotherham: And they bring him unto the Golgotha place, which is, being translated, Skull-place.

text.KJV: And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received it not.
text.NABRE: They gave him wine drugged with myrrh, but he did not take it.
text.NRSV: And they offered him wine mixed with myrrh; but he did not take it.
text.DRB: And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh; and he took it not.
text.Rotherham: And they would have given him, myrrhed wine,—who, however, received it not.

text.KJV: And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots upon them, what every man should take.
text.NABRE: Then they crucified him and divided his garments by casting lots for them to see what each should take.
text.NRSV: And they crucified him, and divided his clothes among them, casting lots to decide what each should take.
text.DRB: And crucifying him, they divided his garments, casting lots upon them, who should take which.
text.Rotherham: And they crucify him, and part asunder his garments, casting a lot upon them—who should have anything.

text.KJV: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.
text.NABRE: It was nine o’clock in the morning when they crucified him.
text.NRSV: It was nine o’clock in the morning when they crucified him.
text.DRB: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.
text.Rotherham: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.

text.KJV: And the superscription of his accusation was written over, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
text.NABRE: The inscription of the charge against him read, “The King of the Jews.”
text.NRSV: The inscription of the charge against him read, “The King of the Jews.”
text.DRB: And the title of his cause was superscribed, King of The Jews.
text.Rotherham: And the inscription of his accusation, had been inscribed—THE KING OF THE JEWS.

text.KJV: And with him they crucify two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.
text.NABRE: With him they crucified two revolutionaries, one on his right and one on his left.
text.NRSV: And with him they crucified two bandits, one on his right and one on his left.
text.DRB: And with him they crucify two thieves; one on the right hand, and another on his left.
text.Rotherham: And, with him, they crucify, two robbers, one on his right hand, and one on his left.

text.KJV: And the scripture was fulfilled, which saith, And he was numbered with the transgressors.
text.DRB: And the Scripture was fulfilled that saith: And with the wicked he was reputed.

text.KJV: And they that passed by railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days,
text.NABRE: Those passing by reviled him, shaking their heads and saying, “Aha! You who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,
text.NRSV: Those who passed by derided him, shaking their heads and saying, “Aha! You who would destroy the temple and build it in three days,
text.DRB: And they that passed by, blasphemed him, wagging their heads, and saying: Vah, he that destroyeth the Temple, and in three days buildeth it:
text.Rotherham: And, the passers-by, were reviling him, shaking their heads, and saying—Aha! thou who wast pulling down the shrine, and building one in three days!

text.KJV: Save thyself, and come down from the cross.
text.NABRE: save yourself by coming down from the cross.”
text.NRSV: save yourself, and come down from the cross!”
text.DRB: save thyself, coming down from the cross.
text.Rotherham: Save thyself,—coming down from the cross.

text.KJV: Likewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes, He saved others; himself he cannot save.
text.NABRE: Likewise the chief priests, with the scribes, mocked him among themselves and said, “He saved others; he cannot save himself.
text.NRSV: In the same way the chief priests, along with the scribes, were also mocking him among themselves and saying, “He saved others; he cannot save himself.
text.DRB: In like manner also the chief Priests mocking said with the Scribes one to another: He saved others, himself he cannot save.
text.Rotherham: Likewise, the High-priests also, mocking one to another, with the Scribes, were saying—Others, he saved, himself, he cannot save!

text.KJV: Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him reviled him.
text.NABRE: Let the Messiah, the King of Israel, come down now from the cross that we may see and believe.” Those who were crucified with him also kept abusing him.
text.NRSV: Let the Messiah, the King of Israel, come down from the cross now, so that we may see and believe.” Those who were crucified with him also taunted him.
text.DRB: Let Christ the King of Israel come down now from the cross that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him, railed at him.
text.Rotherham: The Christ, the King of Israel—let him come down now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And, they who had been crucified with him, were casting it in his teeth.

text.KJV: And when the sixth hour was come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.
text.NABRE: The Death of Jesus. At noon darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.
text.NRSV: When it was noon, darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.
text.DRB: And when it was the sixth hour, there was made darkness upon the whole earth until the ninth hour.
text.Rotherham: And, when it was the sixth hour, darkness, came on all the land—until the ninth hour;

text.KJV: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani? which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
text.NABRE: And at three o’clock Jesus cried out in a loud voice, “ Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani? ” which is translated, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
text.NRSV: At three o’clock Jesus cried out with a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
text.DRB: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a mighty voice, saying: Eloi, Eloi, lamma-sabacthani? Which is being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
text.Rotherham: and, at the ninth hour, Jesus, uttered a cry, with a loud voice—Eloi! Eloi! lama sabachthanei? which is, being translated—My God! [My God!] to what end, didst thou forsake me?

text.KJV: And some of them that stood by, when they heard it, said, Behold, he calleth Elias.
text.NABRE: Some of the bystanders who heard it said, “Look, he is calling Elijah.”
text.NRSV: When some of the bystanders heard it, they said, “Listen, he is calling for Elijah.”
text.DRB: And certain of the standers about hearing, said: Behold, he calleth Elias.
text.Rotherham: And, some of the by-standers, having heard, were saying—See! Elijah, he calleth!

text.KJV: And one ran and filled a spunge full of vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink, saying, Let alone; let us see whether Elias will come to take him down.
text.NABRE: One of them ran, soaked a sponge with wine, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Wait, let us see if Elijah comes to take him down.”
text.NRSV: And someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Wait, let us see whether Elijah will come to take him down.”
text.DRB: And one running and filling a spunge with vinegar, and putting it about a reed, gave him drink, saying: Let be, let us see if Elias come to take him down.
text.Rotherham: And one, running, filled a sponge with vinegar, and, putting it about a reed, was giving him to drink, saying—Stay! let us see whether Elijah is coming, to take him down!

text.KJV: And Jesus cried with a loud voice, and gave up the ghost.
text.NABRE: Jesus gave a loud cry and breathed his last.
text.NRSV: Then Jesus gave a loud cry and breathed his last.
text.DRB: And Jesus putting forth a mighty voice, gave up the ghost.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, sending out a loud voice, ceased to breathe.

text.KJV: And the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom.
text.NABRE: The veil of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
text.NRSV: And the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom.
text.DRB: And the veil of the temple was rent in two, from the top to the bottom.
text.Rotherham: And, the veil of the Temple, was rent into two, from top to bottom.

text.KJV: And when the centurion, which stood over against him, saw that he so cried out, and gave up the ghost, he said, Truly this man was the Son of God.
text.NABRE: When the centurion who stood facing him saw how he breathed his last he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”
text.NRSV: Now when the centurion, who stood facing him, saw that in this way he breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was God’s Son!”
text.DRB: And the Centurion that stood over against him, seeing that so crying he had given up the ghost, said: Indeed this man was the Son of God.
text.Rotherham: Now the centurion, who was standing near, out over against him, seeing, that, thus, he ceased to breathe, said—Truly, this man, was God’s son!

text.KJV: There were also women looking on afar off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;
text.NABRE: There were also women looking on from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of the younger James and of Joses, and Salome.
text.NRSV: There were also women looking on from a distance; among them were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
text.DRB: And there were also women looking on a far off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less, and of Joseph, and Salome;
text.Rotherham: And there were, women also, from afar, looking on,—among whom were both Mary the Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the Little and Joses, and Salome;

text.KJV: (Who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and ministered unto him;) and many other women which came up with him unto Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: These women had followed him when he was in Galilee and ministered to him. There were also many other women who had come up with him to Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: These used to follow him and provided for him when he was in Galilee; and there were many other women who had come up with him to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: and when he was in Galilee, they followed him, and ministered to him, and many other women that came up together with him to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: who, when he was in Galilee, used to follow him, and minister unto him,—and many other women, who had come up with him unto Jerusalem.

text.KJV: And now when the even was come, because it was the preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
text.NABRE: The Burial of Jesus. When it was already evening, since it was the day of preparation, the day before the sabbath,
text.NRSV: When evening had come, and since it was the day of Preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
text.DRB: And when evening was now come (because it was the Parasceve, which is the Sabboth-eve)
text.Rotherham: And evening, already, having begun, since it was a preparation, that is, the eve of a Sabbath,

text.KJV: Joseph of Arimathea, an honourable counsellor, which also waited for the kingdom of God, came, and went in boldly unto Pilate, and craved the body of Jesus.
text.NABRE: Joseph of Arimathea, a distinguished member of the council, who was himself awaiting the kingdom of God, came and courageously went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
text.NRSV: Joseph of Arimathea, a respected member of the council, who was also himself waiting expectantly for the kingdom of God, went boldly to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
text.DRB: came Joseph of Arimathæa a noble Senator, who himself also was expecting the Kingdom of God; and he went in boldly to Pilate, and asked the body of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Joseph of Arimathaea, a noble counsellor, who, also himself, was awaiting the kingdom of God, came; and, venturing, went in unto Pilate, and claimed the body of Jesus.

text.KJV: And Pilate marvelled if he were already dead: and calling unto him the centurion, he asked him whether he had been any while dead.
text.NABRE: Pilate was amazed that he was already dead. He summoned the centurion and asked him if Jesus had already died.
text.NRSV: Then Pilate wondered if he were already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had been dead for some time.
text.DRB: But Pilate marveled if he were now dead. And sending for the Centurion, asked him if he were now dead.
text.Rotherham: But, Pilate, wondered whether, already, he was dead; and, calling near the centurion, questioned him—whether he had, already, died.

text.KJV: And when he knew it of the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
text.NABRE: And when he learned of it from the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
text.NRSV: When he learned from the centurion that he was dead, he granted the body to Joseph.
text.DRB: And when he understood by the Centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
text.Rotherham: And, getting to know from the centurion, he presented the corpse unto Joseph.

text.KJV: And he bought fine linen, and took him down, and wrapped him in the linen, and laid him in a sepulchre which was hewn out of a rock, and rolled a stone unto the door of the sepulchre.
text.NABRE: Having bought a linen cloth, he took him down, wrapped him in the linen cloth and laid him in a tomb that had been hewn out of the rock. Then he rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
text.NRSV: Then Joseph bought a linen cloth, and taking down the body, wrapped it in the linen cloth, and laid it in a tomb that had been hewn out of the rock. He then rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.
text.DRB: And Joseph buying sindon, and taking him down, wrapped him in the sindon, and laid him in a monument, that was hewed out of a rock. And he rolled a stone to the door of the monument.
text.Rotherham: And, buying a fine Indian cloth, he took him down, and wrapped him about with the cloth, and laid him in a tomb, which had been hewn out of a rock,—and rolled near a stone upon the door of the tomb.

text.KJV: And Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses beheld where he was laid.
text.NABRE: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses watched where he was laid.
text.NRSV: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where the body was laid.
text.DRB: And Mary Magdalene and Mary of Joseph beheld where he was laid.
text.Rotherham: Now, Mary the Magdalene, and Mary the mother of Joses, were viewing how he had been laid.

text.NRSV: When the sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they might go and anoint him.
text.Rotherham: And, the Sabbath having passed, Mary the Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, that, coming, they might anoint him.
text.KJV: And when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 The Resurrection of Jesus. When the sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary, the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they might go and anoint him.
text.DRB: And when the Sabboth was past, Mary Magdalene and Mary of James, and Salome bought spices, that coming they might anoint Jesus.

text.NRSV: And very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they went to the tomb.
text.Rotherham: And, very early, on the first of the week, they are coming towards the tomb—when, the sun, arose.
text.KJV: And very early in the morning the first day of the week, they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun.
text.NABRE: Very early when the sun had risen, on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb.
text.DRB: And very early the first of the Sabboths, they come to the monument, the sun being now risen.

text.NRSV: They were saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?”
text.Rotherham: And they were saying one to another—Who, shall roll away for us the stone, out of the door of the tomb?
text.KJV: And they said among themselves, Who shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre?
text.NABRE: They were saying to one another, “Who will roll back the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?”
text.DRB: And they said one to another: Who shall roll us back the stone from the door of the monument?

text.NRSV: When they looked up, they saw that the stone, which was very large, had already been rolled back.
text.Rotherham: And, looking up, they observe that the stone hath been rolled up,—for it was exceeding great.
text.KJV: And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.
text.NABRE: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled back; it was very large.
text.DRB: And looking, they saw the stone rolled back. For it was very great.

text.NRSV: As they entered the tomb, they saw a young man, dressed in a white robe, sitting on the right side; and they were alarmed.
text.Rotherham: And, entering into the tomb, they saw a young man, sitting on the right, clothed with a white robe,—and they were greatly alarmed.
text.KJV: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a long white garment; and they were affrighted.
text.NABRE: On entering the tomb they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a white robe, and they were utterly amazed.
text.DRB: And entering into the monument, they saw a young man sitting on the right hand covered with a white robe: and they were astonished.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “Do not be alarmed; you are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised; he is not here. Look, there is the place they laid him.
text.Rotherham: But, he, saith unto them—Be not alarmed! Jesus, ye are seeking, the Nazarene, the crucified: He hath arisen! he is not here,—See! the place where they laid him!
text.KJV: And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Do not be amazed! You seek Jesus of Nazareth, the crucified. He has been raised; he is not here. Behold, the place where they laid him.
text.DRB: Who saith to them: Be not dismayed; you seek Jesus of Nazareth, that was crucified; he is risen, he is not here, behold the place where they laid him.

text.NRSV: But go, tell his disciples and Peter that he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him, as he told you.”
text.Rotherham: But go your way, tell his disciples, and Peter—He is going before you into Galilee: there, shall ye yourselves see him,—according as he said unto you.
text.KJV: But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
text.NABRE: But go and tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He is going before you to Galilee; there you will see him, as he told you.’”
text.DRB: But go, tell his Disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee; there you shall see him, as he told you.

text.NRSV: So they went out and fled from the tomb, for terror and amazement had seized them; and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.
text.Rotherham: And, going out, they fled from the tomb, for, trembling and transport, were holding them; and, unto no one, said they, anything, for they were afraid.
text.KJV: And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any man; for they were afraid.
text.NABRE: Then they went out and fled from the tomb, seized with trembling and bewilderment. They said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.
text.DRB: But they going forth, fled from the monument. For trembling and fear had invaded them: and they said nothing to anybody, for they were afraid.

text.NRSV: Now after he rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.
text.Rotherham: [[And, arising early, on the first of the week, he was manifested, first, unto Mary the Magdalene, from whom he had cast, seven demons.
text.KJV: Now when Jesus was risen early the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had cast seven devils.
text.NABRE: The Longer Ending The Appearance to Mary Magdalene. [ When he had risen, early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had driven seven demons.
text.DRB: And he rising early the first of the Sabboth, appeared first to Mary Magdalen, out of whom he had cast seven devils.

text.NRSV: She went out and told those who had been with him, while they were mourning and weeping.
text.Rotherham: She, going her way, bare tidings unto them who had come to be with him, as they were mourning and weeping.
text.KJV: And she went and told them that had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
text.NABRE: She went and told his companions who were mourning and weeping.
text.DRB: She went and told them that had been with him, that were mourning & weeping.

text.NRSV: But when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they would not believe it.
text.Rotherham: And, they, hearing that he was living, and had been looked upon by her, disbelieved.
text.KJV: And they, when they had heard that he was alive, and had been seen of her, believed not.
text.NABRE: When they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they did not believe.
text.DRB: And they hearing that he was alive and had been seen of her, did not believe.

text.NRSV: After this he appeared in another form to two of them, as they were walking into the country.
text.Rotherham: But, after these things, unto two from among them, as they were walking, was he manifested, in a different form, as they were going unto a country place;
text.KJV: After that he appeared in another form unto two of them, as they walked, and went into the country.
text.NABRE: The Appearance to Two Disciples. After this he appeared in another form to two of them walking along on their way to the country.
text.DRB: And after this he appeared in another shape to two of them walking, as they were going into the country.

text.NRSV: And they went back and told the rest, but they did not believe them.
text.Rotherham: and, they, departing, bare tidings unto the rest,—but, even them, they believed not.
text.KJV: And they went and told it unto the residue: neither believed they them.
text.NABRE: They returned and told the others; but they did not believe them either.
text.DRB: And they going told the rest: neither them did they believe.

text.NRSV: Later he appeared to the eleven themselves as they were sitting at the table; and he upbraided them for their lack of faith and stubbornness, because they had not believed those who saw him after he had risen.
text.Rotherham: But, afterwards, unto the eleven themselves, as they reclined, was he manifested, and he upbraided their disbelief and hardness of heart,—because, them who had looked upon him when arisen [from among the dead] they believed not;
text.KJV: Afterward he appeared unto the eleven as they sat at meat, and upbraided them with their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they believed not them which had seen him after he was risen.
text.NABRE: The Commissioning of the Eleven. [But] later, as the eleven were at table, he appeared to them and rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart because they had not believed those who saw him after he had been raised.
text.DRB: Last he appeared to those eleven as they sat at the table; and he exprobrated their incredulity and hardness of heart, because they did not believe them that had seen him risen again.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Go into all the world and proclaim the good news to the whole creation.
text.Rotherham: and he said unto them—Go ye into all the world, and proclaim the glad-message, to the whole creation:
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Go into the whole world and proclaim the gospel to every creature.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Going into the whole world preach the Gospel to all creatures.

text.NRSV: The one who believes and is baptized will be saved; but the one who does not believe will be condemned.
text.Rotherham: He that hath believed, and been immersed, shall be saved; but, he that hath disbelieved, shall be condemned:
text.KJV: He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned.
text.NABRE: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved; whoever does not believe will be condemned.
text.DRB: He that believeth and is baptized, shall be saved; but he that believeth not, shall be condemned.

text.NRSV: And these signs will accompany those who believe: by using my name they will cast out demons; they will speak in new tongues;
text.Rotherham: Signs, moreover, shall follow, them who have believed,—these:—In my name, shall they cast, demons, out, with tongues, shall they speak,—[and, in their hands,] they shall take up, serpents;
text.KJV: And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues;
text.NABRE: These signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will drive out demons, they will speak new languages.
text.DRB: And them that believe these signs shall follow: In my name shall they cast out devils; They shall speak with new tongues;

text.NRSV: they will pick up snakes in their hands, and if they drink any deadly thing, it will not hurt them; they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will recover.”
text.Rotherham: And, if, any deadly thing, they have drunk, in nowise, shall it, hurt, them: Upon sick persons, shall they lay, hands, and, well, shall they remain.
text.KJV: They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.
text.NABRE: They will pick up serpents [with their hands], and if they drink any deadly thing, it will not harm them. They will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.”
text.DRB: Serpents shall they take away; And if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; They shall impose hands upon the sick and they shall be whole.

text.NRSV: So then the Lord Jesus, after he had spoken to them, was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
text.Rotherham: The Lord [Jesus], therefore, on the one hand, after talking with them, was taken up into heaven, and sat down on the right hand of God:
text.KJV: So then after the Lord had spoken unto them, he was received up into heaven, and sat on the right hand of God.
text.NABRE: The Ascension of Jesus. So then the Lord Jesus, after he spoke to them, was taken up into heaven and took his seat at the right hand of God.
text.DRB: And so our Lord Jesus after he spake unto them, was assumpted into Heaven, and sat on the right hand of God.

text.NRSV: And they went out and proclaimed the good news everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the message by the signs that accompanied it.
text.Rotherham: They, on the other hand, going forth, proclaimed on every side, the Lord, co-working, and confirming, the word, through, the closely following signs.]] OTHERWISE. [[All the things given in charge unto the companions of Peter, they concisely reported. But, after these things, Jesus also, himself, from east even unto west, sent forth, through them, the sacred and incorruptible proclamation of the age-abiding salvation.]]
text.KJV: And they went forth, and preached every where, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word with signs following. Amen.
text.NABRE: But they went forth and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the word through accompanying signs.] The Shorter Ending [And they reported all the instructions briefly to Peter’s companions. Afterwards Jesus himself, through them, sent forth from east to west the sacred and imperishable proclamation of eternal salvation. Amen.]
text.DRB: But they going forth preached every-where: our Lord working withal, and confirming the word with signs that followed.
